source.subject: Buyer's impact map & SOC2 evidence next steps
messages:
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rajiv Patel <rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 17 Jun 2029 17:03:00 -0400
Subject: Buyer's impact map & SOC2 evidence next steps

Hi Rajiv —

Thanks again for the 30-minute call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum going: 

Call highlights
- Primary use case: real-time clinical summarization in scheduling flows (low-latency, PII handling concerns).
- Decision drivers: latency SLOs, data residency, auditability (immutable logs), and procurement T&Cs.
- Economic buyer: appears to be Leah Kim (CPO). Security will be led by Priya Shah, legal by Tom Hopkins, procurement by an internal procurement mailing list.

Immediate asks we can own
1) SOC 2 Type II report (latest) and a redacted pen test summary — we can share these by EOD Wednesday.
2) Short table answering retention/audit log questions you raised (what we retain, retention windows, access controls).
3) Proposed Mutual Action Plan (MAP) with timelines for security review, PO/contract, and pilot signoff.

Can you confirm two things for us: 
- Final legal contact for contract redlines (Tom ok?), and
- A preferred date/time for a 30-minute procurement review this week (we suggested Fri PST in the call).

I've attached our evidence checklist to make it easy for your team to map asks to artifacts. Ben and Sean are on copy and will prepare the SOC2/pen test output and an audit-logs extract.

Next steps (owner):
- Marissa: send MAP + timeline and checklist (today)
- Ben: assemble SOC2/pen test + log sample (by Wed EOD)
- Rajiv/Procurement: confirm legal contact + procurement demo slot (this week)

Thanks — looking forward to getting this over the line.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0123
[attachment: redwood-evidence-checklist.xlsx (Excel, 42KB)]

From: Rajiv Patel <rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Leah Kim <leah.kim@aureliahealth.com>, Tom Hopkins <tom.h@aureliahealth.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>, procurement@aureliahealth.com
Date: Mon, 17 Jun 2029 18:26:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Buyer's impact map & SOC2 evidence next steps

On Mon, Jun 17, 2029 at 5:03 PM Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Rajiv —
> 
> Thanks again for the 30-minute call today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we keep momentum going: 
> ...

Thanks Marissa — helpful summary. Quick answers from our side: 
- Confirming economic buyer: Leah Kim (CPO) — copying her.
- Legal lead: Tom Hopkins (copied). Procurement will coordinate via procurement@aureliahealth.com.
- Security: Priya Shah will lead the technical review. Priya: you own any follow-ups on logging/encryption questions.

A few follow-ups we need you to confirm in your MAP: 
1) Data residency options (we need EU and US regions for different tenants; which regions do you currently support and can we pin region at tenant-level?).
2) Audit logs: sample format and retention options (we prefer 7 years for some regulatory flows — is that supported via export?).
3) KMS/SSO: high-level doc describing integrations (we use Okta + AWS KMS).

Procurement asks to see the SOC2 Type II report and a high-level pen test summary (no PII) before we schedule contract redlines. Friday afternoon works for a procurement touchpoint — can your team do 2:00pm PT?

Thanks — Rajiv
Rajiv Patel
Director, Product Integrations | Aurelia Health
rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com | +1 (212) 555-0789

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rajiv Patel <rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Leah Kim <leah.kim@aureliahealth.com>, Tom Hopkins <tom.h@aureliahealth.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>, procurement@aureliahealth.com
Date: Tue, 18 Jun 2029 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Buyer's impact map & SOC2 evidence next steps

Rajiv — thanks, that's perfect. Confirming: 2:00pm PT Friday works for us; I've blocked a slot and will share a calendar invite.

Ben and Sean — can you prepare the following deliverables by Wed EOD so we can circulate ahead of Friday's procurement call?
- SOC2 Type II PDF (redacted where necessary)
- Two-page pen test executive summary (findings & remediation status)
- Audit logs sample (CSV) and retention matrix (what we store, retention windows, how to export)
- Short note on region pinning and KMS/SSO high-level architecture (Okta + supported KMS providers)

I'll assemble a 1-page MAP (table: milestone, owner, due date) and share before EOD today.

Marissa

> On Mon, Jun 17, 2029 at 6:26 PM Rajiv Patel <rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com> wrote:
> > Thanks Marissa — helpful summary. Quick answers from our side: ...

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Rajiv Patel <rajiv.patel@aureliahealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, procurement@aureliahealth.com, Tom Hopkins <tom.h@aureliahealth.com>, Priya Shah <priya.shah@aureliahealth.com>, Leah Kim <leah.kim@aureliahealth.com>
Date: Wed, 19 Jun 2029 09:18:00 -0400
Subject: SOC2 & pen test summary + audit log sample (Aurelia)

Hi all — sending the artifacts Ben promised. Links and attachments below. If you need additional redaction or an NDA for the full pen test report, let us know and we can route it through legal.

Attachments:
- SOC2-TypeII-Report-Redwood-2028.pdf (PDF, 2.3MB)
- pen-test-summary-2029-05.txt (Text, 18KB)
- audit-logs-sample.csv (CSV, 120KB)
- redwood-evidence-checklist.xlsx (Excel, 42KB)

Related links:
- Shared evidence folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-redwood-aurelia-evidence
- MAP (draft): https://docs.google.com/document/d/1MAPdraftAurelia/edit

Key notes:
- SOC2: Type II report covers our control environment through Dec 2028. We've redacted customer-specific identifiers.
- Pen test: executive summary lists two low-risk findings (dependency version bump) and one medium-risk issue that has been remediated; full details available under NDA.
- Audit logs: CSV contains sample event types (auth, inference.request, inference.response, admin.change) and timestamps. We support exports and long-term storage via customer-managed S3 for retention beyond default windows.

Marissa, I've uploaded the MAP draft to the folder and flagged procurement items. Looking forward to Friday at 2:00pm PT.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Security & Compliance Liaison | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (650) 555-0199
[attachment: SOC2-TypeII-Report-Redwood-2028.pdf (PDF, 2.3MB)]

source.subject: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps
messages:
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
To: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

Hi Diane / Acme Ops,

Quick heads-up: our monitoring detected a narrow KV-coherence window in the routing layer that is causing intermittent 5xxs and duplicate token emission for a small percentage of requests targeting your tenant in us-west-2. We traced the root cause to a race in the cache invalidation code when a shard rebalances during heavy prefix load.

Immediate plan (short notice):
- We will deploy a consistency-guard hotfix (v1.2.3) to the affected shard pool starting at 10:30 PDT as a rolling update (shard-by-shard).
- Expected impact: 20–35 minutes for full rollout across your active nodes; during the window you may see 5–10% increased p99 latency and a small chance of transient 429/503 while a node is being drained.
- Mitigations enabled: automatic fallback to the previous model variant for mid-stream requests, and an emergency KV lock to prevent write skew during the transition.

What we need from you (quick verification):
1) Please avoid initiating long-running streaming/generation sessions for ~45 minutes starting at 10:25 PDT.
2) After we message "hotfix applied", run this smoke test and reply with pass/fail: curl -s -X POST https://api.redwood.com/v1/generate -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -d '{"model":"open-7b","prompt":"verify:kv-check-123"}'

Attachments: diag-logs-20290507.tar.gz (traces), consistency-guard-hotfix.patch (diff), runbook-rollback.pdf

I know this is short notice — Marissa and Karthik are on standby for any escalations. I’ll update as we progress.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Analytics Ops <ops@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 10:25:33 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

Thanks for the quick note, Sean. Appreciate the proactive communication. A couple quick questions before you proceed:

- Can you confirm whether any data was lost or mutated during the observed window? We saw a couple of out-of-order responses but didn't observe missing outputs yet.
- We have a customer-facing job running streams for inference — is there a recommended retry strategy if a stream fails mid-run?
- Also, can you hold any changes to our billing/usage metrics while this is active? We need to make sure we don't throttle legitimate traffic.

We’ll hold new long-running streams until you confirm the hotfix status. Please include the expected rollback process if we see regression.

Diane Park
Head of Platform, Acme Analytics

From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
To: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 11:05:21 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

On Tue, 07 May 2029 10:25:33 -0700 Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the quick note, Sean. Appreciate the proactive communication. A couple quick questions before you proceed:

Short answers and current state:
- No evidence of permanent data loss in the traces we gathered. The issue is coherency/staleness under very specific rebalance conditions — you may see duplicates or out-of-order lines, but writes appear intact. We attached trace snippets in diag-logs-20290507.tar.gz.
- Retry guidance: Safe to retry idempotent requests or to resume short streams by restarting the session. For long-running streams, prefer a fast reconnect + resume token (we can provide an SDK helper if you want). We do NOT advise blind retries for non-idempotent multi-step pipelines; coordinate with us if you have in-flight transactional sequences.
- Billing: we will not count any anomalous duplicate responses toward your bill; our billing pipeline will mark tokens during the window as reviewable and we will follow up with a corrected usage report.

Update: hotfix v1.2.3 completed rolling deploy to the first two shards at 11:02 PDT with the consistency guard enabled and emergency locks active. No customer-facing errors observed in the first batch beyond marginal latency blips. We will continue rolling; estimated completion 11:40 PDT.

Please run the verification curl above when you can. If you prefer, run the lightweight SDK check: redwood.verify.kv_check("verify:kv-check-123").

Rollback plan (if needed):
- Immediate revert to previous build and removal of emergency locks (target <20 minutes) using our runbook: https://redwood.console/runbooks/KP-591. The runbook is attached as runbook-rollback.pdf. We’ll only initiate rollback if we observe increased error rates >1% or systemic token duplication on your tenant.

I’ll post another update at completion. Marissa will be your escalation lead if anything degrades.

—
Sean Gallagher
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference

From: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Analytics Ops <ops@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 15:40:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

On Tue, 07 May 2029 11:05:21 -0700 Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com> wrote:
> Update: hotfix v1.2.3 completed rolling deploy to the first two shards at 11:02 PDT with the consistency guard enabled and emergency locks active.

Thanks — we ran the smoke check after you completed the rollout and got a successful response for the sample prompt. One production stream needed a single manual retry (the client observed a short disconnect). No data corruption observed on our end.

We appreciate the billing note — please send the corrected usage report and the postmortem timeline (root cause, why the rebalance race happened, mitigation steps) when available. Can you also confirm whether we need to take any action to clear local caches or is the new guard transparent to clients?

Diane Park
Head of Platform, Acme Analytics

---
Sean/GTM team: please follow up with a short postmortem and link to the incident page. We will share that with our internal stakeholders and the customer success team on our side.

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

Hi Diane / Acme Ops,

Quick heads-up: our monitoring detected a narrow KV-coherence window in the routing layer that is causing intermittent 5xxs and duplicate token emission for a small percentage of requests targeting your tenant in us-west-2. We traced the root cause to a race in the cache invalidation code when a shard rebalances during heavy prefix load.

Immediate plan (short notice):
- We will deploy a consistency-guard hotfix (v1.2.3) to the affected shard pool starting at 10:30 PDT as a rolling update (shard-by-shard).
- Expected impact: 20–35 minutes for full rollout across your active nodes; during the window you may see 5–10% increased p99 latency and a small chance of transient 429/503 while a node is being drained.
- Mitigations enabled: automatic fallback to the previous model variant for mid-stream requests, and an emergency KV lock to prevent write skew during the transition.

What we need from you (quick verification):
1) Please avoid initiating long-running streaming/generation sessions for ~45 minutes starting at 10:25 PDT.
2) After we message \"hotfix applied\", run this smoke test and reply with pass/fail: curl -s -X POST https://api.redwood.com/v1/generate -H 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' -d '{\"model\":\"open-7b\",\"prompt\":\"verify:kv-check-123\"}'

Attachments: diag-logs-20290507.tar.gz (traces), consistency-guard-hotfix.patch (diff), runbook-rollback.pdf

I know this is short notice — Marissa and Karthik are on standby for any escalations. I’ll update as we progress.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Analytics Ops <ops@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 10:25:33 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

Thanks for the quick note, Sean. Appreciate the proactive communication. A couple quick questions before you proceed:

- Can you confirm whether any data was lost or mutated during the observed window? We saw a couple of out-of-order responses but didn't observe missing outputs yet.
- We have a customer-facing job running streams for inference — is there a recommended retry strategy if a stream fails mid-run?
- Also, can you hold any changes to our billing/usage metrics while this is active? We need to make sure we don't throttle legitimate traffic.

We’ll hold new long-running streams until you confirm the hotfix status. Please include the expected rollback process if we see regression.

Diane Park
Head of Platform, Acme Analytics


From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
To: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 11:05:21 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

On Tue, 07 May 2029 10:25:33 -0700 Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com> wrote:
> Thanks for the quick note, Sean. Appreciate the proactive communication. A couple quick questions before you proceed:

Short answers and current state:
- No evidence of permanent data loss in the traces we gathered. The issue is coherency/staleness under very specific rebalance conditions — you may see duplicates or out-of-order lines, but writes appear intact. We attached trace snippets in diag-logs-20290507.tar.gz.
- Retry guidance: Safe to retry idempotent requests or to resume short streams by restarting the session. For long-running streams, prefer a fast reconnect + resume token (we can provide an SDK helper if you want). We do NOT advise blind retries for non-idempotent multi-step pipelines; coordinate with us if you have in-flight transactional sequences.
- Billing: we will not count any anomalous duplicate responses toward your bill; our billing pipeline will mark tokens during the window as reviewable and we will follow up with a corrected usage report.

Update: hotfix v1.2.3 completed rolling deploy to the first two shards at 11:02 PDT with the consistency guard enabled and emergency locks active. No customer-facing errors observed in the first batch beyond marginal latency blips. We will continue rolling; estimated completion 11:40 PDT.

Please run the verification curl above when you can. If you prefer, run the lightweight SDK check: redwood.verify.kv_check(\"verify:kv-check-123\").

Rollback plan (if needed):
- Immediate revert to previous build and removal of emergency locks (target <20 minutes) using our runbook: https://redwood.console/runbooks/KP-591. The runbook is attached as runbook-rollback.pdf. We’ll only initiate rollback if we observe increased error rates >1% or systemic token duplication on your tenant.

I’ll post another update at completion. Marissa will be your escalation lead if anything degrades.

—
Sean Gallagher
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Diane Park <diane@acmeanalytics.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Analytics Ops <ops@acmeanalytics.com>
Date: Tue, 07 May 2029 15:40:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Urgent: deploy of consistency-guard hotfix and verification steps

On Tue, 07 May 2029 11:05:21 -0700 Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com> wrote:
> Update: hotfix v1.2.3 completed rolling deploy to the first two shards at 11:02 PDT with the consistency guard enabled and emergency locks active.

Thanks — we ran the smoke check after you completed the rollout and got a successful response for the sample prompt. One production stream needed a single manual retry (the client observed a short disconnect). No data corruption observed on our end.

We appreciate the billing note — please send the corrected usage report and the postmortem timeline (root cause, why the rebalance race happened, mitigation steps) when available. Can you also confirm whether we need to take any action to clear local caches or is the new guard transparent to clients?

Diane Park
Head of Platform, Acme Analytics

---
Sean/GTM team: please follow up with a short postmortem and link to the incident page. We will share that with our internal stakeholders and the customer success team on our side.

source.subject: Dedicated capacity: regional selection & provisioning cadence (Calyx Health)
messages:
From: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@calyxhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing

Hi Laura,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited to get the Dedicated capacity onboarding moving. Attached is the SOW and our initial traffic profile. A few asks to help us line up procurement and netops: 

- Primary question: do you recommend a single-region primary (us-west) with cross-region hot-standby (us-east), or split-primary across both? We expect most traffic from west coast customers but have large east coast accounts we can’t risk losing during an outage.
- Expected sustained QPS: 120 requests/min for the first 30 days, with occasional peaks up to 600 req/min during batch processing windows (approx 45–90m).
- Preferred autoscale behavior: low-latency for small interactive requests, ability to burst for batch jobs. If you have a short template for min/warm/burst pool sizing we can include in the SOW.
- Procurement will need estimated monthly committed GPUs and a short justification doc.

Can you propose a short plan (region choice, min/warm/burst numbers, and a 2-week activation timeline) and we’ll book a wrap-up call with netops/procurement?

Attachment: Calyx_Onboarding_SOW.pdf

Thanks,
Rajesh
Head of Platform Engineering
Calyx Health
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2029 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing

Hi Rajesh — great to see the SOW. I pulled a few items together and put a short checklist attached (redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx). Quick clarifying questions before we finalize recommendations: 

- Are the 120 qps and 600 peak numbers measured as single-token calls or full-generation requests (avg output length)? This affects KV cache sizing and kernel choice.
- Which models do you expect to serve (open LLaMA variant, or a larger fine-tuned model)? If you plan to bring custom weights we need the build artifact by day 5 of the activation window.

Initial proposal (high level) — TL;DR: us-west primary, us-east warm standby. Details: 
- Primary region: us-west (lowest latency for your primary user base).
- Hot standby: us-east for failover and regional read-replicas.
- Min capacity: 2 GPUs always-on for control plane and small interactive traffic.
- Warm pool: 4 GPUs (kept primed with KV cache for common prefixes).
- Burst: allow up to +8 GPUs for scheduled batch windows (with autoscale cooldown of 10–15m).

Next steps:
1) Confirm model details and average token length.
2) Confirm whether Calyx wants cross-region read routing during normal ops or only for failover.
3) Book a 45-minute alignment call (I can propose slots).

I’ve looped in Monica (CS lead) and Sean (ops liaison) to get feasibility and runbook input. Attaching provisioning checklist.

Best,
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Apr 2029 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Calyx — region recommendation + ops notes (doR: 17 Apr)

Team — forwarding Rajesh’s SOW and our first-pass recommendation. Please reply with concrete readiness items and estimated lead times. I’ve quoted Rajesh below for context.

Proposal summary (for alignment with netops & procurement):
- Region plan: us-west primary, us-east hot-standby (active-passive). We recommend active routing from us-west; cross-region read-routing only on failover to avoid inter-region tail latency.
- Capacity sizing: min=2 (always-on), warm=4 (primed for low-latency), burst=+8 for scheduled heavy batches. Expect steady-state cost = committed 6 GPUs (2 min + 4 warm), burst charged as usage.
- Autoscale policy: continuous batching with short 60s aggregation window for small interactive flows; warm-burst policy with 10–15m cooldown after scale down.
- Netops asks: IP allowlist, BGP/peering reqs for cross-region failover, maintenance blackout windows.

Operational concerns: 
- Warm pool priming — we need a short prompt-set from Calyx to prefill KV cache.
- Model artifact: if they bring custom weights we need containerized artifacts and a reproducible build — target delivery by Day 5.
- Monitoring: add the Calyx route to our per-route latency dashboards and set an initial QoQ error budget for the first 14 days.

Please add: rough ETA for IP allowlist provisioning, confirmation on region quota availability from infra, and a short cost estimate for committed vs burst (monthly).

Thanks,
Laura
From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Apr 2029 14:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Calyx — region recommendation + ops notes (doR: 17 Apr)

Quick ops notes: 
- Netops: allowlist process typically 3 business days once we have CIDR blocks and owner contacts. Ask Calyx to provide a single ops contact and a ticket to their firewall team.
- Warm pool priming: recommend we run a scheduled priming job 30m before known batch windows; that will reduce cold-starts for KV-heavy workloads.
- Autoscale knobs: propose min=2, warm=4, burst_ceiling=12 (total). Burst policy = warm-burst (scale to warm pool first, then bring additional machines). Scale down cooldown 10m; scale up aggressive for burst windows.
- Cost estimator: for committed 6xA100-equivalent GPUs ~= estimate $37k/mo (ballpark), burst usage billed on usage; include a 10% buffer for regional egress until we validate traffic.

Link to deal + notes: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/8741

I’ll draft a short operations runbook page (runbook_draft.pdf) and drop into the shared drive after this thread.

—Priyom
From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Calyx Health Procurement <procurement@calyxhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Apr 2029 15:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing — proposed plan

Rajesh — thanks for the SOW. Below is the recommended activation plan we’ll include in the SOW addendum; please confirm and we’ll schedule a 45-minute alignment call with NetOps and Procurement.

Proposed activation plan (summary):
- Region selection: us-west primary, us-east hot standby (active-passive). No cross-region read-routing during normal ops — failover only.
- Capacity: committed = 6 GPUs (2 min + 4 warm). Burst ceiling = 12 GPUs total for scheduled batch windows.
- Autoscale: warm-burst policy; 60s batching window for small requests; aggressive scale-up for bursts, 10–15m cooldown on scale-down.
- Warm pool priming: Calyx to provide a 50–100 prompt-set (representative workloads) to seed KV cache; Redwood will run a priming job before each scheduled batch window.
- Deliverables & timeline:
  - Day 0: SOW addendum signed and CIDR/IP allowlist provided by Calyx.
  - Day 1–3: Infra provisioning and quota checks (us-west + us-east).
  - Day 4–6: Model artifact ingestion and validation (if custom).
  - Day 7–10: Warm pool priming, monitoring dashboards, and runbook handoff.
  - Day 11–14: Go/no-go and soft launch.

Attachments: redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx, runbook_draft.pdf

Propose call slots: Fri 19 Apr 10:00–10:45 PT or Mon 22 Apr 14:00–14:45 PT. Which works best for you + your NetOps contact?

Thanks — looping Procurement so they have the committed numbers for the SOW addendum.

Best regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
redwood.com | laura@redwood.com

text:
From: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@calyxhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2029 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing

Hi Laura,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited to get the Dedicated capacity onboarding moving. Attached is the SOW and our initial traffic profile. A few asks to help us line up procurement and netops: 

- Primary question: do you recommend a single-region primary (us-west) with cross-region hot-standby (us-east), or split-primary across both? We expect most traffic from west coast customers but have large east coast accounts we can’t risk losing during an outage.
- Expected sustained QPS: 120 requests/min for the first 30 days, with occasional peaks up to 600 req/min during batch processing windows (approx 45–90m).
- Preferred autoscale behavior: low-latency for small interactive requests, ability to burst for batch jobs. If you have a short template for min/warm/burst pool sizing we can include in the SOW.
- Procurement will need estimated monthly committed GPUs and a short justification doc.

Can you propose a short plan (region choice, min/warm/burst numbers, and a 2-week activation timeline) and we’ll book a wrap-up call with netops/procurement?

Attachment: Calyx_Onboarding_SOW.pdf

Thanks,
Rajesh
Head of Platform Engineering
Calyx Health


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Apr 2029 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing

Hi Rajesh — great to see the SOW. I pulled a few items together and put a short checklist attached (redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx). Quick clarifying questions before we finalize recommendations: 

- Are the 120 qps and 600 peak numbers measured as single-token calls or full-generation requests (avg output length)? This affects KV cache sizing and kernel choice.
- Which models do you expect to serve (open LLaMA variant, or a larger fine-tuned model)? If you plan to bring custom weights we need the build artifact by day 5 of the activation window.

Initial proposal (high level) — TL;DR: us-west primary, us-east warm standby. Details: 
- Primary region: us-west (lowest latency for your primary user base).
- Hot standby: us-east for failover and regional read-replicas.
- Min capacity: 2 GPUs always-on for control plane and small interactive traffic.
- Warm pool: 4 GPUs (kept primed with KV cache for common prefixes).
- Burst: allow up to +8 GPUs for scheduled batch windows (with autoscale cooldown of 10–15m).

Next steps:
1) Confirm model details and average token length.
2) Confirm whether Calyx wants cross-region read routing during normal ops or only for failover.
3) Book a 45-minute alignment call (I can propose slots).

I’ve looped in Monica (CS lead) and Sean (ops liaison) to get feasibility and runbook input. Attaching provisioning checklist.

Best,
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Apr 2029 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Calyx — region recommendation + ops notes (doR: 17 Apr)

Team — forwarding Rajesh’s SOW and our first-pass recommendation. Please reply with concrete readiness items and estimated lead times. I’ve quoted Rajesh below for context.

Proposal summary (for alignment with netops & procurement):
- Region plan: us-west primary, us-east hot-standby (active-passive). We recommend active routing from us-west; cross-region read-routing only on failover to avoid inter-region tail latency.
- Capacity sizing: min=2 (always-on), warm=4 (primed for low-latency), burst=+8 for scheduled heavy batches. Expect steady-state cost = committed 6 GPUs (2 min + 4 warm), burst charged as usage.
- Autoscale policy: continuous batching with short 60s aggregation window for small interactive flows; warm-burst policy with 10–15m cooldown after scale down.
- Netops asks: IP allowlist, BGP/peering reqs for cross-region failover, maintenance blackout windows.

Operational concerns: 
- Warm pool priming — we need a short prompt-set from Calyx to prefill KV cache.
- Model artifact: if they bring custom weights we need containerized artifacts and a reproducible build — target delivery by Day 5.
- Monitoring: add the Calyx route to our per-route latency dashboards and set an initial QoQ error budget for the first 14 days.

Please add: rough ETA for IP allowlist provisioning, confirmation on region quota availability from infra, and a short cost estimate for committed vs burst (monthly).

Thanks,
Laura


From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Apr 2029 14:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Calyx — region recommendation + ops notes (doR: 17 Apr)

Quick ops notes: 
- Netops: allowlist process typically 3 business days once we have CIDR blocks and owner contacts. Ask Calyx to provide a single ops contact and a ticket to their firewall team.
- Warm pool priming: recommend we run a scheduled priming job 30m before known batch windows; that will reduce cold-starts for KV-heavy workloads.
- Autoscale knobs: propose min=2, warm=4, burst_ceiling=12 (total). Burst policy = warm-burst (scale to warm pool first, then bring additional machines). Scale down cooldown 10m; scale up aggressive for burst windows.
- Cost estimator: for committed 6xA100-equivalent GPUs ~= estimate $37k/mo (ballpark), burst usage billed on usage; include a 10% buffer for regional egress until we validate traffic.

Link to deal + notes: https://app.hubspot.com/deal/8741

I’ll draft a short operations runbook page (runbook_draft.pdf) and drop into the shared drive after this thread.

—Priyom


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Rajesh Malhotra <rajesh@calyxhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.com>, Calyx Health Procurement <procurement@calyxhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Apr 2029 15:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Calyx onboarding: kickoff info + ask on region and burst sizing — proposed plan

Rajesh — thanks for the SOW. Below is the recommended activation plan we’ll include in the SOW addendum; please confirm and we’ll schedule a 45-minute alignment call with NetOps and Procurement.

Proposed activation plan (summary):
- Region selection: us-west primary, us-east hot standby (active-passive). No cross-region read-routing during normal ops — failover only.
- Capacity: committed = 6 GPUs (2 min + 4 warm). Burst ceiling = 12 GPUs total for scheduled batch windows.
- Autoscale: warm-burst policy; 60s batching window for small requests; aggressive scale-up for bursts, 10–15m cooldown on scale-down.
- Warm pool priming: Calyx to provide a 50–100 prompt-set (representative workloads) to seed KV cache; Redwood will run a priming job before each scheduled batch window.
- Deliverables & timeline:
  - Day 0: SOW addendum signed and CIDR/IP allowlist provided by Calyx.
  - Day 1–3: Infra provisioning and quota checks (us-west + us-east).
  - Day 4–6: Model artifact ingestion and validation (if custom).
  - Day 7–10: Warm pool priming, monitoring dashboards, and runbook handoff.
  - Day 11–14: Go/no-go and soft launch.

Attachments: redwood_provisioning-checklist.xlsx, runbook_draft.pdf

Propose call slots: Fri 19 Apr 10:00–10:45 PT or Mon 22 Apr 14:00–14:45 PT. Which works best for you + your NetOps contact?

Thanks — looping Procurement so they have the committed numbers for the SOW addendum.

Best regards,
Laura Bennett
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
redwood.com | laura@redwood.com

source.subject: Wiring diagram for POC owners, legal gates & procurement milestones
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Post-demo recap + wiring diagram proposal

Hi Ethan / Priya —

Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a proposed "wiring diagram" to make the POC procurement path as predictable as possible. High-level notes from our side: 

- Success criteria we heard: (1) 99th pct latency < 500ms for chat endpoints; (2) automated logging of token-level usage for billing and audit; (3) data residency limited to US-east region.
- Suggested POC scope: 4-week technical trial with Redwood Dedicated (single GPU reservation), sample privacy-safe dataset, and two integration points: chat widget and backend batch scoring.
- Key owners we surfaced on your side: Ethan (technical sponsor), Priya (procurement), plus an expected legal reviewer.

Attached is a draft MAP (CareWell_MAP_v1.xlsx) that breaks the POC into milestones, owners, and gating docs. Proposed next steps: 
1) You confirm the procurement route (can procurement sign a 30-day trial SOW?)
2) We provide SOC2 summary + short evidence pack for legal (attached)
3) Align on integration kickoff date (we suggested 10/17)

If that looks right, can we lock in a 30-min kickoff for Tues 10/17? I’ll loop in engineering and the CS lead.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0134

Attachments: CareWell_MAP_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Redwood_SOC2-Summary.pdf (pdf)
From: Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-demo recap + wiring diagram proposal

Thanks Aisha — very helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Procurement: our preferred path is a short SOW (30 days) but procurement needs a pricing ceiling and liability language. Priya is looped for that.
- Legal/security: our counsel will want SOC2 Type II and a data-retention statement; can you confirm whether Redwood supports 90-day retention for logs and a delete workflow for KV caches?
- Stakeholder: I’ll be the technical owner; our CISO (Ravi Menon) will be the legal point. I’ll introduce him if we need contract redlines.

On milestones: the MAP looks good. Can you add a line item for a security review completion (we aim to finish internal review within 7 business days after SOC2/evidence delivery)? Also include an acceptance sign-off step we can send to our legal.

Happy to take kickoff 10/17 — 10:00am PT works for me.

—Ethan

On Thu, Oct 4, 2028 at 09:15 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Ethan / Priya —
> 
> Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a proposed "wiring diagram" to make the POC procurement path as predictable as possible. High-level notes from our side: 
> 
> - Success criteria we heard: (1) 99th pct latency < 500ms for chat endpoints; (2) automated logging of token-level usage for billing and audit; (3) data residency limited to US-east region.
> - Suggested POC scope: 4-week technical trial with Redwood Dedicated (single GPU reservation), sample privacy-safe dataset, and two integration points: chat widget and backend batch scoring.
> - Key owners we surfaced on your side: Ethan (technical sponsor), Priya (procurement), plus an expected legal reviewer.
> 
> Attached is a draft MAP (CareWell_MAP_v1.xlsx) that breaks the POC into milestones, owners, and gating docs. Proposed next steps: 
> 1) You confirm the procurement route (can procurement sign a 30-day trial SOW?)
> 2) We provide SOC2 summary + short evidence pack for legal (attached)
> 3) Align on integration kickoff date (we suggested 10/17)
> 
> If that looks right, can we lock in a 30-min kickoff for Tues 10/17? I’ll loop in engineering and the CS lead.
> 
> Best,
> Aisha
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: CareWell AI Procurement <procurement@carewell.ai>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>
Cc: Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 11:10:00 -0700
Subject: Draft SOW pricing ceiling + MAP update

Hi Priya / Procurement team —

Per the ask, attaching a one-page SOW template and updated MAP with a pricing ceiling for the 30-day POC. Pricing assumptions: reserved single-GPU Dedicated estimate = $9,500 for the 30-day reservation (includes limited burst), engineering onboarding (one engineer, up to 12 hrs), and priority support for the trial. We can convert to a time-and-materials model if you prefer.

I've added a security review milestone and a legal signoff step to the MAP. See the attached CareWell_RFP.pdf (we captured the procurement items you listed).

Next ask from us: please share the name and email for the person who will receive the SOW and handle redlines (we typically get those through DocuSign).

Best,
Aisha

Attachments: CareWell_RFP.pdf (pdf)
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
To: Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>, Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 14:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft SOW pricing ceiling + MAP update

Adding a couple items from the operations side: 

- We'll prepare a short runbook that outlines the integration steps for the chat endpoint and the batch scoring job (deliverable before kickoff).
- On pricing: we can add a single-line MR for an overage cap and include a refundable credit if we exceed estimated token usage by >20%.
- For the procurement flow, does your legal prefer a master services agreement (MSA) + SOW, or executing a standalone SOW for this POC? That will determine the DocuSign flow.

If you can share the procurement contact, we’ll prepare the DocuSign envelope ready to send after internal review.

—Sean

On Thu, Oct 4, 2028 at 11:10 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Priya / Procurement team —
> 
> Per the ask, attaching a one-page SOW template and updated MAP with a pricing ceiling for the 30-day POC. Pricing assumptions: reserved single-GPU Dedicated estimate = $9,500 for the 30-day reservation (includes limited burst), engineering onboarding (one engineer, up to 12 hrs), and priority support for the trial. We can convert to a time-and-materials model if you prefer.
> 
> I've added a security review milestone and a legal signoff step to the MAP. See the attached CareWell_RFP.pdf (we captured the procurement items you listed).
> 
> Next ask from us: please share the name and email for the person who will receive the SOW and handle redlines (we typically get those through DocuSign).
> 
> Best,
> Aisha
From: Priya Nair <priya.nair@carewell.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Cc: Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai>, CareWell AI Procurement <procurement@carewell.ai>
Date: Thu, 04 Oct 2028 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Draft SOW pricing ceiling + MAP update

Hi Aisha / Sean —

Thanks for the clear MAP and SOW draft. Procurement is comfortable with a standalone 30-day SOW for the POC. A few items we need before we can sign: 

1) SOC2 Type II report (or a summary + auditor contact) — your SOC2 summary PDF is a good start but we will request the auditor contact.
2) Data retention and deletion workflow for cached data (we need confirmation in writing of the 90-day retention and a record deletion procedure).
3) A named signatory for the SOW (we handle redlines via DocuSign; please provide contact for the signatory).

Our legal/ciso (Ravi Menon) will be copied on the next email once we have the SOC2 auditor contact. We aim to complete procurement review within 5 business days after receipt of the SOC2 auditor info.

Priya Nair
Director, Procurement — CareWell AI
procurement@carewell.ai

On Thu, Oct 4, 2028 at 10:02 AM Ethan Rivers <ethan@carewell.ai> wrote:
> Thanks Aisha — very helpful. A few clarifications from our side: 
> 
> - Procurement: our preferred path is a short SOW (30 days) but procurement needs a pricing ceiling and liability language. Priya is looped for that.
> - Legal/security: our counsel will want SOC2 Type II and a data-retention statement; can you confirm whether Redwood supports 90-day retention for logs and a delete workflow for KV caches?
> - Stakeholder: I’ll be the technical owner; our CISO (Ravi Menon) will be the legal point. I’ll introduce him if we need contract redlines.
> 
> On milestones: the MAP looks good. Can you add a line item for a security review completion (we aim to finish internal review within 7 business days after SOC2/evidence delivery)? Also include an acceptance sign-off step we can send to our legal.
> 
> Happy to take kickoff 10/17 — 10:00am PT works for me.
> 
> —Ethan

- Legal/security: our counsel will want SOC2 Type II and a data-retention statement; can you confirm whether Redwood supports 90-day retention for logs and a delete workflow for KV caches?
- Stakeholder: I’ll be the technical owner; our CISO (Ravi Menon) will be the legal point. I’ll introduce him if we need contract redlines.

On milestones: the MAP looks good. Can you add a line item for a security review completion (we aim to finish internal review within 7 business days after SOC2/evidence delivery)? Also include an acceptance sign-off step we can send to our legal.

Happy to take kickoff 10/17 — 10:00am PT works for me.

—Ethan

On Thu, Oct 4, 2028 at 09:15 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Ethan / Priya —
> 
> Thanks again for taking the demo this morning. Quick recap and a proposed "wiring diagram" to make the POC procurement path as predictable as possible. High-level notes from our side: 
> 
> - Success criteria we heard: (1) 99th pct latency < 500ms for chat endpoints; (2) automated logging of token-level usage for billing and audit; (3) data residency limited to US-east region.
> - Suggested POC scope: 4-week technical trial with Redwood Dedicated (single GPU reservation), sample privacy-safe dataset, and two integration points: chat widget and backend batch scoring.
> - Key owners we surfaced on your side: Ethan (technical sponsor), Priya (procurement), plus an expected legal reviewer.
> 
> Attached is a draft MAP (CareWell_MAP_v1.xlsx) that breaks the POC into milestones, owners, and gating docs. Proposed next steps: 
> 1) You confirm the procurement route (can procurement sign a 30-day trial SOW?)
> 2) We provide SOC2 summary + short evidence pack for legal (attached)
> 3) Align on integration kickoff date (we suggested 10/17)
> 
> If that looks right, can we lock in a 30-min kickoff for Tues 10/17? I’ll loop in engineering and the CS lead.
> 
> Best,
> Aisha

Per the ask, attaching a one-page SOW template and updated MAP with a pricing ceiling for the 30-day POC. —Sean

Subject: BrightBridge visit — Redwood demo & NDA

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the screening call. We'd like to schedule an on-site demo at Redwood HQ for our data & platform team (6 people). Could you share 2–3 available windows in late September / early October? Ideally a single conference suite with AV and a small breakout area.

We can sign an NDA ahead of time — can you send the preferred DocuSign link? Also, please let us know any visitor requirements (badging, laptop policy, parking).

Samantha

BrightBridge

Subject: Re: BrightBridge visit — Redwood demo & NDA

Hi Samantha — great to hear.

Thanks for the windows. We have two options we can offer at the moment:
- Sept 28, 13:00–15:30 (Main Suite A — AV rack + projector)
- Oct 3, 10:00–12:30 (Conference 3B — smaller, good for a focused demo)

Laura is on this thread to confirm actual room availability and convert any existing hold to a confirmed reservation once the NDA is in place. Monica will coordinate badges and visitor logistics.

For the NDA, here's our DocuSign flow: https://www.docusign.com/transaction/XYZ-12345 — if you e-sign, please forward the signed PDF back to us.

Quick note on visitor policy: laptops are allowed in demo rooms but we ask guests to connect via our guest wifi (details provided at check-in). Parking is limited — street parking or the visitor lot (lot C) is recommended; we can reserve a temporary permit if you let us know license plates.

Attached: visitor_requirements.pdf (short policy + parking instructions).

If Sept 28 works, we can tentatively block Main Suite A for 13:00–16:00 and firm it after NDA.

Cheers,
Aditya
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference

Attachments: visitor_requirements.pdf (PDF, 48KB)

To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Samantha Briggs <samantha.briggs@brightbridge.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-09-07T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BrightBridge visit — Redwood demo & NDA

Hi all — quick updates from space ops:

1) Main Suite A is currently on a tentative hold for another partner on Sept 28 until Sept 10. I can place a soft hold for BrightBridge and add you to the waitlist; if the other hold converts, we'll offer Conference 2C (similar AV, smaller footprint) or push to Oct 3 in Conference 3B.

2) I attached the AV checklist (suite_av_setup.docx) and the badge intake form (badge-form.pdf). Please have attendees fill out the badge form with full legal names and the last 4 of SSN/ID for visitor processing; bring a government ID for pick-up.

3) Catering: we can do boxed lunches (menu options in the AV doc). Need final headcount by 72 hours before.

I'll keep the soft hold for Sept 28 13:00–16:00 and will convert to confirmed once we receive the signed NDA PDF. If you want the guarantee, Oct 3 in 3B is available now and I can confirm immediately.

Best,
Laura
Venue & Events Coordinator
Redwood Inference

Attachments: suite_av_setup.docx (DOCX, 120KB); badge-form.pdf (PDF, 65KB)

To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@brightbridge.com
Date: 2028-09-08T14:41:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BrightBridge visit — Redwood demo & NDA

I e-signed the DocuSign agreement earlier today and attached the signed copy here. We can do Sept 28 if the soft hold converts — prefer Main Suite A if possible.

A couple of quick Qs:

- Can you do 1 vegetarian boxed lunch out of 6?

Attached: signed_brightbridge_ndas.pdf (PDF, 210KB)

Samantha

To: Samantha Briggs <samantha.briggs@brightbridge.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>

Confirming:
- Main Suite A is now converted to a confirmed reservation for Sept 28, 13:00–16:00.
- Late arrivals are fine; on arrival, guests should check in at the lobby kiosk and call the on-site contact. For your group the on-site contact is Sean Gallagher (sean.gallagher@redwood.com, ext. 4301). He can meet late arrivals at the suite door.
- Catering: 6 boxed lunches, 1 vegetarian — noted. Final headcount deadline: 2028-09-25 11:59 PDT.

source.subject: Vendor onboarding: Crestline Instruments vendor master creation
messages:
From: Oliver Hayes <oliver.hayes@crestline.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Aug 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding: Crestline Instruments — vendor master intake

Hi Kimberly,

Thanks for the quick connect earlier — following up with the vendor documents for Crestline Instruments so we can get set up in your vendor master. Attached are the initial items your team requested during the intake call:

- Crestline_W9.pdf (completed & signed W-9)
- Crestline_BankLetter.pdf (bank letter for ACH setup)
- Crestline_InvoiceSample.xlsx (sample invoice, PO references included)

A few quick details to help on your side:
- Legal name: Crestline Instruments, Inc.
- DBA: Crestline Labs
- EIN: 12-3456789
- Billing contact: Maya Chen, Billing Manager, maya.chen@crestline.com, +1 415-555-0199
- Preferred invoice email: invoices@crestline.com
- Preferred PO email: purchasing@crestline.com
- Payment terms: Net 30 (we can discuss)
- We accept ACH; bank details are in the attached bank letter.

Let us know if you need any notarized docs, certified signatures, or additional beneficial ownership forms. Happy to jump on a 15-minute call if that speeds things up.

Thanks,
Oliver Hayes
Accounts Receivable Manager
Crestline Instruments
oliver.hayes@crestline.com
+1 415-555-0123

Attachments: Crestline_W9.pdf (W-9), Crestline_BankLetter.pdf (bank letter), Crestline_InvoiceSample.xlsx (sample invoice)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Hayes <oliver.hayes@crestline.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Aug 2028 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Vendor onboarding: Crestline Instruments — vendor master intake

Hi Oliver — appreciate the attachments and the clear summary. A few clarifying asks before I create the vendor master record in our ERP: 

1) Can you confirm the legal entity name exactly as it should appear on tax/PO documents? (Your W-9 lists a line item; want to match that to avoid tax withholding issues.)
2) Please confirm the remit/billing address to put on the vendor profile (physical address vs PO box).
3) Are there any alternate invoice contacts or EDI routing IDs we should capture? If you prefer, we can map a primary invoice email and a secondary for escalation.
4) Banking: bank letter is helpful — please confirm that the account owner matches the legal entity above (required for ACH enrollment).
5) If you have a vendor portal URL or vendor ID in your systems, include that so we can map vendor references.

Next steps on our side: I will create the vendor stub once we have the exact legal name, remit address, and confirm the primary invoice email. Finance (Marcus) — can you confirm we need a W-8/W-9 verification step for this entity and whether we should flag any tax withholding? Procurement (Sean) — once approved, I will set PO routing to purchasing@crestline.com unless you instruct otherwise.

If easier, Oliver, a short call tomorrow morning to walk through would work — I'm available 9–11am PT.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Vendor Onboarding Manager
Redwood Inference

Hi Oliver — appreciate the attachments and the clear summary. A few clarifying asks before I create the vendor master record in our ERP: 

1) Can you confirm the legal entity name exactly as it should appear on tax/PO documents? (Your W-9 lists a line item; want to match that to avoid tax withholding issues.)

3) Are there any alternate invoice contacts or EDI routing IDs we should capture? If you prefer, we can map a primary invoice email and a secondary for escalation.
4) Banking: bank letter is helpful — please confirm that the account owner matches the legal entity above (required for ACH enrollment).
5) If you have a vendor portal URL or vendor ID in your systems, include that so we can map vendor references.

Next steps on our side: I will create the vendor stub once we have the exact legal name, remit address, and confirm the primary invoice email. Finance (Marcus) — can you confirm we need a W-8/W-9 verification step for this entity and whether we should flag any tax withholding? Procurement (Sean) — once approved, I will set PO routing to purchasing@crestline.com unless you instruct otherwise.

If easier, Oliver, a short call tomorrow morning to walk through would work — I'm available 9–11am PT.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park

source.subject: BlueOak / Redwood integrator entry criteria & first 30 days
messages:
From: Tom Kepler <tom.kepler@blueoak.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lisa Chen <lisa.chen@blueoak.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Partner onboarding inquiry: BlueOak x Redwood

Hi Connor,

Thanks for the intro from Lisa. BlueOak is evaluating integrator partnerships to support a planned implementation wave for several enterprise customers in Q4. We typically bring 15–25 consultants per engagement; our initial ask is: what does Redwood expect as "entry criteria" for an SI to get production enablement (sandbox access, training, licensed models, and co-sell leads)?

We'd like to understand:
- Minimum technical prerequisites (accounts, infra)
- Typical first-30-days enablement cadence
- Any compliance evidence (SOC2/pen test) you require before access
- Commercials for partner seats / sandbox tokens for PS teams

Attaching an initial RFQ with high-level requirements. Can we schedule a 30–45m kickoff call next week to go over the program and a sample SOW?

Thanks,
Tom Kepler
Director of Integrations
BlueOak Advisors

Attachment: initial_rfq.pdf (application/pdf, stub)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Kepler <tom.kepler@blueoak.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner onboarding inquiry: BlueOak x Redwood

Hi Tom — great to meet you over email. Welcome — BlueOak looks like a strong potential SI partner.

Short answers up front and then a proposed next step:
- Entry criteria: company MSA + basic security attestation (we provide a partner security checklist), two technical contacts, and one sandbox user per consultant for initial ramp.
- First 30 days: we run a "Partner Entry Canvas" (2-hour kickoff, 2 technical enablement sessions, access provisioning, and a sandbox lab). I attached our 30-day checklist for visibility.
- Compliance: we can share SOC2 Type II summary and controlled access notes; full evidence is shared under NDA / MSA.
- Commercials: partner seats are discounted on an annual consumption commitment; we can surface options during the kickoff.

Next step: can you do Friday 8/25 at 11am PT for a kickoff? If that works I'll send a calendar invite and a short pre-kick deck.

I'll include Camila (Partner Programs) and Sean (Solutions) on the thread for enablement logistics.

Regards,
Connor O'Brien
Director, Partner Sales | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0142

Attachment: 30day-entry-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, stub)

From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 13:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner onboarding inquiry: BlueOak x Redwood

Connor / Sean — pulling internal notes into the thread so we have a single source of truth (copying Connor's message below).

Planning bullets (internal):
- Pre-kickoff deliverables for BlueOak: partner-access-template.docx (IAM list), training roster (names + roles), and network/system inventory for sandbox configuration.
- Workshop plan: Day 0 (2h business kickoff), Day 3 (technical deep dive: model variants, deployment modes), Day 7 (hands-on lab: Redwood Console + Dedicated routing), Day 14 (billing/cost controls + Optimize recommendations).
- Security: provide SOC2 summary and we will request a signed security addendum before issuing long-lived API keys. Short-lived sandbox keys OK for hands-on labs.
- Enablement owners: Camila = program lead; Sean = technical lead; Connor = GTM and escalation.
- Materials to prepare prior to 8/25: pre-kick slide deck, partner lab accounts, and a sample mutual action plan (MAP).

I'll attach the partner-access-template and draft the MAP this afternoon.

Cheers,
Camila Reyes
Partner Programs Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: partner-access-template.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, stub)

On Wed, Aug 22, 2028 at 11:05 AM Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Tom — great to meet you over email. Welcome — BlueOak looks like a strong potential SI partner.
>
> Short answers up front and then a proposed next step:
> - Entry criteria: company MSA + basic security attestation (we provide a partner security checklist), two technical contacts, and one sandbox user per consultant for initial ramp.
> - First 30 days: we run a "Partner Entry Canvas" (2-hour kickoff, 2 technical enablement sessions, access provisioning, and a sandbox lab). I attached our 30-day checklist for visibility.
> - Compliance: we can share SOC2 Type II summary and controlled access notes; full evidence is shared under NDA / MSA.
> - Commercials: partner seats are discounted on an annual consumption commitment; we can surface options during the kickoff.
>
> Next step: can you do Friday 8/25 at 11am PT for a kickoff? If that works I'll send a calendar invite and a short pre-kick deck.
>
> Regards,
> Connor O'Brien
> Director, Partner Sales | Redwood Inference

From: Tom Kepler <tom.kepler@blueoak.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lisa Chen <lisa.chen@blueoak.com>
Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 17:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner onboarding inquiry: BlueOak x Redwood

Connor / Camila / Sean — Friday 8/25 11am PT works for us, thanks. I've added Lisa and we'll send a brief pre-read by EOD Thursday. Also attaching the BlueOak system inventory you'll likely need for sandbox access.

I'll also get Legal looping for a simple MPA — if you can point me at the DocuSign template or sample MPA we use that will speed things.

See you Friday.

Best,
Tom
Director of Integrations
BlueOak Advisors

Attachment: blueoak_system_list.csv (text/csv, stub)

From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Tom Kepler <tom.kepler@blueoak.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Partner onboarding inquiry: BlueOak x Redwood

Hi Tom — great to meet you over email. Welcome — BlueOak looks like a strong potential SI partner.

Short answers up front and then a proposed next step:
- Entry criteria: company MSA + basic security attestation (we provide a partner security checklist), two technical contacts, and one sandbox user per consultant for initial ramp.
- First 30 days: we run a \"Partner Entry Canvas\" (2-hour kickoff, 2 technical enablement sessions, access provisioning, and a sandbox lab). I attached our 30-day checklist for visibility.
- Compliance: we can share SOC2 Type II summary and controlled access notes; full evidence is shared under NDA / MSA.
- Commercials: partner seats are discounted on an annual consumption commitment; we can surface options during the kickoff.

Next step: can you do Friday 8/25 at 11am PT for a kickoff? If that works I'll send a calendar invite and a short pre-kick deck.

I'll include Camila (Partner Programs) and Sean (Solutions) on the thread for enablement logistics.

Regards,
Connor O'Brien
Director, Partner Sales | Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0142

Attachment: 30day-entry-checklist.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet, stub)


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>

Date: Wed, 22 Aug 2028 13:27:00 -0700

Connor / Sean — pulling internal notes into the thread so we have a single source of truth (copying Connor's message below).

Planning bullets (internal):
- Pre-kickoff deliverables for BlueOak: partner-access-template.docx (IAM list), training roster (names + roles), and network/system inventory for sandbox configuration.
- Workshop plan: Day 0 (2h business kickoff), Day 3 (technical deep dive: model variants, deployment modes), Day 7 (hands-on lab: Redwood Console + Dedicated routing), Day 14 (billing/cost controls + Optimize recommendations).
- Security: provide SOC2 summary and we will request a signed security addendum before issuing long-lived API keys. Short-lived sandbox keys OK for hands-on labs.
- Enablement owners: Camila = program lead; Sean = technical lead; Connor = GTM and escalation.
- Materials to prepare prior to 8/25: pre-kick slide deck, partner lab accounts, and a sample mutual action plan (MAP).

I'll attach the partner-access-template and draft the MAP this afternoon.

Cheers,
Camila Reyes
Partner Programs Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: partner-access-template.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, stub)

messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Blake <tom.blake@windsortech.com>, Priya Rao <priya@windsortech.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Aug 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Windsor pilot — proposed demo + materials attached

Tom / Priya — thanks again for the time yesterday. As discussed, I've attached a one‑page pilot plan and the initial SOW draft so your product leads can get a sense of scope and TCO.

Proposed demo slot (live):
- Wednesday, Aug 22 — 10:30–11:15am PT (30–45 min demo + Q&A)

If that doesn't work, I can offer: Aug 23 2pm PT or Aug 28 11am PT.

Agenda (30–45 min):
- 10m: goals and success metrics; 10m: live inference demo (routing, latency, cost view); 10–20m: integration and next steps (SOW / timeline).

Attachments: Windsor_SOW_v1.pdf (application/pdf), Q2_pilot_plan.xlsx (spreadsheet)

Thanks — happy to adapt.

—Aditya
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
https://redwoodinference.com
From: Tom Blake <tom.blake@windsortech.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@windsortech.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Aug 2028 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Windsor pilot — proposed demo + materials attached

Aditya — thanks for the docs and the clear agenda. Quick update from our side: we just had a product‑planning review and the roadmap owner decided to shift focus for the next quarter toward a data‑platform integration project. That reprioritization means we need to pull the LLM/inference pilot off the immediate roadmap.

Additionally, a couple of the folks who would own the evaluation are traveling the week of Aug 20–24 (including me). Given those two constraints, we won't be able to commit to a live demo on Aug 22.

My ask: can we pause scheduling for now and pick this up after their roadmap cadence—likely in mid‑October? We still want to evaluate Redwood later this year; it's just not high priority for Q3.

If helpful, you'd be welcome to send the recording of the demo or a short recorded walkthrough for the internal team to watch asynchronously.

Appreciate your flexibility —
Tom
Tom Blake
Director of Product, Windsor Technologies
tom.blake@windsortech.com | (312) 555-2211
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 12 Aug 2028 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Windsor pilot — proposed demo + materials attached

Aditya / Sean — forwarding Tom's note. Quick thoughts:

- This is a classic roadmap siphon (data platform > inference this quarter). Prospect still interested but asks for a mid‑Oct revisit.
- Tom's travel window (Aug 20–24) creates a concrete scheduling blocker, separate from the deprioritization. We'll want to document both reasons in HubSpot.
- Pipeline action: move deal to "Nurture — Roadmap" stage, set a follow‑up task for Oct 12 to re‑engage the product owner.

Suggested response: offer three things (pick any):
1) A short recorded demo + one‑pager they can circulate (low lift; keeps momentum).
2) A hold of reserved demo slots in Oct (so they don't have to replay scheduling in Q4).
3) A small trial credit commitment contingent on re‑engage date (helps prioritize internally).

Also: ask Tom if the SOW / scope doc would be useful to share with procurement now (they might want cost visibility even if timelines shift).

cc: I'll log this in HubSpot (WD-2028-1493) and add notes.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 21 Aug 2028 16:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Windsor pilot — proposed demo + materials attached

Tom — thanks for the transparency and the timeline. Totally understand the roadmap pull — we've seen this pattern often.

To keep things simple and useful for your team while you focus on the data platform work, here's what we can do (pick any or all):

1) I can record a 15–20 minute walkthrough of the demo (no live QA) and send the recording + slide summary for your team to watch asynchronously.
2) Reserve two 45‑minute demo slots in the week of Oct 15 so your PMs can book without extra back‑and‑forth. Suggested times: Oct 16 10:30am PT / Oct 18 1:30pm PT.
3) Offer a $2k trial credit applied if you restart the pilot by Nov 30 (simple paperwork) — if that would help unblock prioritization internally, we can surface it to your procurement.

If you prefer we completely pause, say the word and we'll archive the SOW and revisit in Oct. Otherwise, send me which option you want and I'll action it.

Attachment: Windsor_SOW_v1.pdf (application/pdf) — happy for you to share with procurement. HubSpot record: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345 (WD-2028-1493)

Thanks again — safe travels next week.

—Aditya

source.subject: Dedicated sizing: latency & token landscape — follow-up questions
messages:
From: Lina Ortega <lina@aperture.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procure Team <procure@aperture.ai>, DevOps Contact <ops+aperture@aperture.ai>
Date: 2028-08-07T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Dedicated sizing: initial token/latency profile

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the demo last week — team is keen to move to a reserved/dedicated footprint for our customer-facing chat endpoint. Attached is a 48-hour sample of traffic and the security questionnaire the procurement team requested. High-level:
- Peak concurrent sessions: ~1,200 (15:00–16:00 PDT)
- Typical prompt mix: 70% short (<=40 tokens), 20% medium (41–200), 10% long (200–2,000)
- Average response lengths: 60 tokens for short prompts, 450 tokens for medium, ~1,400 tokens for long
- We have a p99 latency SLO of 300ms for short prompts; medium/long can tolerate up to 1,000ms but prefer <800ms

Can you confirm what GPU mix you’d recommend and whether token-level caching or session-affinity would materially change fleet sizing for us? We can jump on a quick call if needed.

Thanks,
Lina Ortega
Head of Platform Engineering
Aperture AI

Attachments: aperture_traffic_sample.csv, aperture_security_req.pdf

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Lina Ortega <lina@aperture.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-08-07T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing: initial token/latency profile

Lina — thanks for the sample and for the clear SLOs. Quick read of the CSV looks sensible; a few clarifying asks before we produce a preliminary sizing:

1) Session behavior and KV cache reuse:
   - Of the concurrent sessions you mentioned, what fraction are multi-turn (expected KV cache reuse across requests) vs stateless single-shot interactions? If you don’t have exact numbers, a rough split (e.g., 60/40) is enough.

2) Burst characteristics:
   - You mentioned the 15:00–16:00 peak. Is that a sustained plateau (60 min at peak) or a spike (5–10 min hot window inside that hour)?

3) Long-response tail handling:
   - For the long prompts (~1,400 tokens responses) do those originate from a small set of users (few heavy sessions) or broadly across many users? This impacts memory/KV allocation and transient occupancy.

4) Latency SLO enforcement policy:
   - Do you prefer strict tail guarantees (p99) for short flows (we’d size for headroom) or are you open to a hybrid: short prompt strict, fallback to a cheaper model for long prompts under overload?

We’ll attach a quick sizer workbook (redwood_prelim_sizer.xlsx) after this note once Marissa runs the initial throughput model. Sean — can you run a quick occupancy estimate for a mixed prompt distribution and recommend a GPU SKU mix for Aperture? I suspect a mix of q4_2/gn4x equivalents with some oversubscription for short prompts will be the right approach, but confirm.

Would you be available for a 30-minute call tomorrow 10:00–11:30 PDT to go over numbers?

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

> On 2028-08-07T08:12:00-07:00 Lina Ortega <lina@aperture.ai> wrote:
> > (attached: aperture_traffic_sample.csv, aperture_security_req.pdf)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-08-07T11:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing: initial token/latency profile

Looping in my initial calc — I ran a quick pass and uploaded the workbook (redwood_prelim_sizer.xlsx). Key assumptions I used (documented in the sheet):
- GPU candidate: 2x gn4x-equivalent (high-memory) nodes for long-response shards; 6x q4_2-equivalent for short/medium optimized lanes
- KV cache assumed reuse for 55% of concurrent sessions (based on our prompt/session heuristics)
- Burst model: primary peak is a 12-minute hot window inside the 60-minute range (based on the CSV inter-arrival distribution)
- Safety headroom: 25% for p99 under expected traffic; additional 15% buffer for deploy day

Preliminary estimate: to meet p99=300ms for short prompts and target 800ms for medium, you’ll need roughly 8-10 effective GPU slots (mix above) with autoscale to 14 slots for sustained long-response traffic. If you can tolerate a hybrid fallback (route long prompts to a half-precision long-tail model), the baseline can drop to ~6 effective slots.

Notes/questions for Aperture to confirm (I copied into the workbook):
- Exact multi-turn fraction (Karthik’s Q1)
- Do you have a preference for model variants we should include (open LLMs vs. our Redwood-verified mixes)?
- Any constraints on on-prem/private deployment (affects latency if VPC peering is involved)

I also added an attachment stub for a suggested rollout plan (tab: canary->scale).

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Senior Performance Engineer, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood_prelim_sizer.xlsx

title: Dedicated sizing: latency & token landscape — follow-up questions
preview:
From: Lina Ortega <lina@aperture.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procure Team <procure@aperture.ai>, DevOps Contact <ops+aperture@aperture.ai>
Date: 2028-08-07T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Dedicated sizing: initial token/latency profile

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the demo last week — team is keen to move to a reserved/dedicated footprint for our customer-facing chat endpoint. Attached is a 48-hour sample of traffic and the security questionnaire the procurement team requested. High-level:
- Peak concurrent sessions: ~1,200 (15:00–16:00 PDT)
- Typical prompt mix: 70% short (<=40 tokens), 20% medium (41–200), 10% long (200–2,000)
- Average response lengths: 60 tokens for short prompts, 450 tokens for medium, ~1,400 tokens for long
- We have a p99 latency SLO of 300ms for short prompts; medium/long can tolerate up to 1,000ms but prefer <800ms

Can you confirm what GPU mix you’d recommend and whether token-level caching or session-affinity would materially change fleet sizing for us? We can jump on a quick call if needed.

Thanks,
Lina Ortega
Head of Platform Engineering
Aperture AI

Attachments: aperture_traffic_sample.csv, aperture_security_req.pdf

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Lina Ortega <lina@aperture.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-08-07T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated sizing: initial token/latency profile

Lina — thanks for the sample and for the clear SLOs. Quick read of the CSV looks sensible; a few clarifying asks before we produce a preliminary sizing:

1) Session behavior and KV cache reuse:
   - Of the concurrent sessions you mentioned, what fraction are multi-turn (expected KV cache reuse across requests) vs stateless single-shot interactions? If you don’t have exact numbers, a rough split (e.g., 60/40) is enough.

2) Burst characteristics:
   - You mentioned the 15:00–16:00 peak. Is that a sustained plateau (60 min at peak) or a spike (5–10 min hot window inside that hour)?

3) Long-response tail handling:
   - For the long prompts (~1,400 tokens responses) do those originate from a small set of users (few heavy sessions) or broadly across many users? This impacts memory/KV allocation and transient occupancy.

4) Latency SLO enforcement policy:
   - Do you prefer strict tail guarantees (p99) for short flows (we’d size for headroom) or are you open to a hybrid: short prompt strict, fallback to a cheaper model for long prompts under overload?

We’ll attach a quick sizer workbook (redwood_prelim_sizer.xlsx) after this note once Marissa runs the initial throughput model. Sean — can you run a quick occupancy estimate for a mixed prompt distribution and recommend a GPU SKU mix for Aperture? I suspect a mix of q4_2/gn4x equivalents with some oversubscription for short prompts will be the right approach, but confirm.

Would you be available for a 30-minute call tomorrow 10:00–11:30 PDT to go over numbers?

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
