Tom / Marta —

Thanks for flagging this. We understand the impact and apologize for the disruption. Our engineers have isolated the root cause to KV cache eviction pressure on the 32k serving pool and applied short-term mitigations (throttling and a tenant-only fallback to a 16k-optimized variant).

We will:
- Continue to monitor the canary for the next 2 hours and revert if we detect regressions.
- Provide a timeline for a permanent fix (KV eviction policy + instrumented metrics) within 72 hours.
- Offer a one-time credit equivalent to one week's hosted usage for the impacted tenant while we complete the fix (we can refine the amount with finance).

Best,
Aisha on behalf of Redwood Leadership
——

Please add suggested language re: credits and legal signoff if needed — we want to be ready if Tom asks for SLA credit and exec escalation.

— Aisha


From: Tom Hsu <tom.hsu@borealishealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@borealishealth.com>
Cc: infra-team@borealishealth.com
Date: 2028-04-11T12:03:00Z
Subject: Re: [SEV] Sudden p99 spikes / 500s on long-window (32k) requests

Appreciate the quick triage and the canary. Our infra team confirms that the bulk of errors were during the 07:00–08:10 UTC window and that the fallback to 16k kept the job progressing for non-critical batches.

Two asks:
1) Can you commit to a firm ETA for the permanent fix window (the 72-hour estimate is okay but we need a calendar date)?
2) We will expect a formal credit proposal from Redwood procurement that maps to impacted usage. Please put this in writing to our procurement contact (procure@borealishealth.com).

If you want to involve Redwood execs for faster approvals on credits, we can loop our VP of Platform.

— Tom
VP Product Ops, Borealis Health


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Dev Patel <dev_patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn_hart@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-04-11T15:42:00Z
Subject: Post-mortem next steps & commercial ask — Borealis Health

Summary and action items for internal follow-up (for the executive update):

Summary:
- Incident window: 2028-04-11T07:00 — 2028-04-11T08:40 UTC (most severe).
- Root cause: KV cache eviction pressure on the 32k-serving pool amplified batch latency under sudden concurrency spike.
- Short-term mitigations: tenant-only throttle + fallback to 16k variant (applied 07:40 UTC), purged stale cache segment. Canary looks stable; p99 returned to expected ranges by ~08:20 UTC.

Action items (owners & ETA):
- Dev: Ship eviction-friendly cache policy + additional telemetry to detect this pattern automatically (ETA: 2028-04-18).
- Sean: Draft the formal credit proposal and coordinate legal/finance signoff (ETA: 2028-04-12 EOD).
- Aisha: Send an exec note to Borealis (tom.hsu@borealishealth.com) with the proposed credit and timeline once Sean confirms numbers (ETA: 2028-04-11 17:00 UTC).
- Platform: Add tenant-scoped fallback capability to router config for similar long-window spikes (ETA: 2 weeks).

References: hubspot BH-8421, logs/drive link attached.

I'll prepare the exec note and run it by Evelyn for sign-off.

— Aisha
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference

source.subject: Storix throughput surge — unified command, stakeholder alert, and board notice
messages:
From: Samir Joshi <samir.j@storix.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Ops-Team <ops@storix.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T08:14:00-07:00
Subject: High error rates & end-to-end latency spike — immediate help needed

Evelyn / Redwood on-call,

We started seeing a dramatic increase in 5xxs and token-level latency across our checkout micro-batch pipeline at ~07:40 PT. Error rate climbed from ~0.3% to 6.4% and median response time jumped from ~190ms to ~1.8s. Our customers are seeing payment timeouts.

We have attached a short CSV with sample errors and timestamps. We need immediate triage and a plan we can share with the execs and our merchants. Can we get a short conference and an ETA to a mitigation?

Immediate contact: Samir Joshi (ops lead) — +1 415-555-0182.

(Attachment: storix-prod-errors-0800-1200.csv)

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Joshi
VP, Platform Operations
Storix

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Samir Joshi <samir.j@storix.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T08:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: High error rates & end-to-end latency spike — immediate help needed

Samir — thanks for flagging. We're standing up a unified command now.

What we need right away from you: a) recent model/variant being served, b) whether you saw concurrent traffic pattern changes (bursting clients), c) any recent routing overrides or pinned variant changes since midnight PT.

I'll pull telemetry and route resources. David/Kim — can you join the call at :30? Karthik, please start a temporary throttling plan we can push to Dedicated tenants if needed.

I'll send a short exec-readout after the first triage call.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T08:31:00-07:00
Subject: RE: High error rates & end-to-end latency spike — immediate help needed

On Apr 5, 2028, at 08:22, Evelyn Hart wrote:
> I'll pull telemetry and route resources. David/Kim — can you join the call at :30?

Joining now. Initial readout from runtime: we see a sharp increase in concurrent open KV cache sizes for the model variant "redwood-7b-slim-q8" on a subset of pods in us-west-2. Error patterns correlate with OOMs followed by retries; autoscaler registered repeated pod restarts between 07:38–07:52.

Mitigation plan (initial):
- Bump the pod-level memory guard (fast path) and trigger rolling restart of affected pool.
- Temporarily route 30% of traffic to a cache-warm variant (redwood-7b-warm) with smaller KV footprint.
- Apply circuit-breaker for clients with >80 RPS per IP.

I'll implement the route shifts and watch error rate delta. Sharing logs to the drive link and attaching quick notes.

(Attachment: initial-root-cause-notes.pdf)

-David

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: High error rates & end-to-end latency spike — immediate help needed

Team — short status to escalate to leadership and the board if we cannot get stable in the next hour.

Current status (09:05 PT):
- Error rate: 6.4% -> 2.1% after routing 30% to warm variant.
- Median latency: 1.8s -> 680ms (user impact persists for heavy batch clients).
- Root cause hypothesis: sudden high-cardinality session keys causing KV growth + a small regression in kernel memory footprint after yesterday's runtime tweak.

Immediate asks for exec note:
- Need approval for temporary capacity burst (reserve 12 GPUs in us-west-2) — estimate incremental cost: $8.5k/day.
- Agree on customer-facing message template and CEO-level alert.

If we cannot get sub-1s median by 10:30 PT, recommend full inbound throttling on non-critical merchants.

-Kimberly

From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Evans <laura.evans@kilnpay.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T09:42:00-07:00
Subject: Executive brief (internal + board heads) — Storix incident summary and requested approvals

Folks — drafting the executive summary to share with CEO / CFO / Board watchers. Posting here so you can flag edits. We must keep it concise and prescriptive.

Executive Summary (DRAFT):
- Incident: Elevated error rates and latency impacting Storix real-time payouts, onset 07:40 PT. Peak 5xx rate 6.4% affecting ~14% of transactions.
- Immediate Impact: Increased checkout timeouts for certain merchants; support tickets rising. No data exfiltration or integrity issues detected.
- Cause (interim): Rapid KV cache growth combined with a minor runtime memory regression on redwood-7b-slim-q8 pods in us-west-2.
- Actions taken: Traffic split to warm variant, rolling restarts of affected pool, circuit-breakers for extreme clients, and request for capacity burst.
- Ask: Approve 12-GPU burst in us-west-2 (cost ~$8.5k/day) and sign-off to notify affected merchants with the attached customer message.

Customer message (proposed):
"We’re addressing a service degradation affecting transaction latency and timeouts. Our engineers are actively mitigating the issue; you may see short delays. We apologize and will provide a follow-up within two hours with a mitigation ETA."

Please reply with APPROVE / REJECT / MODIFY in thread. I will escalate to CEO and Finance if we have APPROVE.

Evelyn

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Executive brief (internal + board heads) — Storix incident summary and requested approvals

APPROVE for capacity burst.

Add: Finance and Sales should be prepared to offer a short-term goodwill credit to the largest affected merchants; recommend hold of up to 10% of monthly committed spend for immediate relief if SLA triggers are hit. I'll loop in finance after Evelyn escalates.

Also recommend we include a short FAQ for merchant operations (what to expect, retries we recommend, contact channel).

-S

From: Samir Joshi <samir.j@storix.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Ops-Team <ops@storix.com>, Laura Evans <laura.evans@kilnpay.com>
Date: 2028-04-05T13:22:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Storix throughput surge — unified command, stakeholder alert, and board notice

Team — quick customer update: thank you for rapid response. Error rate back down to 0.4% and median latency 230ms after the burst and full warm-variant routing. Merchant-reported incidents trending down.

Requests from our side:
- Post-mortem window tomorrow 09:00 PT to go over permanent fixes and a timeline for reverting to default variant.
- Request temporary SLA credit for two top merchants while we complete regression testing.

We'll standby for your board note.

Samir

source.subject: Request: Stability & Activation Bridge for Vanguard Enterprise Deal
messages:
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 09:12:00 -0400
Subject: Request: Stability & Activation Bridge for Vanguard Enterprise Deal

Team —

Quick context: Vanguard has signaled a conditional go/no-go for pilot activation at the end of Q2 (target internal kick-off 2028-05-15). Procurement needs the SOW and a mapped activation path before 2028-04-20. They flagged two gating concerns in the technical review:

1) Stability under long-tail sequence variance — they expect multi-minute sessions with mixed short/long prompts.
2) Deterministic fallback behavior and an "activation bridge" plan (how we degrade gracefully if a dedicated pool saturates).

I've attached a one-page customer MAP and the current SOW draft (SOW_draft_vanguard.docx). Request: Can Eng + Product provide a scoped stability gate and activation-bridge implementation plan we can commit to in the SOW? Specifically we need:

- Short answer on feasibility for Canary path + fallback model variant (yes/no + approx dev effort in engineer-weeks).
- Minimum telemetry hooks and trace IDs we must add to satisfy Vanguard's audit team.
- A timeboxed plan for a low-risk canary (3 checkpoints: smoke, perf, rollback).

source.subject: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list
messages:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Cara Jensen <cara@finlytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list

Hi Cara — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so your engineering team can evaluate Hosted as a viable path for FinLytics' inference workload.

Summary of the call:
- Primary use: realtime document classification + on-demand summarization for financial reports.
- Expected peak QPS: ~12 requests/s with 256-token average responses; tail bursts to 40 QPS during earnings minutes.
- SLO: p99 latency <= 400ms for short responses (<=128 tokens).
- Data needs: no persistent PII storage, but audit logs for queries required for compliance.

Action items we recommend for a Hosted evaluation:
1) Quickstart endpoints: provision two Hosted endpoints — live/primary (gptx-16k/optimized) and canary (gptx-8k/cost-optimized).
2) Rate profile & limits: we’ll pre-configure rate limits aligned to your QPS profile and enable burst capacity for 60s windows.
3) KV caching: configure prefix caching for repeated prompts (e.g., template summaries) to reduce token spend.
4) Observability: enable the 30-day traces + contribution-breakdown on the Console for the pilot.

Attachments: I’ve attached a short worksheet (finlytics_quickstart_req.xlsx) to capture traffic patterns and a ratecard for Hosted. Please fill the worksheet and send it back; we’ll provision trial endpoints and a short-lived API key.

Proposed next steps:
- You: complete the worksheet with the traffic sample and reply by EOD Wed.
- Us: provision trial endpoints + set rate caps; schedule a 30-min ops check with your infra team.

If Hosted looks good after the pilot, we can discuss Dedicated pools or a Private VPC for data residency and higher SLA. For the pilot, Hosted is the fastest path.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0187

Attachment stubs: finlytics_quickstart_req.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-hosted-ratecard-v2.pdf (pdf)
From: Cara Jensen <cara@finlytics.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Platform Team <platform@finlytics.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list

Thanks Aditya — that writeup is helpful. A few clarifying Qs from our infra lead: 

1) Can Hosted guarantee p99 latency for the 12 QPS baseline, or will that require Dedicated?
2) How do rate caps behave during burst windows — do requests queue or fail fast? We need predictable failure semantics to surface in our app.
3) For audit logs: are query contents stored in the control plane logs for the Hosted trial? We need to confirm retention and access controls.

We can get the worksheet back by tomorrow. Also looping in Platform (platform@finlytics.com) to coordinate the ops check.

— Cara
Cara Jensen
Head of Product Ops, FinLytics
cara@finlytics.com

On Mon, Apr 2, 2028 at 09:12 AM Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Cara — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so your engineering team can evaluate Hosted as a viable path for FinLytics' inference workload.
> ...
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Cara Jensen <cara@finlytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 13:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list

Cara — quick answers below, and I’ll attach an internal ops note for our pilot configuration.

1) p99 latency: For the described baseline (~12 QPS, avg 256 tokens) Hosted (gptx-16k/optimized) is expected to meet p99 <= 400ms in our benchmarks on similar workloads. We provision with continuous batching and autoscaling to hit that SLO. If you need an SLA-backed p99 we can discuss Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) or a higher-support Hosted tier.

2) Burst behavior: by default Hosted enforces rate caps — excess requests receive 429 (fail-fast). For pilot work we can enable short-duration burst windows where we allow transient oversubscription and queue for up to ~10s before returning 429s. We’ll mark this option in the trial config and you can choose fail-fast vs queued for the ops check.

3) Audit logs & retention: Trial Hosted endpoints will write request metadata to our control plane logs; request bodies (query text) are not persisted by default for Hosted trial mode. If you require full query retention and exportable audit logs, Private or Dedicated with a VPC export path would be the recommended path. Retention windows and access controls are configurable and we can surface the SOC2 controls during the ops check.

I’ve attached a short pilot config (attachment: pilot_ops_note.txt) and included a suggested endpoint matrix below: 

Endpoint matrix (pilot):

preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Cara Jensen <cara@finlytics.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list

Hi Cara — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so your engineering team can evaluate Hosted as a viable path for FinLytics' inference workload.

Summary of the call:
- Primary use: realtime document classification + on-demand summarization for financial reports.
- Expected peak QPS: ~12 requests/s with 256-token average responses; tail bursts to 40 QPS during earnings minutes.
- SLO: p99 latency <= 400ms for short responses (<=128 tokens).
- Data needs: no persistent PII storage, but audit logs for queries required for compliance.

Action items we recommend for a Hosted evaluation:
1) Quickstart endpoints: provision two Hosted endpoints — live/primary (gptx-16k/optimized) and canary (gptx-8k/cost-optimized).
2) Rate profile & limits: we’ll pre-configure rate limits aligned to your QPS profile and enable burst capacity for 60s windows.
3) KV caching: configure prefix caching for repeated prompts (e.g., template summaries) to reduce token spend.
4) Observability: enable the 30-day traces + contribution-breakdown on the Console for the pilot.

Attachments: I’ve attached a short worksheet (finlytics_quickstart_req.xlsx) to capture traffic patterns and a ratecard for Hosted. Please fill the worksheet and send it back; we’ll provision trial endpoints and a short-lived API key.

Proposed next steps:
- You: complete the worksheet with the traffic sample and reply by EOD Wed.
- Us: provision trial endpoints + set rate caps; schedule a 30-min ops check with your infra team.

If Hosted looks good after the pilot, we can discuss Dedicated pools or a Private VPC for data residency and higher SLA. For the pilot, Hosted is the fastest path.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Solutions | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0187

Attachment stubs: finlytics_quickstart_req.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-hosted-ratecard-v2.pdf (pdf)

3) For audit logs: are query contents stored in the control plane logs for the Hosted trial? We need to confirm retention and access controls.

From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>

Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Apr 2028 13:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted evaluation — inference pathways & action list

Cara — quick answers below, and I’ll attach an internal ops note for our pilot configuration.

1) p99 latency: For the described baseline (~12 QPS, avg 256 tokens) Hosted (gptx-16k/optimized) is expected to meet p99 <= 400ms in our benchmarks on similar workloads. We provision with continuous batching and autoscaling to hit that SLO. If you need an SLA-backed p99 we can discuss Dedicated (reserved GPU pools) or a higher-support Hosted tier.

2) Burst behavior: by default Hosted enforces rate caps — excess requests receive 429 (fail-fast). For pilot work we can enable short-duration burst windows where we allow transient oversubscription and queue for up to ~10s before returning 429s. We’ll mark this option in the trial config and you can choose fail-fast vs queued for the ops check.

3) Audit logs & retention: Trial Hosted endpoints will write request metadata to our control plane logs; request bodies (query text) are not persisted by default for Hosted trial mode. If you require full query retention and exportable audit logs, Private or Dedicated with a VPC export path would be the recommended path. Retention windows and access controls are configurable and we can surface the SOC2 controls during the ops check.

I’ve attached a short pilot config (attachment: pilot_ops_note.txt) and included a suggested endpoint matrix below: 

Endpoint matrix (pilot):
- redwood-finlytics-primary: gptx-16k/optimized — rate cap baseline 15 QPS, burst + queue enabled (10s), logs: metadata only
- redwood-finlytics-canary: gptx-8k/cost — rate cap baseline 5 QPS, burst disabled (fail-fast), logs: metadata only

If that looks OK I’ll provision the keys once we get the worksheet back.

Best —
Aditya

source.subject: Scale decision readout — cost vs latency for Helix AI
messages:
From: Priya Rao <pri@helix.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus@helix.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2028 09:16:00 -0800
Subject: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Per our internal review we've pulled a Q4 usage dump and a short latency summary so you can see where we spiked (attached: helix_q4_usage_report.csv). Two quick points that are top of mind for the renewal conversation:

- Token growth: total tokens rose ~3.6x QoQ after we shipped multi-turn agents; forecast shows continued growth if we keep the same routing.
- Latency-sensitive routes: ~12% of requests are user-facing actions where median latency > 300ms (we need to keep under 200–250ms to preserve UX).

We are evaluating whether to increase reserved capacity at renewal or move more critical routes to a higher-perf model with auto-fallback. Cost is a concern — our CFO wants predictable commit numbers, but product wants the low-latency path for core flows.

Can you run a quick readout and recommended options? Ideally: committed token proposals, what a 20% headroom buys us, and a latency-cost tradeoff (e.g., reserved cheaper model + small burst to high-perf vs full commit on high-perf).

We're available next week for a 30–45m session. Attaching the CSV and the short internal latency export.

Attachments: helix_q4_usage_report.csv (CSV, 78KB), latency-benchmarks-2up.pdf (PDF, 420KB)

—
Priya Rao
Head of Platform
Helix AI
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <pri@helix.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus@helix.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jan 2028 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Thanks Priya — this is great context. I took a quick pass on the CSV and latency PDF. Short answer: we can give you two practical options to present to finance and product:

Option A — Cost‑efficient reserved capacity (baseline): commit to a larger pool on the standard model + 10–15% burst credits. Expected cost savings vs full high‑perf commit: ~28% but median latency for the 12% sensitive routes will remain near current numbers.

Option B — Latency prioritized (hybrid): reserve ~70% on the high‑perf variant for key routes (routing by path) and keep the rest on the standard model with aggressive prefix/KV caching. Cost is ~45% higher than Option A but median latency drops to ~160–200ms for critical routes.

I CC'd Kimberly and Sean to help validate the latency expectations and finalize a short proposal. We can send a formal one‑pager + pricing matrix after we sync.

Are you available Wed 1/24 10am PT for a 30m readout? If that window doesn't work, propose two others and I'll lock it.

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com | (415) 555‑0192
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Jan 2028 08:42:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Kim / Sean — forwarding Priya's export and my note to them. Need quick input so we can finalize a recommended proposal for Helix before Wed. TL;DR of what I think we should validate: 

1) Latency math: do we have a credible path to ~160–200ms median on the high‑perf variant for Helix's request sizes? If not, what headroom % or different model is realistic?
2) Cost math: can you validate the estimate ranges (Option A ~ baseline commit + burst, Option B ~ +45% cost). If you prefer, run the numbers for a 20% headroom commit instead of 15/70 split.
3) Ops: if Helix wants route‑level routing by path (they're ok with complexity), how fast can we put that policy in place and what's the operational risk?

Quoted message below. Please reply inline where possible — I want to keep the thread so Priya sees the final one‑pager from me (I'll remove our internal notes).

-- Forwarded message --
From: Priya Rao <pri@helix.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2028 09:16:00 -0800
Subject: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

[original message and attachments omitted]
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jan 2028 14:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Jordan / Sean — quick bullets from product/architecture standpoint after a short test run against Helix's exported sample: 

- High‑perf variant (quantized + attention kernels tuned for short sequences) shows median token generation time dropping ~35% on their most common path; end‑to‑end median latency falls into the 150–180ms range for the sampled UIs. Good signal that Option B is attainable for those routes.
- The 20% headroom compute commit buys us cushion for burst-to-high perf without risking queueing, assuming their growth follows the provided forecast. If they want sub‑150ms median we'd need a bigger commit or more aggressive sharding (cost increases nonlinearly).
- Ops note (Sean): route‑level routing is ready for staged rollout. We can enable path routing and canary 5% of traffic in 48 hours; full rollout ~5 business days assuming no config issues.

I'll attach a short latency bench PDF (latency-benchmarks-2up.pdf) and a draft one‑pager for external consumption.

—
Kimberly Park
Sr. Solutions Architect — Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com
From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
To: Priya Rao <pri@helix.ai>
Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus@helix.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 25 Jan 2028 16:02:00 -0800
Subject: Scale recommendation & next steps (proposal attached)

Priya / Marcus — thanks again for the data and the discussion today. Attached is a concise proposal (proposal-draft-v3.docx) that captures the recommended path and the economics. Executive summary below: 

- Recommended path (Hybrid Latency‑Prioritized): reserve 70% capacity on the high‑perf variant for latency‑sensitive routes; reserve 30% on the cost‑efficient standard model for bulk background processing. Include a 20% headroom buffer across the reserved pool for Q1 bursts.
- Expected impact: median latency for critical routes ~160–180ms, edge cases <200ms with canary rollouts. Estimated ~38% blended cost delta vs current baseline (detailed in attached pricing matrix).
- Risk & fallback: route‑level routing will be canaried at 5% and rolled to 50% in 48h if metrics look good. If we see CPU/GPU saturation we can fall back to standard model for non‑critical paths automatically.

Next steps: sign the attached amendment (two options inside: 12‑month commit with 20% headroom or a trial 3‑month pilot with burst credits). I can schedule an internal legal/finance handoff and send a DocuSign link once you confirm which option you want to pursue.

Proposed 30m follow-up: Thu 1/27 9am PT — would that work for you?

Attachments: proposal-draft-v3.docx (DOCX, 95KB)

Best,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
jordan@redwood.com | (415) 555‑0192

Subject: Re: Fwd: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Jordan / Sean — quick bullets from product/architecture standpoint after a short test run against Helix's exported sample: 

- High‑perf variant (quantized + attention kernels tuned for short sequences) shows median token generation time dropping ~35% on their most common path; end‑to‑end median latency falls into the 150–180ms range for the sampled UIs. Good signal that Option B is attainable for those routes.
- The 20% headroom compute commit buys us cushion for burst-to-high perf without risking queueing, assuming their growth follows the provided forecast. If they want sub‑150ms median we'd need a bigger commit or more aggressive sharding (cost increases nonlinearly).

- Recommended path (Hybrid Latency‑Prioritized): reserve 70% capacity on the high‑perf variant for latency‑sensitive routes; reserve 30% on the cost‑efficient standard model for bulk background processing. Include a 20% headroom buffer across the reserved pool for Q1 bursts.

text:
From: Priya Rao <pri@helix.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus@helix.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jan 2028 09:16:00 -0800
Subject: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. Per our internal review we've pulled a Q4 usage dump and a short latency summary so you can see where we spiked (attached: helix_q4_usage_report.csv). Two quick points that are top of mind for the renewal conversation:

- Token growth: total tokens rose ~3.6x QoQ after we shipped multi-turn agents; forecast shows continued growth if we keep the same routing.
- Latency-sensitive routes: ~12% of requests are user-facing actions where median latency > 300ms (we need to keep under 200–250ms to preserve UX).

We are evaluating whether to increase reserved capacity at renewal or move more critical routes to a higher-perf model with auto-fallback. Cost is a concern — our CFO wants predictable commit numbers, but product wants the low-latency path for core flows.

Can you run a quick readout and recommended options? Ideally: committed token proposals, what a 20% headroom buys us, and a latency-cost tradeoff (e.g., reserved cheaper model + small burst to high-perf vs full commit on high-perf).

We're available next week for a 30–45m session. Attaching the CSV and the short internal latency export.

Attachments: helix_q4_usage_report.csv (CSV, 78KB), latency-benchmarks-2up.pdf (PDF, 420KB)

—
Priya Rao
Head of Platform
Helix AI


From: Jordan Blake <jordan@redwood.com>

Cc: Marcus Lee <marcus@helix.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jan 2028 11:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Q1 renewal: usage readout + latency concerns

Thanks Priya — this is great context. I took a quick pass on the CSV and latency PDF. Short answer: we can give you two practical options to present to finance and product:

Option A — Cost‑efficient reserved capacity (baseline): commit to a larger pool on the standard model + 10–15% burst credits. Expected cost savings vs full high‑perf commit: ~28% but median latency for the 12% sensitive routes will remain near current numbers.

Option B — Latency prioritized (hybrid): reserve ~70% on the high‑perf variant for key routes (routing by path) and keep the rest on the standard model with aggressive prefix/KV caching. Cost is ~45% higher than Option A but median latency drops to ~160–200ms for critical routes.

I CC'd Kimberly and Sean to help validate the latency expectations and finalize a short proposal. We can send a formal one‑pager + pricing matrix after we sync.

Are you available Wed 1/24 10am PT for a 30m readout? If that window doesn't work, propose two others and I'll lock it.

Best,
Jordan Blake

jordan@redwood.com | (415) 555‑0192

messages:
From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@medinovahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@medinovahealth.com
Date: Fri, 10 Dec 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Live preview request + procurement checklist

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the product overview yesterday. The execs liked the flow and we'd like to schedule a short 30–45 minute live preview next week so our SRE and procurement folks can see behavior under live load.

Quick heads up from procurement: before a live demo they require a completed vendor security questionnaire and at least a SOC2 summary / policies doc. They also sent our standard NDA back with a few clauses redlined (attached).

Can you confirm:
- Whether Redwood can complete the attached questionnaire in advance (we're ideally looking for answers within 3 business days)
- If you can share SOC2 summary and where we can point our security team for evidence

If procurement won't sign off quickly we'll likely ask to move the live preview out — happy to take an async recording in the meantime.

Best,
Elena Rossi
Director, Vendor Programs
Medinova Health

Attachments: Medinova_Vendor_Questionnaire.xlsx (stub), NDA_redline_from_Medinova.docx (stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@medinovahealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 10 Dec 2027 11:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Live preview request + procurement checklist

Elena — thanks for the clear note and the files. We can absolutely be responsive here. A few points: 

- We'll start populating the questionnaire today and aim to return a draft within 48 hours.
- I'll attach a short SOC2 summary and a runbook for data handling (see attached Redwood_PSC-SOC2-Summary.pdf). For full reports we can follow your secure transfer/process.
- Regarding the NDA redlines: I looked through them and there are three clauses that will need legal review on our side (mainly scope of liability and data deletion timelines). I can send a redlined response by EOD Monday.

Options for the demo while procurement finishes their review:
1) Hold the live preview until procurement confirms (preferred for SRE deep-dive).
2) Run a limited live preview that excludes PII-related flows and call it a technical walkthrough.
3) Send a 20–30 minute recorded walkthrough now and reconvene for live once paperwork is closed.

Which would your team prefer? If you want option 2 we can provision a restricted demo environment and lock off certain features quickly.

Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Sales Engineer
Redwood Inference
Mobile: (415) 555-0192

Attachments: Redwood_PSC-SOC2-Summary.pdf (stub)

From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@medinovahealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@medinovahealth.com
Date: Mon, 13 Dec 2027 08:34:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Live preview request + procurement checklist

Ben — appreciate the quick turnaround. Procurement reviewed your SOC2 summary and they flagged a couple of missing items on the questionnaire around data residency and retention controls. They also confirmed they cannot greenlight any live environment demo until the vendor questionnaire is fully answered and the NDA is agreed.

Given our exec calendar, if the paperwork isn't done by end of day this Thursday (12/16) we'll need to push the live preview to the week of Jan 10. If you can get the completed questionnaire to us by Wednesday we can probably keep the December window.

If it's helpful we can accept a recorded technical walkthrough this week to keep momentum — not a replacement, but a stop-gap.

Thanks,
Elena

Procurement contact: procurement@medinovahealth.com

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@medinovahealth.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Dec 2027 10:02:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Live preview request + procurement checklist

Hi Elena & Ben — looping in Sean from Supplier Risk to help with the questionnaire.

Sean, quick summary: Medinova needs clear answers on data residency, retention, and an evidence path for SOC2 control ownership. They also returned an NDA with three redlines Ben flagged — legal will coordinate but we can expedite.

I recommend we: 
- Ben/SE team completes the questionnaire draft by EOD Wed (12/15).
- Sean provides explicit language around retention and deletion to insert into the questionnaire where needed.
- Legal (on both sides) exchange redlines and aim to have an agreed PDF by Thu 12/16.

If those milestones slip, propose we record a 25-minute technical walkthrough this week and tentatively book the live preview for Jan 11–13. I'll prepare a lightweight scope doc to show what a restricted live preview would include.

Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference

Attachment: draft-live-preview-scope.md (internal stub)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@medinovahealth.com>, procurement@medinovahealth.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2027 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Live preview request + procurement checklist

Team — quick status update and proposed plan of record: 

Status:
- Questionnaire: initial draft completed and uploaded to the shared Drive (link above). Ben/Sales Eng will finalize answers to the retention/residency sections with Sean tomorrow morning.
- SOC2 summary: attached in prior email (Redwood_PSC-SOC2-Summary.pdf). Full reports available through secure channel on request.
- NDA: our legal accepts two of the three redlines and proposes alternate language on the liability clause; I'll circulate the redlined counter by EOD for your review.

Plan:
- If Medinova confirms questionnaire + NDA countersigned by Thu 12/16, we keep the live preview slot on Fri 12/17 10:00–10:45 PT.
- If not, we will send a recorded technical walkthrough by Fri 12/17 and tentatively reschedule the live preview for Tue 2028-01-11 09:00–09:45 PT.

Next steps for Medinova: review the questionnaire draft in Drive and confirm whether you can accept a recorded walkthrough this week if paperwork isn't done.

Thanks — Ben

Attachments: Medinova_Questionnaire_Draft_Fill.xlsx (stub), Ben_NDA_counter_redline.docx (stub)

- If not, we will send a recorded technical walkthrough by Fri 12/17 and tentatively reschedule the live preview for Tue 2028-01-11 09:00–09:45 PT.

Next steps for Medinova: review the questionnaire draft in Drive and confirm whether you can accept a recorded walkthrough this week if paperwork isn't done.

Thanks — Ben

messages:
From: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@heliomed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@heliomed.com
Date: Tue, 30 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Renewal + vendor reboarding requirement

Hi Ben —

Quick one: procurement at HelioMed is requiring all vendors up for renewal in Q4 to go through a reboarding refresh in our ProcurePath portal. That includes an updated SOW (to reflect the +20% capacity bump we discussed), SOC 2 evidence upload, and a short supplier questionnaire.

I've attached the Procurement Questionnaire your team can fill out (HelioMed_Procurement_Questionnaire.xlsx). Our timeline is tight: procurement wants the portal completed and PO routing confirmed by Dec 7 so Finance can issue the PO before year-end.

Can you confirm who our procurement team should expect documents from (contact + email), whether you can provide a revised SOW with the term extension and the capacity increase, and whether Redwood has a preferred PO template or needs any special routing (e.g., remit to, GL codes)?

If easier, happy to jump on a 20–30 min call early this week.

Thanks,
Liam O'Sullivan
Head of Procurement
HelioMed
procurement@heliomed.com

Attachment: HelioMed_Procurement_Questionnaire.xlsx
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@heliomed.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Nov 2027 10:01:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + vendor reboarding requirement

Hi Liam —

Thanks for the heads up and the questionnaire. We can definitely support the reboarding path and the capacity bump. High level:

- Who you'll hear from: Marissa (CS) will own the SOW update and evidence upload items.
- PO/finance routing: Stephanie (Sales Ops) will confirm Redwood's PO template and any GL/accounting details.
- Timing: we'll aim to have the draft SOW and completed questionnaire back to you by Dec 3 so you have time to validate in ProcurePath ahead of your Dec 7 target.

Would you be available for a 25-min call tomorrow (Wed) at 11:00 AM PT to run through the portal steps and confirm the remit/GL info? If not, propose alternatives and we'll adapt.

Marissa/Stephanie — looping you in to pick up the two tasks above.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.ai
T: (415) 555-0187

On Tue, Nov 30, 2027 at 09:12 AM Liam O'Sullivan <liam@heliomed.com> wrote:
> Hi Ben —
>
> Quick one: procurement at HelioMed is requiring all vendors up for renewal in Q4 to go through a reboarding refresh in our ProcurePath portal....
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@heliomed.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 01 Dec 2027 08:35:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + vendor reboarding requirement — mutual action plan

Hi Liam —

Appreciate the note. Short MAP below to make progress against your Dec 7 cutoff:

1) SOW update (Marissa): draft v2 attached. Changes: extend term 12→18 months, add 20% committed capacity, update billing cadence section to reflect quarterly billing. Deliverable: SOW_HelioMed_v2_draft.pdf — please flag any commercial edits by EOD Dec 3.

2) Evidence and questionnaire (Marissa): we'll upload our SOC 2 attestation and a short vendor statement into ProcurePath. I will take point on uploading the SOC 2 PDF and completing the HelioMed_Procurement_Questionnaire.xlsx.

3) PO template & routing (Stephanie): Stephanie, can you confirm Redwood's remit address and if we require specific GL codes? HelioMed uses a PO-first flow so we'll also need them to confirm the PO number once issued.

4) Commercial term/discounts (Sean): for the capacity bump + term extension, Sean — can you confirm if there's an automated uplift or if we need an approval for a one-time credit/discount? HelioMed sometimes requests small term credits for multi-quarter commits.

Proposed next steps: 25-min call Wed 12/1 11:00 AM PT (30 if needed) to validate the SOW and portal checklist. If that works, Ben please send a calendar invite.

Thanks — I'll start the upload once we have any requested edits to the SOW.

Marissa Cole
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai
Attachment: SOW_HelioMed_v2_draft.pdf
From: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie_nguyen@redwood.ai>
To: Liam O'Sullivan <liam@heliomed.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 05 Dec 2027 15:21:00 -08:00
Subject: HelioMed — ProcurePath complete & PO routed (PO# 12345)

Hi Liam —

Quick update from Redwood Finance/OPS:

- We completed the HelioMed ProcurePath reboarding this morning. SOC 2 attestation and the questionnaire are uploaded.
- HelioMed has created PO# 12345 and linked it to our account in ProcurePath. We received the PO PDF (attached).
- DocuSign link for the SOW amendment is below — it captures the 18-month term and the +20% capacity commitment. Please sign the amendment so we can attach it to PO# 12345 in your system.

DocuSign (amendment): https://app.hellosign.com/s/abc123-helio-amend

Attached: HelioMed_PO_12345.pdf

Ben — please confirm if Redwood needs any special remit/GL code on our invoice. Sean — any commercial credits approved for this amendment? If so, confirm amount and I will ensure the amendment reflects the agreed credit line.

Thanks —
Stephanie Nguyen
Sales Operations, Redwood Inference
stephanie_nguyen@redwood.ai
Attachment: HelioMed_PO_12345.pdf

> Appreciate the note. Short MAP below to make progress against your Dec 7 cutoff:...

source.subject: Request to shift product walkthrough so CTO can join (Dec 1/2 options)
messages:
From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@prospectco.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Samantha Nguyen <samantha.nguyen@prospectco.com>
Date: 2027-11-20T09:22:00-08:00
Subject: Can we move next week's walkthrough? CTO travel conflict

Hi Ava,

Quick note — our CTO, Priya Rao, just had her travel window change and there's a high chance she won't be back in time for the slot we scheduled next week. We agreed internally that Priya should be present for the initial demo (first 20–30 minutes) to cover orchestration and security priorities.

Could we look at moving the walkthrough to either Dec 1 morning PST or Dec 2 mid-afternoon PST? If neither works, we can ask our VP of Eng to join, but Priya would prefer to be on the call.

We're still very interested — just want the right sponsor there. Thanks for being flexible.

Best,
Daniel
Daniel Park
CTO, ProspectCo
---
ProspectCo | daniel.park@prospectco.com
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@prospectco.com>
Cc: Samantha Nguyen <samantha.nguyen@prospectco.com>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-11-20T12:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can we move next week's walkthrough? CTO travel conflict

Thanks for the heads up, Daniel — totally understand wanting Priya there.

Connor and Laura: can you two confirm availability for the windows below so we can lock something with ProspectCo? Laura, I'd like you to lead the sponsor-forward section (security + routing/fallback) for the first 25 minutes. Connor — please be ready to deep-dive on latency/batching if Priya wants throughput numbers.

ProspectCo proposed windows:
- Option A: Dec 1, 9:00–10:00 AM PST (our preference)
- Option B: Dec 2, 2:00–3:00 PM PST

If neither works for Redwood team, suggest two alternatives and we'll offer a calendar update. I've attached an updated agenda with a sponsor-led opening and a condensed demo script.

I'll hold the original slot tentatively for 24 hours; if Priya confirms she can make it, we'll keep it. Otherwise we'll propose a new time.

Thanks — Ava

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0142
Redwood Inference 
www.redwood.com
Attachment: Demo-Agenda-v2.pdf
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-11-21T08:43:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can we move next week's walkthrough? CTO travel conflict

Ava — either slot works for me, I can rearrange for Dec 1 morning. Laura, can you confirm the 9:00–10:00 AM PST window? If Priya is only available for the first 20 mins, proposal:
- 9:00–9:25 sponsor + high-level architecture (Laura leads)
- 9:25–9:45 deep-dive performance + deployment patterns (Connor)
- 9:45–10:00 Q&A + next steps (Ava)

Also flagging for Sean: if Priya ends up not being able to attend, do we want to push the demo back to week of Dec 6 or proceed with VP attendance and a short pre-read for Priya? My recommendation is proceed if VP joins — but make clear we'll schedule a follow-up CTO session for detailed security/compliance items.

Connor
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
connor.obrien@redwood.com
From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@prospectco.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Samantha Nguyen <samantha.nguyen@prospectco.com>
Date: 2027-11-29T10:15:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Can we move next week's walkthrough? CTO travel conflict

Ava — quick update: Priya's flight was rebooked and there is a 70% chance she'll be delayed until evening Dec 1. She asked we propose Dec 2, 2:00–3:00 PM PST as the safer option. Can Redwood make Dec 2, 2:00 PM PST? If not, we'll have our VP of Engineering (Samantha) attend for the sponsor portion and Priya will join follow-up.

I'll confirm by EOD on Nov 30 if Priya can swing Dec 1 morning, but plan on Dec 2 afternoon for now. Thanks for the flexibility.

—Daniel
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@prospectco.com>, Samantha Nguyen <samantha.nguyen@prospectco.com>
Cc: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-11-30T17:02:00-08:00
Subject: Confirmed: Dec 2, 2:00–3:00 PM PST — updated agenda & calendar invite

Thanks, Daniel — Dec 2 @ 2:00 PM PST works for Redwood and we've updated the plan per the sponsor-first flow. Laura will open with the 20–25 minute sponsor section; Connor will cover performance/latency; I'll close with rollout and pricing/next steps.

I've attached the updated agenda and a short sponsor talking points doc for Priya (and Samantha) to review before the call. Link to updated calendar invite: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=proj_demo_20271201

If Priya confirms she can join earlier, we'll keep the invite and add her to the panel — no other changes needed. If she can't make it, we'll run the session with Samantha and follow up with a CTO-focused deep dive separately.

Action items:
- Ava: send final collateral and pre-read (today) — DONE (attached).
- Laura: prepare sponsor slide + security bullet points (Dec 1).
- Connor: prepare perf artefacts (benchmarks, latency graphs) and quantization notes.
- Daniel/Samantha: confirm if Priya can make Dec 1 by EOD Nov 30.

Thanks everyone — looking forward to the walkthrough.

Ava Chen
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
Attachments: Demo-Agenda-v2.pdf; Sponsor-Talking-Points.docx

source.subject: SOC2 / Privacy discovery: private deployment & network isolation
messages:
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Claire Nesbitt <claire.nesbitt@medaxion.com>, Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@medaxion.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Nov 2027 15:08:00 -0800
Subject: SOC2 / Privacy discovery: private deployment & network isolation

Hi Claire / Tom —

Thanks again for the time today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we don’t lose momentum: 

1) SOC 2 / privacy posture
   - You mentioned you’re mid-SOC 2 Type II readiness with an expected report window in Q1 2028. Can you confirm whether you have an existing SOC 2 Type II report (for any prior year) or if this is the first Type II you’re expecting? We’ll need to know what evidence you can share now vs. later for procurement.

2) Deployment topology & network isolation
   - Primary ask: we need your preferred topology for private deployment (VPC peering, Transit Gateway, or fully air-gapped on-prem). If you’re open to it, please send a current network diagram or a contact who manages networking.

3) Vendor security questionnaire
   - I’ve attached a short Redwood vendor questionnaire link (and a one-page options summary) to speed evaluation. If you prefer, we can run through the questionnaire on a follow-up call.

4) Evidence and timeline
   - Requested artifacts: SOC 2 status statement, firewall/proxy architecture overview (redacted ok), and a contact for your infra/security lead.

Proposed next steps:
- Claire: share whatever SOC 2 artifacts you can (even an engagement letter or timeline).
- Tom: share a current network diagram or indicate whether you’d like a short call with our solutions architect to walk through options (VPC peering vs. dedicated private cluster vs. air-gapped deploy).
- We’ll sync internally (Sean + Monica) to prepare an evidence checklist and a trimmed questionnaire specific to private deployments.

I’ve attached a baseline options PDF that outlines tradeoffs and typical evidence requests for each topology. If you’re available, can we schedule a 30-min follow-up next week (Nov 29–30)?

Cheers,
Jordan Blake
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0114

Attachments: redwood-private-deploy-options-v1.pdf (PDF)

From: Claire Nesbitt <claire.nesbitt@medaxion.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@medaxion.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Nov 2027 18:22:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC2 / Privacy discovery: private deployment & network isolation

Jordan — thanks for the clear recap. Quick answers from our side: 

- SOC 2: We do not have a completed Type II yet. We completed a Type I in 2026 and engagement for Type II kicked off in Oct 2027; auditor expects report in mid-Feb 2028. We can share the SOC 2 Type I report and the engagement letter (redacted) today.

- Network diagram: Tom owns infra; I’ll ask him to attach an internal diagram. For now I can share a high-level VPC sketch (non-sensitive). If Redwood needs more detail, we’re open to a call with your solutions architect — but full on-prem air-gap would require board approval.

- Questionnaire: please send the vendor questionnaire link. We prefer to fill it in and return, but a live walkthrough could accelerate things.

We’re available Nov 30 (morning PT) for a 30-min call.

Thanks,
Claire Nesbitt
Head of Product & Privacy, Medaxion
claire.nesbitt@medaxion.com

Attachments: medaxion-vpc-sketch-hr.jpg (image, redacted)

From: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@medaxion.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Claire Nesbitt <claire.nesbitt@medaxion.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 08:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC2 / Privacy discovery: private deployment & network isolation

Jordan / Claire — jumping in. As Claire said, full air-gapped deploy would be a stretch for our roadmap right now. Our preferred path is:

- Stage 1: VPC-to-VPC peering in our AWS org (restricted subnets, NGFW in front) for POC.
- Stage 2: Dedicated private cluster (reserved capacity) with stricter egress controls and customer-managed keys once SOC 2 Type II is available.

I’m attaching a sanitized network sketch and a list of the teams that will need to sign off. For the questionnaire — please send the trimmed vendor questionnaire; we’ll aim to return it within 5 business days. If you want, schedule a 30-min call on Nov 30 10:00 PT and include your solutions architect.

Tom
Head of Infrastructure, Medaxion	om.alvarez@medaxion.com

Attachments: medaxion-network-sketch-sanitized.pdf (PDF), signoff-stakeholders-list.xlsx (XLSX)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Claire Nesbitt <claire.nesbitt@medaxion.com>, Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@medaxion.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Nov 2027 09:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SOC2 / Privacy discovery: private deployment & network isolation

Perfect — thanks all. Quick internal notes and asks (cc’ing our infra and security):

- Sean / Monica: can you prepare the trimmed vendor questionnaire (focused on private/VPC options + evidence checklist) and a one-page runbook for the VPC peering POC? I attached a request template we typically send to customers that lists the diagram fields we need.
- Claire / Tom: I’ll upload the questionnaire link and a short DocuSign SLA for the POC. Please share the sanitized diagram and the signoff list when you can — that will help us scope any networking call.

Proposed meeting: Nov 30 10:00 PT — I’ll send a calendar invite with an agenda: 1) confirm topology for POC, 2) walkthrough of trimmed questionnaire, 3) timeline & evidence handoff.

Thanks — talk soon.
Jordan

---

Attachments referenced: vendor-questionnaire-link.txt (plain text with pre-populated vendor RFI link)

source.subject: SOC 2 Type II report access & trust artifacts — next steps
messages:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Emma Jones <emma.jones@greenskyhealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-11-21T10:12:00-08:00
Subject: SOC 2 Type II report access & trust artifacts — next steps

Hi Emma — great to meet you on the call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps from our side related to compliance artifacts: 

1) What we can share today: attached is a short one-page summary of Redwood's current SOC 2 posture (SOC2_Summary_2027Q3.pdf) and a one‑pager on our attestation cadence.

2) Full report access: we can provide the full SOC 2 Type II report (for the period Nov 2026–May 2027) under a standard NDA / confidentiality path. If you prefer, we can also provide an interim attestation / bridge letter while the full report is routed via the Trust Portal.

3) Trust Portal access: to speed things up, we can provision an account for GreenSky's procurement group so you can download reports and artifact packages. source.subject: Re: Request for additional evaluation credits
messages:
From: Oliver Chan <oliver@datasynth.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2027 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: extra evaluation credits for POC

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks again for the demo last week. We’re close to finishing an internal proof-of-concept and I wanted to request an additional 150k eval tokens to finish a final run of benchmarks for our RedTeam. Our current grant is close to exhaustion and procurement will likely take 4–6 weeks.

Is there a way to get a one-time top-up? Happy to provide a short scope doc if that helps.

Best,
Oliver
Director of ML, DataSynth
oliver@datasynth.ai

--
DataSynth | building synthetic data at scale
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chan <oliver@datasynth.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Nov 2027 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: extra evaluation credits for POC

Oliver —

Appreciate the note and glad the demo was useful. We can do a one-time top-up for partners and qualifying pilots, but I need a couple things before I can approve:

- Short scope (what you’ll run and expected token usage)
- Target timeline and expected procurement cadence
- Any prior accounts/teams that have requested extensions in the last 90 days

Ben (CC’ed) — can you pull a usage snapshot for DS-2741 and any known prior extension history? I’ll hold here until we confirm there isn’t a pattern.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Nov 2027 08:44:00 -08:00
Subject: Fwd: usage snapshot for DS-2741 (logs attached)

Kimberly — forwarding the snapshot and a short summary. I pulled token usage across the last 120 days and flagged extension requests. Attaching usage-snapshot-DS-2741.csv and the original request thread.

Summary:
- Original trial credits granted 2027-07-02 (100k tokens).
- One manual extension (+75k) on 2027-08-29 tied to a sales checkpoint.
- Two subsequent ad-hoc top-ups (+50k each) requested by the same domain and approved by different reps (2027-09-13, 2027-10-27).
- Current activity shows three distinct API keys tied to the same billing email (accounting@datasynth.ai) with overlapping active windows.

Red flags: repeated manual top-ups within a short timeframe; overlapping API keys could indicate parallel pilots or multi-accounting. Recommend we pause further manual crediting until we confirm identity and use-case.

Ben
Rev Ops | Redwood Inference

[attachment: usage-snapshot-DS-2741.csv]
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Nov 2027 09:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: usage snapshot for DS-2741 (logs attached)

Thanks Ben — that aligns with what I expected. Neha, can you run the account/identity linkage check (emails, billing, and any browser fingerprints we have) and confirm whether these API keys belong to distinct legal entities or are likely controlled by the same org? Sean, loop in CS if this customer escalates.

I’ll respond to Oliver with a timeline (we shouldn’t promise anything until we’re sure).

Kim
From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 17 Nov 2027 11:27:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: identity linkage check: DS-2741

Quick update — ran the identity linkage with the signals we capture (billing email, IP ranges during onboarding, OAuth provider hashes). Findings:

- All three API keys map to accounts created from the same CIDR block (roughly the same /24 in us-west).
- Billing emails differ but are mailbox aliases forwarded to accounting@datasynth.ai.
- One of the accounts used a disposable address during signup (now deleted).

Conclusion: high likelihood these keys are controlled by the same organization and were used to obtain multiple top-ups. Recommend we classify as potential abuse and deny additional manual credits until DS provides legal entity confirmation and an executed pilot contract.

Neha Kapoor
Trust & Safety Analyst | Redwood Inference
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chan <oliver@datasynth.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional evaluation credits

Oliver —

Thanks for your patience while we reviewed your request. Our operations review identified multiple API keys and mailbox aliases that point to a single controlling organization. Given the repeated manual top-ups over the last 90 days, we need to treat this as a potential policy circumvention case.

Next steps we require before any additional credits:
1) Signed pilot statement of work or purchase order referencing DS-2741
2) Confirmation of the legal entity and a single billing contact (we cannot accept mailbox aliases as proof)
3) For transparency, please provide the short scope doc you mentioned (what you’ll run and the expected token consumption).

As a temporary measure we will not approve another manual top-up; we will keep your existing active credits unchanged so your current experiments continue. Once we receive the items above we can fast-track a one-time extension decision.

If you believe there is a mistake on our side, reply with the requested artifacts and we’ll re-open the review.

Regards,
Kimberly Park
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com

[attachment: request-email-oliver.eml]

- All three API keys map to accounts created from the same CIDR block (roughly the same /24 in us-west).
- Billing emails differ but are mailbox aliases forwarded to accounting@datasynth.ai.
- One of the accounts used a disposable address during signup (now deleted).

Conclusion: high likelihood these keys are controlled by the same organization and were used to obtain multiple top-ups. Recommend we classify as potential abuse and deny additional manual credits until DS provides legal entity confirmation and an executed pilot contract.

Neha Kapoor
Trust & Safety Analyst | Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Oliver Chan <oliver@datasynth.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 21 Nov 2027 14:05:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Request for additional evaluation credits

Oliver —

Thanks for your patience while we reviewed your request. Our operations review identified multiple API keys and mailbox aliases that point to a single controlling organization. Given the repeated manual top-ups over the last 90 days, we need to treat this as a potential policy circumvention case.

Next steps we require before any additional credits:
1) Signed pilot statement of work or purchase order referencing DS-2741
2) Confirmation of the legal entity and a single billing contact (we cannot accept mailbox aliases as proof)
3) For transparency, please provide the short scope doc you mentioned (what you’ll run and the expected token consumption).

As a temporary measure we will not approve another manual top-up; we will keep your existing active credits unchanged so your current experiments continue. Once we receive the items above we can fast-track a one-time extension decision.

If you believe there is a mistake on our side, reply with the requested artifacts and we’ll re-open the review.

Regards,
Kimberly Park

kimberly@redwood.com

messages:
From: Lena Park <lena.park@brightbridgehealth.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@brightbridgehealth.com
Date: Mon, 1 Nov 2027 08:22:00 -0800
Subject: SIG: delivery timeline - evidence & calendar

Hi Irene,

Thanks again for taking the SIG request. Our security team needs a consolidated runbook of artifacts and a calendar of committed delivery dates so procurement can complete the compliance gate. The external deadline for our vendor review is 2027-11-15 — we need all evidence uploaded and a short Q&A call scheduled before then.

Top-level asks:
- Confirm who at Redwood will own each artifact category (SOC2 sections, network diagrams, app flow, log retention exports, third-party attestations).
- Provide a shared calendar with concrete delivery dates (we prefer mid-week drops to allow our reviewers time).
- Weekly status note each Friday until completion.

I attached the customer-facing SIG checklist for reference (see highlighted fields). If you can send an initial assignment + dates by 2027-11-03 EOD PT, that would keep us on track.

Thanks,
Lena
Lena Park
Security & Vendor Risk
BrightBridge Health
attachments: SIG-artifact-checklist.xlsx
From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>

Cc: Lena Park <lena.park@brightbridgehealth.com>

Subject: Re: SIG: delivery timeline - evidence & calendar

Thanks Lena — I can take point for coordinating this on our side. Marissa / Sean, looping you both to split ownership.

Proposed plan (please confirm ownership and ETA by EOD 2027-11-03):
- Marissa: SOC2 controls mapping, policy docs, and evidence links (target drop 2027-11-10)
- Sean: Network diagrams, VPC flow, and log retention/export (target drop 2027-11-12)
- Irene: Packaging, upload to shared Drive, and scheduling the Q&A call (I will send calendar invites for 2027-11-09 and 2027-11-12)

I'll also create a single shared calendar entry and add the BrightBridge reviewer group so the dates are visible. Attaching the checklist we've been using internally for artifact tracking.

Action items:
- Marissa: confirm SOC2 artifact readiness and any blockers
- Sean: confirm feasibility of generating the retention export by 11/12 and whether you need any infra tickets opened
- Irene: I will do the Drive folder & invite; will send a weekly status note every Friday through 11/15.

Thanks — quick replies welcome, want to get calendar locked.

Best,
Irene
Irene Choi
Manager, Customer Security Operations
Redwood Inference
attachments: SIG-artifact-checklist.xlsx
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Lena Park <lena.park@brightbridgehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Nov 2027 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG: delivery timeline - evidence & calendar

Irene — I can own the SOC2 mapping and policy artifacts. Status:
- Most policy docs are current; I will compile evidence links and redact where needed.
- Deliverable estimate: I will upload the policy PDF bundle and control evidence links to the Drive folder by 2027-11-10 EOD.

Blocker: I need access to a log retention export to demonstrate retention policies for a sample tenant. Sean — can you confirm whether we can produce a 30/90/365-day retention export for the sample dataset? If not, say so and I will flag it to BrightBridge as a limitation.

I created a short text file with the specific log queries I need (attached).

Marissa
attachments: log-export-requests.txt

To: Lena Park <lena.park@brightbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Nov 2027 17:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: SIG: delivery timeline - evidence & calendar

Hi Lena — quick status update and confirmed calendar entries.

Summary so far:
- SOC2 artifacts (Marissa) -> Drive upload targeted 2027-11-10 (Marissa confirmed).
- Network/retention exports (Sean) -> Sean indicated work required; Sean and infra have an action to produce a 30-day retention export sample. Target: 2027-11-12.
- Redwood will host a short Q&A session for reviewers on 2027-11-09 at 10:00 AM PT (30 minutes). I sent the invite and included the BrightBridge reviewer group. A second slot for follow-up is on 2027-11-12 at 11:00 AM PT.

We created a shared Drive folder and dropped the checklist and Marissa's log request file. Public Drive link: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3cSIGArtifacts

Next steps:
- Marissa: upload policy bundle by 11/10 EOD
- Sean: confirm log export run and attach sample by 11/12 EOD
- Irene: send consolidated Friday status notes until 11/15; escalate if any deliverable misses the target so procurement can be notified.

If BrightBridge would prefer a different cadence or needs additional artifacts, let us know; otherwise we are on track for the 11/15 vendor gating.

Thanks,
Irene

Redwood Inference

source.subject: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay
messages:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

Team —

Quick ask: VerdantPay (Samir Khan / Leah Ortiz) are asking for a one-off increase to their sandbox credits and an extension of their POC window beyond our standard PLG limits. Key context:

- Current trial: 30-day sandbox + 50k complimentary tokens (PLG default).
- Ask: +60k complimentary tokens (total request 110k) and an additional 45 days on the POC (so a total ~75-day window).
- Why: they completed core integration (payments risk scoring) and ran a small production-like workload; latency and accuracy look good. They need runway to run a broader transaction mix and validate risk thresholds with their compliance team.
- Opportunity: Marissa estimates a ~$1.2M ARR TCV if they convert to a dedicated deployment and commit to a 12-month reserved plan.

I attached a usage snapshot and the latest MAP. The customer is time-sensitive — they want a yes/no by EOD Thursday so they can schedule an internal compliance review.

Ask to leadership / deal desk: can we approve a one-time concession? If yes, please call out any limits/conditions (SOW/PO required, max credits, billing hold, marketing intro, etc.).

Attachments: verdant_usage_report.csv, mutual_action_plan_v2.pdf

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

+1 from Sales leadership — recommend approval with controls.

Quick bullets:
- Strategic fit: vertical is fintech payments; they already have an engineering champion and a clear integration plan in the MAP.
- Commercial upside: projected ARR ~$1.2M; short-term ARR realization depends on successful staging run in the extended window.
- Risk: small (they signed NDA and share mocked payment traffic), but we need assurances on data handling for live tokens.

Proposed concession (sales rec):
- One-time complimentary uplift: 50k extra credits (total 100k).
- Tenor: 45 additional days.
- Conditions: MAP signed, SOW or PO signed within 30 days of uplift, require a technical checkpoint at day 30 of extension.

If Finance and Legal are comfortable, this should close the gap and push them to commit to Dedicated in Q1. I've uploaded the usage report and an updated MAP to the drive.

Attachments: verdant_usage_report.csv, mutual_action_plan_v2.pdf (drive links included)

On Tue, Oct 12, 2027 at 08:14 AM Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> Quick ask: VerdantPay (Samir Khan / Leah Ortiz) are asking for a one-off increase to their sandbox credits and an extension of their POC window beyond our standard PLG limits. Key context:
> 
> - Current trial: 30-day sandbox + 50k complimentary tokens (PLG default).
> - Ask: +60k complimentary tokens (total request 110k) and an additional 45 days on the POC (so a total ~75-day window).
> - Why: they completed core integration (payments risk scoring) and ran a small production-like workload; latency and accuracy look good. They need runway to run a broader transaction mix and validate risk thresholds with their compliance team.
> - Opportunity: Marissa estimates a ~$1.2M ARR TCV if they convert to a dedicated deployment and commit to a 12-month reserved plan.
> 
> I attached a usage snapshot and the latest MAP. The customer is time-sensitive — they want a yes/no by EOD Thursday so they can schedule an internal compliance review.
> 
> Ask to leadership / deal desk: can we approve a one-time concession? If yes, please call out any limits/conditions (SOW/PO required, max credits, billing hold, marketing intro, etc.).
> 
> Thanks,
> Alex Martinez
> Account Executive, Redwood Inference
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 13 Oct 2027 09:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

I've run the numbers on net exposure if we grant the full request. Short answer: we should limit the free credits.

Summary:
- Cost estimate to Redwood (GPU + infra + egress) for 60k extra tokens over the extended run = ~$8.5k (conservative). If they exceed the uplift materially, the marginal cost increases linearly.
- Revenue upside remains attractive (Marissa's $1.2M ARR), but we should protect Redwood on accounting and billing.

Finance recommendation:
- Approve up to 40k additional complimentary credits (bringing total to 90k).
- Approve full 45-day extension only conditionally: require an accepted SOW/PO or signed commitment for a Reserved capacity quote before the end of the extension, OR a pre-agreed billing-trigger that converts usage > 90k into a deferred invoice.
- Set a 30-day technical checkpoint and require the customer to sign the MAP milestones.

Attached: forecast_spend.xlsx (cost model). Happy to walk through the numbers if helpful.

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>, Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

Team —

Quick ask: VerdantPay (Samir Khan / Leah Ortiz) are asking for a one-off increase to their sandbox credits and an extension of their POC window beyond our standard PLG limits. Key context:

- Current trial: 30-day sandbox + 50k complimentary tokens (PLG default).
- Ask: +60k complimentary tokens (total request 110k) and an additional 45 days on the POC (so a total ~75-day window).
- Why: they completed core integration (payments risk scoring) and ran a small production-like workload; latency and accuracy look good. They need runway to run a broader transaction mix and validate risk thresholds with their compliance team.
- Opportunity: Marissa estimates a ~$1.2M ARR TCV if they convert to a dedicated deployment and commit to a 12-month reserved plan.

I attached a usage snapshot and the latest MAP. The customer is time-sensitive — they want a yes/no by EOD Thursday so they can schedule an internal compliance review.

Ask to leadership / deal desk: can we approve a one-time concession? If yes, please call out any limits/conditions (SOW/PO required, max credits, billing hold, marketing intro, etc.).

Attachments: verdant_usage_report.csv, mutual_action_plan_v2.pdf

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>

- Risk: small (they signed NDA and share mocked payment traffic), but we need assurances on data handling for live tokens.

Proposed concession (sales rec):
- One-time complimentary uplift: 50k extra credits (total 100k).
- Tenor: 45 additional days.
- Conditions: MAP signed, SOW or PO signed within 30 days of uplift, require a technical checkpoint at day 30 of extension.

If Finance and Legal are comfortable, this should close the gap and push them to commit to Dedicated in Q1. I've uploaded the usage report and an updated MAP to the drive.

Attachments: verdant_usage_report.csv, mutual_action_plan_v2.pdf (drive links included)

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 13 Oct 2027 09:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time sandbox uplift & 45-day POC tenor exception for VerdantPay

I've run the numbers on net exposure if we grant the full request. Short answer: we should limit the free credits.

Summary:
- Cost estimate to Redwood (GPU + infra + egress) for 60k extra tokens over the extended run = ~$8.5k (conservative). If they exceed the uplift materially, the marginal cost increases linearly.
- Revenue upside remains attractive (Marissa's $1.2M ARR), but we should protect Redwood on accounting and billing.

Finance recommendation:
- Approve up to 40k additional complimentary credits (bringing total to 90k).
- Approve full 45-day extension only conditionally: require an accepted SOW/PO or signed commitment for a Reserved capacity quote before the end of the extension, OR a pre-agreed billing-trigger that converts usage > 90k into a deferred invoice.
- Set a 30-day technical checkpoint and require the customer to sign the MAP milestones.

Attached: forecast_spend.xlsx (cost model). Happy to walk through the numbers if helpful.
