source.subject: Renewal demo: slot tradeoffs & ops plan
messages:
From: Alex Ramirez <alex.ramirez@acmehealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Ops <ops@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 05 Oct 2027 15:12:00 -0700
Subject: QBR / roadmap demo for renewal week of Oct 11

Hi Kimberly,

Hope you're well — we're kicking off renewal planning and want to lock a roadmap demo + QBR with the Redwood team during the week of Oct 11. Renewal is mid-November, so ideally we walk execs through roadmap highlights and then do an ops deep-dive for engineering and security.

Stakeholders: myself (product), Jorge (engineering), Priya (security), and ops. We're aiming for a single touchpoint if possible but can split exec vs ops. Target length: 60-90 minutes. Preferred windows: mornings PT (9–11) on Oct 12 or Oct 13.

Can you propose a couple options and any pre-reads you recommend? Also flag if we need to include Redwood security/ops folks.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Ramirez
Director of Product, Acme Health
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Alex Ramirez <alex.ramirez@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 06 Oct 2027 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR / roadmap demo for renewal week of Oct 11

Alex — thanks for the clear ask. We can accommodate a combined session but have a couple of tradeoffs to call out so the right stakeholders get what they need:

- Option A (single session, 75 mins): 20-min exec highlights + 45-min ops/deep dive + 10-min Q&A. Best for busy execs + a technical audience willing to jump into details.
- Option B (split): 30-min exec highlight (PMs + execs) + separate 60-min ops deep-dive (engineering/security). Better signal for security/ops concerns and reduces stakeholder fatigue.

Proposed slots (PT):
- Tue Oct 12, 09:00–10:15 (fits Option A)
- Wed Oct 13, 09:30–10:45 (fits Option A)
- Tue Oct 12, 14:00–15:00 + Wed Oct 13, 10:00–11:00 (split Option B)

I attached a short draft agenda and a mutual action plan that maps asks to owners — that'll help you decide which format makes sense. If you want Redwood security on the ops deep-dive, Soojin (cc'd) can join — otherwise we can share a compliance appendix.

If you'd like us to lock an exact time and send a calendar invite, tell us which slot and whether you prefer combined or split.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
attachments: QBR-draft-agenda.pdf, mutual-action-plan.xlsx
From: Alex Ramirez <alex.ramirez@acmehealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Ops <ops@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 07 Oct 2027 11:27:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR / roadmap demo for renewal week of Oct 11

Kimberly — thanks, this is helpful. Quick preference from our side: we'd like to keep it a single session if possible (exec + ops together) to reduce calendar complexity, but we need a longer ops portion — 75–90 minutes total.

Is it realistic to do 90 minutes on Tue Oct 12 starting 9am PT? We have Priya from security who would need to drop in for the compliance section only (around minute 40–60). If Redwood can put Soojin or another ops/sec engineer on the call for that segment, that would be ideal.

If 9am PT doesn't work, please propose the next closest morning window. We'll confirm quickly.

Thanks,
Alex

On Wed, Oct 6, 2027 at 9:03 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com> wrote:
> Alex — thanks for the clear ask. We can accommodate a combined session but have a couple of tradeoffs to call out...
From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Alex Ramirez <alex.ramirez@acmehealth.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 09 Oct 2027 11:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: QBR / roadmap demo for renewal week of Oct 11

Alex — locking in Tue Oct 12, 09:00–10:30 PT (90 minutes) for the combined QBR + ops deep-dive.

Agenda (high level):
- 0–20m: Executive roadmap & renewal signal (Kimberly + Alex)
- 20–55m: Ops deep-dive (Soojin + Acme engineering)
- 55–75m: Compliance appendix / targeted Qs (Priya joins)
- 75–90m: Action items & next steps (mutual action plan review)

I'm attaching the calendar invite (Acme_Renewal_QBR_invite.ics) and have updated the mutual-action-plan.xlsx with owners and deadlines. If anything needs shifting, ping me and Sean (ops scheduling) — we'll keep the invite flexible for one quick reschedule window.

See you Tuesday,
Marissa Cole
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
attachments: Acme_Renewal_QBR_invite.ics

source.subject: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Anita Patel <anita.patel@greentechenergy.com>, Miguel Torres <miguel.torres@greentechenergy.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-09-18T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs

Hi Anita / Miguel —

Thanks again for the productive discovery call last week. We'd like to lock in a 90-minute POC kickoff where we can:
- review success criteria and acceptance tests,
- confirm network and data pre-reqs, and
- assign owners to the mutual action plan.

Proposed windows (all PT):
- Tue Sep 21, 10:00–11:30
- Wed Sep 22, 14:00–15:30
- Thu Sep 23, 09:00–10:30

Please indicate availability and any calendar constraints (procurement or legal needs to join). I attached a short POC_Preflight_Checklist that calls out the technical items Diego will cover and the SOC2 snapshot for your procurement team.

Preliminary success criteria (high level):
1) Demo reproduces sanitize-run on a small sample dataset end-to-end,
2) Avg token latency for inference < 300ms at 1 r/s during demo traffic profile,
3) Basic access controls (SSO) validated and audit logging enabled for the session.

Diego: can you confirm the network egress IPs and any firewall rules we should include in the checklist? Marissa / Sean: I’ll need you on the call for pricing/next-step cadence.

Thanks —
Evelyn Hart
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
E: evelyn.hart@redwood.com | M: +1 (415) 555-0198

Attachments:
- POC_Preflight_Checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet, 2 sheets)
- SOC2_snapshot.pdf (pdf, summary)
From: Miguel Torres <miguel.torres@greentechenergy.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Anita Patel <anita.patel@greentechenergy.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Lena Hoffmann <lena.hoffmann@greentechenergy.com>, Greentech Energy Procurement <procurement@greentechenergy.com>
Date: 2027-09-18T12:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs

Thanks Evelyn — looking at the team calendar. Procurement needs at least 72 hours' notice for any vendor onboarding invite (they manage NDAs + payment terms). Also, Infosec (Lena) will want to run the SOC2 and the network plan by their checklist prior to any shared dataset being uploaded.

Our availability: Wed Sep 22 works best for engineers (14:00 PT ok), but procurement can only join on Thu Sep 23 after 11:00 PT. Could we do a staggered schedule: full kickoff with engineering + product on Wed (technical deep-dive) and Thu morning for procurement/security + executive intro? If that's workable, we can keep momentum.

-Miguel
Miguel Torres
Lead Platform Engineer — Greentech Energy
m.torres@greentechenergy.com
From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Miguel Torres <miguel.torres@greentechenergy.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Lena Hoffmann <lena.hoffmann@greentechenergy.com>
Date: 2027-09-18T13:08:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs

On 2027-09-18 12:03 PM, Miguel Torres wrote:
> Thanks Evelyn — looking at the team calendar. Procurement needs at least 72 hours' notice for any vendor onboarding invite...

Hi all — quick technical notes so procurement/infosec know the ask:
- Demo mode will run against a sanitized sample (we do not request full production data).
- Firewall: outbound HTTPS to the following CIDR blocks required: 35.184.12.0/24, 34.120.48.0/23 (we'll confirm final list before the session).
- Ports: 443 only.
- SSO: we support SAML and OIDC; if you want SSO during the session, please send a test IdP metadata URL 48 hours in advance.
- Keys: if you prefer not to share keys, we can run the demo in a read-only session brokered by us.

I can present the runtime knobs during the Wed technical meeting and run the prefix-caching demo. If procurement needs Thu, I propose:
- Wed Sep 22 14:00–15:30 PT — engineering + product (technical kickoff)
- Thu Sep 23 11:00–11:45 PT — procurement + infosec (contract/controls sync)

If that split works, I'll prepare two calendar invites and a 1-page success-criteria summary.

Diego Fernandez
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
Diego.Fernandez@redwood.com
From: Greentech Energy Procurement <procurement@greentechenergy.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Miguel Torres <miguel.torres@greentechenergy.com>, Lena Hoffmann <lena.hoffmann@greentechenergy.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-09-20T10:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs

Anita here (procurement cover) — apologies for the short delay. We can join Thu Sep 23 at 11:00 PT. A couple of items we need before the procurement sync:
1) Standard vendor packet (W-9, contact, MSA URL) — if you have an MSA template, please share.
2) Clarify billing cadence for the dedicated trial (monthly vs one-time POC charge).
3) SOC2: the snapshot you attached is helpful; can you confirm the date of the report and the auditor?

If you don’t have the MSA, we can run preliminary discussion and block 30 minutes to cover procurement questions.

Thanks —
Anita Patel
Vendor Management, Greentech Energy
procurement@greentechenergy.com
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Miguel Torres <miguel.torres@greentechenergy.com>, Anita Patel <anita.patel@greentechenergy.com>
Cc: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Lena Hoffmann <lena.hoffmann@greentechenergy.com>, Greentech Energy Procurement <procurement@greentechenergy.com>
Date: 2027-09-21T16:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: POC preflight: align kickoff window & procurement/security pre-reqs

Perfect — thanks for the confirmations.

Plan summary and owners (mutual action plan):
- Wed Sep 22, 14:00–15:30 PT — Technical kickoff (Diego lead; Evelyn backup) — agenda: architecture overview, sample run, performance knobs.
- Thu Sep 23, 11:00–11:45 PT — Procurement & Security sync (Evelyn lead; Marissa on pricing; Anita leading procurement questions).
- Pre-kickoff deliverables (owners & due dates):
  * Vendor packet & preliminary MSA link — Evelyn (EOD Tue Sep 21) — DocuSign link will be shared.
  * Final firewall/IP list — Diego (EOD Tue Sep 21) — included in calendar invite notes.
  * Test IdP metadata (if using SSO) — Greentech (Lena/Miguel) (48 hours before the relevant session).
  * Success criteria doc (one-pager) — Diego/Marissa (EOD Tue Sep 21).

Success criteria (finalized):
- End-to-end sanitized sample run with logs captured and a short Jupyter notebook demonstrating inference calls.
- Measured 95th percentile token latency < 350ms under demo load.
- Audit log entries created and accessible post-session.
- Pricing/next-step commitment window agreed within 10 business days of demo.

Attachments: I'll upload the final preflight checklist and the MSA link to the Drive folder and post the DocuSign link in this thread before the procurement call.

See calendar invites coming from my account shortly. If anyone needs to swap times, flag now — otherwise I'll send invites for the two sessions.

Best,
Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Email: evelyn.hart@redwood.com
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0198

---
On Wed Sep 22: Diego will present runtime demo; on Thu Sep 23: procurement/security sync.

messages:
From: Olivia Chen <olivia@praxishealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Prairie Procurement <procurement@praxishealth.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Intro + security questionnaire before demo

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks for the call last week. We'd like to move forward with a live demo, but our InfoSec lead has asked that we complete the standard vendor security questionnaire and confirm an executed NDA before scheduling any vendor-facing walkthrough. I've attached the questionnaire (praxis_security_questionnaire.xlsx) and our procurement intake checklist for your reference.

Can you confirm whether your team can complete the questionnaire within 3 business days and provide an NDA counterparty? If you need a point of contact on our side for technical evidence, that's Sean Gallagher (sean.gallagher@praxishealth.com).

Best,
Olivia
Olivia Chen
Head of Product Operations
Praxis Health

Attachment: praxis_security_questionnaire.xlsx (Excel), procurement_checklist.pdf (pdf)
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia@praxishealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + security questionnaire before demo

Hi Olivia — thanks for sending this over.

We've reviewed the questionnaire and can complete the majority of the items quickly; there are two sections that will need input from our security team (SOC 2 evidence and architecture diagram exports). Ben and Marissa are looped to coordinate the evidence. We can also return a standard Redwood NDA for countersignature — attaching our NDA and a short SLA summary for your procurement team.

Proposed approach:
- Ben will fill the technical items and assemble evidence (expected 48–72 hrs).
- Marissa will follow up with a redline-ready NDA (DocuSign link to follow).
- Once procurement confirms receipt of the signed NDA and evidence, we can lock a 60-minute demo.

Given your timeline, we expect to have assembled the evidence by EOD Thursday unless anything unexpected pops up. If that happens we may need to move the demo window.

Thanks,
Jasmine Liu
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0182

Attachments: redwood_standard_nda.pdf, redwood_sla_summary.pdf
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@praxishealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 16 Sep 2027 08:29:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + security questionnaire before demo

Hi team,

Quick note from InfoSec — a couple of items in the questionnaire are blocking for our internal process:
- We need an exported architecture diagram that shows network boundaries and where Redwood would process customer data.
- Clarification on data retention and log access (who can request logs and SLA around retention).
- Proof of SOC2 Type II or equivalent recent attestation.

If you can provide the architecture diagram and a SOC2 copy, procurement can proceed with NDA review. Otherwise we can't authorize a live demo with product access.

One more thing: a couple of our executives are traveling the week of Sept 20–24, so if we can't clear these items by Friday the 17th, it'll be better to postpone the demonstration until the week of Sept 27 when leadership is available.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Lead, Information Security
Praxis Health
From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Chen <olivia@praxishealth.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@praxishealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Sep 2027 16:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Intro + security questionnaire before demo — proposed next steps

Hi Olivia and Sean — thanks for the context. Short update from our side and a proposal to avoid wasted time next week: 

Status:
- Ben attached an architecture diagram and the SOC 2 attestation to our secure share (link below).
- Marissa has prepared the NDA and opened a DocuSign envelope; procurement link also included.
- We still need confirmation on a couple of questionnaire clarifications (data retention windows and log export request process) — Ben flagged two lines in the spreadsheet and added inline comments.

Given your exec travel window (Sept 20–24), we recommend pausing the live demo until the week of Sept 27 so leadership can attend and procurement can finalize signatures. If that works for you, here's a couple of proposed slots: 1) Tues, Sept 28 10:00–11:00 PT or 2) Wed, Sept 29 14:00–15:00 PT.

Links/Attachments referenced:
- Secure Evidence Share: https://docsend.com/view/abc123-docs
- DocuSign NDA envelope: https://docusign.net/envelope/XYZ-9876
- Questionnaire with comments: praxis_security_questionnaire.xlsx (comments added)
- Attachments included: redwood_standard_nda.pdf, redwood_sla_summary.pdf

Next steps: please confirm preferred slot (or propose alternatives) and procurement can sign the NDA once they run the DocuSign envelope. We'll mark the demo tentative until we receive confirmation that the questionnaire items are resolved.

Best,
Jasmine Liu
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

source.subject: [CLAR-3245] Dedicated GPU pool oversubscription — immediate customer escalation
messages:
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
To: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:05:00 +0000
Subject: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245)

Team —

Quick summary: between 01:30–01:58 UTC we observed sustained queue depth growth on the dedicated pool backing ClarityMetrics (pool-id: dp-6b2). Token processing latency has spiked from baseline 85ms/token to 610ms/token for seq_len>1024 requests; error rate (5xx) climbed from 0.2% to ~6.5%. The autoscaler attempted a scale but could not allocate additional reserved capacity due to a hard limit on the pool (reserved_ceiling=48 GPUs).

Key artifacts attached: pool-metrics-20270908.csv (per-node CPU/GPU/queue stats), claritymetrics-impact-log.txt (sampled user errors).

Immediate impact: ClarityMetrics is hitting their reserved throughput commit and we're seeing tail latency on inference calls. We have a Jira (INF-5987) capturing live traces.

Ask:
- Marissa / David: please notify the account (we need to set expectations)
- Alex: can you validate whether we can temporarily route low-priority routes for CLAR to shared capacity (best-effort) and enable granular per-route QoS?
- Sean / Vivek: recommend exec visibility if we need cross-tenant bursting or manual capacity reallocation.

I'll keep sampling and will post an ETA for mitigation within 30 minutes.

-Ethan
SRE, Redwood Inference

---
From: Evan Brooks <evan@claritymetrics.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: ClarityMetrics SRE <sre@claritymetrics.com>, procurement@claritymetrics.com
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:18:30 +0000
Subject: Re: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245)

Marissa / David —

We started seeing application errors at ~01:40 UTC and some customers are reporting timeouts. This is directly impacting our credit-card fraud score updates (batch window is slipping). We are on a 24/7 production SLA and the errors are causing financial risk. Please advise immediate mitigation and when we can expect normal throughput.

Attached: examples of failed inference payloads (claritymetrics-impact-log.txt).

We need clarity on:
- Is this isolated to our dedicated pool or is it a regional capacity problem?
- Will you be able to burst us onto other capacity in the next hour?
- If not, we will need temporary credits/penalty discussion while this is unresolved.

Please escalate — we will be tracking on our side as well.

—Evan
Head of Engineering, ClarityMetrics

---
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:25:10 +0000
Subject: Exec escalation: ClarityMetrics dedicated-pool saturation (CLAR-3245)

Vivek/Rishi —

Heads-up: ClarityMetrics (strategic account, annual committed spend) is experiencing a dedicated GPU pool saturation event that is causing both latency and 5xx spikes. They're outside their burst window and are seeing production impact (Head of Eng just emailed). Ethan has collected traces (INF-5987).

Preliminary mitigation options:
1) Authorize cross-tenant bursting: manually permit CLAR to use 8 GPUs from the shared burst pool for 2 hours.
2) Enable degraded-mode routing: shift non-critical routes to a cached-embedding path to reduce token volume.
3) Fast lane: increase their dedicated quota (requires operations lead + billing signoff).

I recommend we authorize option 1 immediately to stop the bleeding while engineering works option 2. We need an explicit yes/no from Vivek within 15 minutes so I can confirm to the customer and avoid contract impact.

David/Sean/Alex — please execute the temporary config if approved.

-Marissa
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

---
From: Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:37:45 +0000
Subject: RE: Exec escalation: ClarityMetrics dedicated-pool saturation (CLAR-3245)

Short tech note / action plan:

1) Isolation: confirmed saturation is localized to dp-6b2 (one placement group). We are seeing uneven KV-cache pressure and a hot-shard effect on model shards 3/7 which indicates a routing imbalance.

2) Immediate mitigations implemented (02:36 UTC):
   - Enabled per-route sampling to drop low-priority long-context calls (applied via rate-limit policy).
   - Provisioned best-effort burst: temporarily routed CLAR's non-SLA traffic to the shared burst pool (8 GPUs) — best-effort, no SLA guarantee.
   - Activated model-variant fallback for heavy seq_len requests (small quantized variant) to reduce GPU memory pressure.

3) Next steps (ETA timelines):
   - 02:55 UTC: monitor error rate and tail latency (30-min stabilization window).
   - 03:30 UTC: if error rate >1% persist, request manual reallocation of 6 GPUs from non-critical reserved pools (requires billing/ops approval).

Notes: routing the traffic to shared pool will reduce 95th percentile latency but may increase cost (we'll track token-level deltas). We need Marissa to confirm customer-facing messaging and Vivek for approval to reallocate if the burst doesn't stabilize.

-Alex
Senior Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference

---
From: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.ai>
To: Evan Brooks <evan@claritymetrics.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: ClarityMetrics SRE <sre@claritymetrics.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex_martinez@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek_kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 09 Sep 2027 18:22:00 +0000
Subject: RE: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245) — mitigation and next steps

Evan —

Thanks for the quick flag. Short update on where we landed and next steps:

What we did (timeline):
- 02:36 UTC: routing changes + shared-burst routing (8 GPUs) + model-variant fallback (Alex).
- 02:50–03:20 UTC: error rate reduced from ~6.5% to 0.8% and tail latency fell to ~150ms/token for the majority of requests.
- 04:10 UTC: initiated policies to permanently rebalance model shard routing for CLAR's pool (elastic config changes scheduled overnight).

Why it happened: a combination of an unusually bursty job pattern from CLAR's long-context analytics job and uneven placement on dp-6b2 produced a hot-shard. Our autoscaler respected the reserved ceiling which prevented an automatic expansion that would have preserved latency.

What we're doing next:
- Short term: we have a scheduled reallocation (INF-5987) to rebalance shard placement. We'll also propose a dedicated headroom buffer for CLAR (configurable via Reserved capacity options).
- Medium term: product will expose per-route headroom controls and improved shard balancing logic to eliminate hot-shard sensitivity.

Compensation/contract: Marissa is coordinating commercial outreach; we'll follow our SLA process. For now, we committed to a 2% credit on the affected billing window while we finish the postmortem.

Attachments shared: pool-metrics-20270908.csv, requested-burst-approval.pdf. HubSpot deal updated: https://redwood.hubspot.com/deal/CLAR-3245.

Evan — can you confirm whether the fallthrough incidents impacted reconciliation batch 01 (yes/no) so we can include concrete failure counts in the postmortem?

Appreciate the patience and we'll follow up with a full timeline + RCA by EOD tomorrow.

-David
Technical Account Lead, Redwood Inference

We are on a 24/7 production SLA and the errors are causing financial risk. Please advise immediate mitigation and when we can expect normal throughput.
>
> —Evan

From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.ai>

Date: Wed, 08 Sep 2027 02:05:00 +0000
Subject: ALERT: Dedicated pool oversubscription impacting ClarityMetrics (CLAR-3245)

Team —

Quick summary: between 01:30–01:58 UTC we observed sustained queue depth growth on the dedicated pool backing ClarityMetrics (pool-id: dp-6b2). Token processing latency has spiked from baseline 85ms/token to 610ms/token for seq_len>1024 requests; error rate (5xx) climbed from 0.2% to ~6.5%. The autoscaler attempted a scale but could not allocate additional reserved capacity due to a hard limit on the pool (reserved_ceiling=48 GPUs).

Key artifacts attached: pool-metrics-20270908.csv (per-node CPU/GPU/queue stats), claritymetrics-impact-log.txt (sampled user errors).

Immediate impact: ClarityMetrics is hitting their reserved throughput commit and we're seeing tail latency on inference calls. We have a Jira (INF-5987) capturing live traces.

Ask:
- Marissa / David: please notify the account (we need to set expectations)
- Alex: can you validate whether we can temporarily route low-priority routes for CLAR to shared capacity (best-effort) and enable granular per-route QoS?
- Sean / Vivek: recommend exec visibility if we need cross-tenant bursting or manual capacity reallocation.

I'll keep sampling and will post an ETA for mitigation within 30 minutes.

-Ethan
SRE, Redwood Inference

source.subject: SIG follow-up: private connectivity artifacts and IP allowlist for Medisync
source.messages:
From: Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Chopra <priya.chopra@medisync.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Hi Jordan / Priya,

Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's SIG last week. The InfoSec review flagged a few missing items for completion before the procurement packet can move forward:

- A network architecture diagram showing where PrivateLink and VPC peering sit relative to Medisync VPCs (routing, NAT gateways, and transit).
- A definitive list of egress IPs and CIDR ranges we need to allow (for monitoring callbacks and webhook IPs).
- Clarification on whether Redwood requires customer-initiated peering (we want to know which side initiates and how routes are exchanged).
- Any guidance on ephemeral port ranges and whether stateful NAT is required for outbound connections to Redwood-hosted components.
- Proof artifacts we can include with the SIG (e.g., exported VPC peering confirmations, sample PrivateLink DNS entries, and an allowlist CSV).

Can you provide these items, or point to where they are stored? We are trying to get SIG evidence packaged by next Wednesday. Also: happy to schedule a short call if it's easier.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Wu
Network Engineer, Medisync Health

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Alex — appreciate the list. Looping in Laura (Security Response Lead) and Sean (CS) who will assemble the artifacts. Quick plan:

- Laura: can you confirm the egress IP list and attach the topology diagram + allowlist CSV?
- Sean: can we offer a 2-hour staging test window for Medisync to validate connectivity?
- Alex / Priya: if you have a preferred format for the artifacts (Drive folder or zipped evidence pack), let us know.

We should be able to get a first-pass artifact pack by EOD Monday. If anything requires a security exception or longer lead time (e.g., router configs), we'll flag it immediately.

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
Phone: (415) 555-0142

Attachment: none

---

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Sep 2027 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Alex / Jordan — attaching the artifacts you asked for and clarifying a few behavior points. Attachments are uploaded to the Drive link below and included as stubs here:

Attachments (stubs):
- redwood_network_artifact_v1.png (architecture diagram, PNG)
- redwood_allowlist_ips_20270903.csv (comma-separated list of IPs and CIDRs)
- sig_evidence_matrix_v1.pdf (mapping of SIG questions to artifacts)

Key clarifications:
- PrivateLink vs VPC Peering: PrivateLink (our Private Endpoint offering) terminates at a managed interface endpoint in the customer's VPC. Traffic to Redwood services goes over the endpoint; no route table modification is required for the endpoint itself, but service-specific egress (monitoring callbacks) may come from shared egress IPs.
- VPC Peering: If you choose peering instead, your side must create the peering connection and accept it; routes are exchanged via your route tables. We will provide the expected prefixes to add to your route tables.
- Egress IPs: see redwood_allowlist_ips_20270903.csv — the file contains two groups: stable egress IPs (for webhooks/healthchecks) and regional NAT pools for outbound token validation. Use the 'stable-egress' list for allowlisting callbacks.
- Ephemeral ports: outbound connections from your infra to our services use ephemeral client ports (recommended range 1024-65535). For embedded agents that open reverse connections, allow TCP 443 inbound from our stable IPs.
- Proof artifacts: the sig_evidence_matrix maps each SIG question to the specific artifact (e.g., PrivateLink DNS screenshot, peering acceptance console screenshot). We can also export console confirmations if you need signed/dated screenshots.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleDriveLink

If you need the CSV in a specific format (e.g., prefix list JSON for Azure/AWS terraform), let me know and we'll generate it.

Laura Bennett
Security Response Lead, Redwood Inference

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
To: Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 6 Sep 2027 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Alex — quick operational note on testing and rollout:

- We can run a staging validation window on Thu, 9 Sep, 14:00–16:00 PT. During that window we will:
  - Validate DNS resolution for PrivateLink endpoints from your staging VPC.
  - Execute a set of connectivity checks (TLS handshake, webhook callback simulation, and egress verification).
- Please confirm the staging VPC CIDR and a contact to run packet captures if needed.
- HubSpot ticket for tracking: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/contact/987654321 (added test steps and success criteria).

If staging looks good we can schedule a short maintenance window for production cutover.

Thanks,
Sean Gallagher
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Priya Chopra <priya.chopra@medisync.com>
Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Hi Jordan / Priya,

Thanks again for walking us through Redwood's SIG last week. The InfoSec review flagged a few missing items for completion before the procurement packet can move forward:

- A network architecture diagram showing where PrivateLink and VPC peering sit relative to Medisync VPCs (routing, NAT gateways, and transit).
- A definitive list of egress IPs and CIDR ranges we need to allow (for monitoring callbacks and webhook IPs).
- Clarification on whether Redwood requires customer-initiated peering (we want to know which side initiates and how routes are exchanged).
- Any guidance on ephemeral port ranges and whether stateful NAT is required for outbound connections to Redwood-hosted components.
- Proof artifacts we can include with the SIG (e.g., exported VPC peering confirmations, sample PrivateLink DNS entries, and an allowlist CSV).

Can you provide these items, or point to where they are stored? We are trying to get SIG evidence packaged by next Wednesday. Also: happy to schedule a short call if it's easier.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Wu
Network Engineer, Medisync Health

---

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Date: Fri, 3 Sep 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Jordan Blake

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Alex Wu <alex.wu@medisync.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 4 Sep 2027 08:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: SIG follow-up: network artifacts for PrivateLink/peering

Alex / Jordan — attaching the artifacts you asked for and clarifying a few behavior points. Attachments are uploaded to the Drive link below and included as stubs here:

Attachments (stubs):
- redwood_network_artifact_v1.png (architecture diagram, PNG)
- redwood_allowlist_ips_20270903.csv (comma-separated list of IPs and CIDRs)
- sig_evidence_matrix_v1.pdf (mapping of SIG questions to artifacts)

Key clarifications:
- PrivateLink vs VPC Peering: PrivateLink (our Private Endpoint offering) terminates at a managed interface endpoint in the customer's VPC. Traffic to Redwood services goes over the endpoint; no route table modification is required for the endpoint itself, but service-specific egress (monitoring callbacks) may come from shared egress IPs.
- VPC Peering: If you choose peering instead, your side must create the peering connection and accept it; routes are exchanged via your route tables. We will provide the expected prefixes to add to your route tables.
- Egress IPs: see redwood_allowlist_ips_20270903.csv — the file contains two groups: stable egress IPs (for webhooks/healthchecks) and regional NAT pools for outbound token validation. Use the 'stable-egress' list for allowlisting callbacks.
- Ephemeral ports: outbound connections from your infra to our services use ephemeral client ports (recommended range 1024-65535). For embedded agents that open reverse connections, allow TCP 443 inbound from our stable IPs.
- Proof artifacts: the sig_evidence_matrix maps each SIG question to the specific artifact (e.g., PrivateLink DNS screenshot, peering acceptance console screenshot). We can also export console confirmations if you need signed/dated screenshots.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1aBcdExampleDriveLink

If you need the CSV in a specific format (e.g., prefix list JSON for Azure/AWS terraform), let me know and we'll generate it.

Laura Bennett
Security Response Lead, Redwood Inference

source.subject: DPA options and data residency guardrails for Summit Financial pilot
messages:
From: Ava Collins <ava.collins@summitfinancial.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Summit Legal <legal@summitfinancial.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: DPA and residency questions for pilot

Hi Sean,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the Summit team is excited about the latency profile you showed. Before procurement can greenlight the pilot, Legal and InfoSec asked me to get clarity on two things we care about for GLBA scope:

1) DPA / addendum variants: do you have a single master DPA plus vertical addenda (GLBA banking supplement, HIPAA BAA, FERPA), or do you ship tailored DPAs per customer? What options would you propose for a 6‑month pilot?

2) Data residency and custody: for the pilot we need to understand whether we can ensure US‑only processing, where keys live (customer KMS vs Redwood managed), and whether you support a private deployment vs dedicated pool.

I attached a short diagram of our data flow and an initial legal checklist. Can you provide a short options matrix and rough lead times this week?

Appreciate it,
Ava
Ava Collins
Sr. Product Manager, Platform Integrations
Summit Financial
(415) 555‑0199
Attachments: summit-dataflow-v1.png (image), summit-legal-checklist.docx (docx)
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Collins <ava.collins@summitfinancial.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot

Hi Ava — great to hear, and thanks for the clear checklist. Short answer up front: we support both a standardized DPA with vertical supplements and customer‑specific negotiated addenda for enterprise pilots. Attached is a matrix (DPA_region_options_v2.pdf) summarizing options and typical timelines. Highlights:

- Option A (fastest): Standard DPA + GLBA supplement (no edits) — ~2 business days to sign for pilots.
- Option B (negotiated GLBA addendum): Redwood legal negotiates limited redlines — ~2–3 weeks depending on scope.
- Option C (Private deployment / On‑prem control plane): Requires enterprise onboarding and separate contract — ~6–10 weeks.

Residency / Keys:
- Hosted (Redwood API): regional processing only (we can restrict to US regions), Redwood holds ephemeral keys; customer data encryption at rest using Redwood KMS.
- Dedicated (reserved capacity): customer data remains isolated in a dedicated GPU pool; we support customer KMS integration (Bring Your Own Key) with HSM-backed providers — moderate effort (1–3 weeks to integrate keys).
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): full customer KMS + audit control; longer lead time and SOW.

Next steps I propose: 1) confirm pilot model (hosted vs dedicated vs private), 2) pick Option A or B for legal, 3) security questionnaire exchange. If useful I can loop in Marcus for KMS details and Vanessa for SLA/procurement.

I attached the DPA options matrix and a checklist for residency (“redwood-residency-checklist.xlsx”).

Best,
Sean Gallagher
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attached: DPA_region_options_v2.pdf, redwood-residency-checklist.xlsx
From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA and residency questions for pilot — recommendations

Sean — forwarding to coordinate internally. My read: Summit wants low lift and speed to pilot, but procurement/legal will want some residency assurances. Recommended path to balance speed and control:

1) Start pilot on Hosted with Option A (Standard DPA + GLBA supplement, no redlines) to get a quick proof of value (target first week).
2) In parallel, open a DSAT/Security exchange and scope KMS integration for Dedicated if they want keys retained (track as parallel track; don't block pilot).
3) Offer a migration path in the DPA: 30‑day notice to switch to Dedicated/Private and a short SOW for KMS integration.

Operational asks for the pilot:
- Confirm pilot traffic expected (RPS / tokens per month) so we can size a dedicated pool if needed.
- Confirm whether any PHI will flow (GLBA + possible HE/PHI overlap). If PHI is expected we may need BAA conversation.

I'll volunteer to own procurement coordination if Sean can confirm the pilot model.

—Vanessa
Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 09:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot — procurement side

All — procurement note: if we do Option A pilot (standard DPA + GLBA supplement), Finance can approve a short pilot agreement up to $25k without a full SOW. For anything requiring Dedicated/Private or BYOK KMS we need a signed purchase order + SOW (lead time 3–6 weeks for legal review). Action items for sales:

- Sean: confirm estimated pilot ARR or monthly spend and expected seat / concurrent model so Finance can set the PO threshold.
- Sean/Vanessa: please capture any purchase order number Summit prefers on the initial paperwork.

I'll prepare a template SOW-lite for pilots that references migration to Dedicated and an optional KMS addendum. Will share it by EOD.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Director, Commercial Operations | Redwood Inference
From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot — security items

Looping in a couple security details that InfoSec will need before Dedicated/BYO key flow:

- KMS integration: which provider (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault)? We support all three for BYOK; we need the crypto boundary diagram and a test vector plan.
- Audit evidence: Summit will likely ask for SOC 2 Type II plus a short attestation for regional processing. If PHI is in scope we'll need to talk BAA and PHI handling (min retention, redact options).
- Network controls: for Dedicated we can support private peering and restrict egress; for Hosted we can provide subnet-level residency (US regions) but not customer‑side peering.

Recommend: proceed with Hosted Option A for immediate pilot; capture KMS target provider now so we can size the Dedicated migration work if they elect it.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal Security Engineer | Redwood Inference

2) Data residency and custody: for the pilot we need to understand whether we can ensure US‑only processing, where keys live (customer KMS vs Redwood managed), and whether you support a private deployment vs dedicated pool.

I attached a short diagram of our data flow and an initial legal checklist. Can you provide a short options matrix and rough lead times this week?

Appreciate it,
Ava
Ava Collins
Sr. Product Manager, Platform Integrations
Summit Financial
(415) 555‑0199
Attachments: summit-dataflow-v1.png (image), summit-legal-checklist.docx (docx)


From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Collins <ava.collins@summitfinancial.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot

Hi Ava — great to hear, and thanks for the clear checklist. Short answer up front: we support both a standardized DPA with vertical supplements and customer‑specific negotiated addenda for enterprise pilots. Attached is a matrix (DPA_region_options_v2.pdf) summarizing options and typical timelines. Highlights:

- Option A (fastest): Standard DPA + GLBA supplement (no edits) — ~2 business days to sign for pilots.
- Option B (negotiated GLBA addendum): Redwood legal negotiates limited redlines — ~2–3 weeks depending on scope.
- Option C (Private deployment / On‑prem control plane): Requires enterprise onboarding and separate contract — ~6–10 weeks.

Residency / Keys:
- Hosted (Redwood API): regional processing only (we can restrict to US regions), Redwood holds ephemeral keys; customer data encryption at rest using Redwood KMS.
- Dedicated (reserved capacity): customer data remains isolated in a dedicated GPU pool; we support customer KMS integration (Bring Your Own Key) with HSM-backed providers — moderate effort (1–3 weeks to integrate keys).
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): full customer KMS + audit control; longer lead time and SOW.

Next steps I propose: 1) confirm pilot model (hosted vs dedicated vs private), 2) pick Option A or B for legal, 3) security questionnaire exchange. If useful I can loop in Marcus for KMS details and Vanessa for SLA/procurement.

I attached the DPA options matrix and a checklist for residency (“redwood-residency-checklist.xlsx”).

Best,
Sean Gallagher
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Attached: DPA_region_options_v2.pdf, redwood-residency-checklist.xlsx


From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 30 Aug 2027 13:20:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: DPA and residency questions for pilot — recommendations

Sean — forwarding to coordinate internally. My read: Summit wants low lift and speed to pilot, but procurement/legal will want some residency assurances. Recommended path to balance speed and control:

1) Start pilot on Hosted with Option A (Standard DPA + GLBA supplement, no redlines) to get a quick proof of value (target first week).
2) In parallel, open a DSAT/Security exchange and scope KMS integration for Dedicated if they want keys retained (track as parallel track; don't block pilot).
3) Offer a migration path in the DPA: 30‑day notice to switch to Dedicated/Private and a short SOW for KMS integration.

Operational asks for the pilot:
- Confirm pilot traffic expected (RPS / tokens per month) so we can size a dedicated pool if needed.
- Confirm whether any PHI will flow (GLBA + possible HE/PHI overlap). If PHI is expected we may need BAA conversation.

I'll volunteer to own procurement coordination if Sean can confirm the pilot model.

—Vanessa
Vanessa Ortiz
Head of Customer Solutions | Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 09:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot — procurement side

All — procurement note: if we do Option A pilot (standard DPA + GLBA supplement), Finance can approve a short pilot agreement up to $25k without a full SOW. For anything requiring Dedicated/Private or BYOK KMS we need a signed purchase order + SOW (lead time 3–6 weeks for legal review). Action items for sales:

- Sean: confirm estimated pilot ARR or monthly spend and expected seat / concurrent model so Finance can set the PO threshold.
- Sean/Vanessa: please capture any purchase order number Summit prefers on the initial paperwork.

I'll prepare a template SOW-lite for pilots that references migration to Dedicated and an optional KMS addendum. Will share it by EOD.

Thanks,
Laura Bennett
Director, Commercial Operations | Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 31 Aug 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: DPA and residency questions for pilot — security items

Looping in a couple security details that InfoSec will need before Dedicated/BYO key flow:

- KMS integration: which provider (AWS KMS, GCP KMS, Azure Key Vault)? We support all three for BYOK; we need the crypto boundary diagram and a test vector plan.
- Audit evidence: Summit will likely ask for SOC 2 Type II plus a short attestation for regional processing. If PHI is in scope we'll need to talk BAA and PHI handling (min retention, redact options).
- Network controls: for Dedicated we can support private peering and restrict egress; for Hosted we can provide subnet-level residency (US regions) but not customer‑side peering.

Recommend: proceed with Hosted Option A for immediate pilot; capture KMS target provider now so we can size the Dedicated migration work if they elect it.

—Marcus
Marcus Lin
Principal Security Engineer | Redwood Inference

source.subject: URGENT: Checkout failures after p99 latency spike — SDK retry behavior?
messages:
From: Elena Ramos <elena@bluecartretail.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos@bluecartretail.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: API p99 spike is causing checkout failures (prod-logs attached)

Hi Karthik,

We're seeing a sharp increase in checkout failures starting ~08:45 PDT today. Affected flows are our mobile checkout and subscription billing endpoints that call the Redwood hosted completion API. Customers are seeing spinner/timeouts and some double charges when retries hit.

Quick summary:
- p99 latency jumped from ~600ms to 6s for a sustained 20 minutes around 08:45–09:05.
- Our clients report many 504/timeout errors and then our SDK retried aggressively, resulting in duplicate requests.
- We attached a small log slice (prod-logs-snippet.txt) showing repeated identical request IDs and retry patterns.

This is impacting our checkout conversion. We need immediate action and an executive update — our Head of Ops is asking for remediation steps and whether this is on Redwood's side. Please advise on root cause and short-term mitigations.

Thanks,
Elena Ramos
Head of Engineering | BlueCart Retail
(elena@bluecartretail.com)

Attachments: prod-logs-snippet.txt
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Ramos <elena@bluecartretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 09:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: API p99 spike is causing checkout failures (prod-logs attached)

Elena —

I'm really sorry — we know how painful checkout regressions are. Thanks for the logs. I've looped in Marissa (CS) and Sean (SRE) for triage. Initial asks so we can move fast:

1) Can you confirm whether the BlueCart SDK version is the managed release (v3.1.0) or an older pinned build?
2) Are there any client-side retry overrides turned on? (maxAttempts, no backoff)
3) If possible, please enable a short-term client-side mitigation: reduce retries to 1 and add exponential backoff -> 500ms, 1s. That will cut retry amplification while we investigate.

Short-term we can also:
- Roll to a fallback model variant for your traffic (lower CPU) if SRE recommends it.
- Temporarily increase capacity on the Dedicated pool that services your region.

Sean — can you pull the service p95/p99 charts for 08:30–09:30 and share any correlated error spikes (429/504/connection resets)? Marissa — I'll coordinate the exec note.

I'll keep this thread as the single source of truth.

— Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Head of Sales, Strategic Accounts | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai
From: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: Elena Ramos <elena@bluecartretail.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos@bluecartretail.com>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 11:05:12 -0700
Subject: Re: API p99 spike is causing checkout failures (prod-logs attached)

Karthik / team — quick triage notes (SRE):

- Dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/bluecart/p99-spike (short-term view attached).
- Service metrics: p95 is stable (~650ms). p99 shows a sharp tail to ~6–8s between 08:44–09:03, with error ratio uptick (504/connection reset) and a spike in request retries originating from a small set of client IPs.
- Correlation: We see repeated identical payloads & request IDs during the window; pattern strongly suggests client-side retry amplification rather than a sustained server saturation event.

What likely happened: an SDK change in BlueCart's fleet (reported as v3.2-beta in the header fields) changed the retry/backoff defaults to more aggressive immediate retries on timeouts. When a transient upstream connection issue occurred (short-lived backend connection resets at 08:44), the aggressive client retries multiplied requests into the hosted API and produced sporadic 504s/p99 spikes.

Immediate mitigations we can do now:
- (Action A) Add a temporary routing header to mark BlueCart traffic and route to a warmed pool to reduce cold-start risk (done — small routing rule applied at 11:00).
- (Action B) Recommend BlueCart roll a hotfix to their SDK to restore exponential backoff and maxAttempts=1–2.
- (Action C) We can expose an opt-in header X-Redwood-Client-Retry=disabled as a short-term mitigation; if BlueCart sets it their requests will bypass automatic client-side retry amplification detection that we use for batching logic (we will also treat those requests as single-attempt).

I've attached a short SRE analysis (sre-analysis.pdf) with traces and sample spans. From initial evidence, this is primarily client-triggered amplification. We will continue to monitor.

— Sean
Sean Gallagher
Principal SRE | Redwood Inference
sean@redwood.ai

Attachments: sre-analysis.pdf
From: Elena Ramos <elena@bluecartretail.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos@bluecartretail.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 22 Aug 2027 13:30:44 -0700
Subject: Re: API p99 spike is causing checkout failures (prod-logs attached)

Karthik / team — thanks for the quick triage and the temporary routing change. Our engineering lead confirmed that a canary of the v3.2-beta SDK was mistakenly promoted to 20% of traffic this morning and that change did alter retry defaults. We're pushing a hotfix now to revert to exponential backoff and lower maxAttempts.

Immediate asks from BlueCart:
- Please confirm whether Redwood will consider a partial SLA credit given the customer impact (we'll need this for our finance lead).
- Can we schedule a 30-min executive bridge today to align on timelines and mitigation? Our CEO wants an update.

We will apply the SDK hotfix within the hour and report back once live. Appreciate the help and the SRE analysis.

— Elena Ramos
Head of Engineering | BlueCart Retail

On Sun, 22 Aug 2027 at 11:05, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Karthik / team — quick triage notes (SRE):
> 
> - Dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/dashboards/bluecart/p99-spike (short-term view attached).
> - Service metrics: p95 is stable (~650ms). p99 shows a sharp tail to ~6–8s between 08:44–09:03, with error ratio uptick (504/connection reset) and a spike in request retries originating from a small set of client IPs.
> - Correlation: We see repeated identical payloads & request IDs during the window; pattern strongly suggests client-side retry amplification rather than a sustained server saturation event.
> 
> — Sean

source.subject: Post-call: Pilot handoff & launch-readiness for Solstice Health
messages:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Julia Park <julia.park@solsticehealth.com>; it-admin@solsticehealth.com
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: Pilot handoff & launch-readiness for Solstice Health

Hi Julia / Solstice team,

Thanks again for a productive conversation today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have shared alignment before we kick off the pilot: 

Top pain points you described
- High volume of short-form clinical note summarization with inconsistent templates across facilities
- Latency spikes when agents hit bursts during morning triage windows
- Strict PHI handling and audit log requirements for any vendor-hosted inference

Pilot goals (what success looks like)
1) 90%+ template consistency for generated notes across 3 clinic templates
2) Median inference latency < 250ms for single-request path under expected load profile
3) Full audit logs for all requests and automated retention to meet your compliance schedule

Proposed pilot scope (4 weeks)
- Dataset: ~10k de-identified historical notes (we'll provide the ingestion script; you provide exported files via secure bucket)
- Evaluation: prompt set of 120 representative examples (we'll run daily evals and share quality dashboard)
- Owners: Julia (Solstice) — product owner; Kimberly (Redwood) — engagement lead; Sean (Redwood) — ops/onboarding lead
- Success metrics: template F1 on labeled examples, 95th latency percentile target, and manual QA pass rate >= 85%

Access & setup asks (items required to start week 1)
- Grant a short-lived service account with read access to the S3 bucket (instructions in attachment)
- Confirm who will provide de-identification: Solstice internal ETL or Redwood script? (If you want Redwood to run it, we need a signed data-processing addendum)
- Approve the pilot SOW draft (attached) or suggest edits by EOD Friday

Timeline
- Week 0 (this week): Access & SOW signoff
- Week 1: Data ingest + baseline runs
- Weeks 2–3: Prompt tuning, evals, and latency profiling
- Week 4: Acceptance testing and handoff runbook

Attachments: pilot-access-checklist.xlsx (bucket/service-account template), redwood-onboarding-guide.pdf, solstice-sow-draft.docx

Immediate asks for this thread
- Julia: confirm you can provide the service account by Friday and who owns de-id (internal vs. Redwood)
- IT Admin: please confirm the preferred method to share the bucket (cross-account role vs. pre-signed URLs)
- Sean / Kimberly (internal): we will follow up with an ops kickoff once access is confirmed

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0128

--
Attachment(s): pilot-access-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-onboarding-guide.pdf (PDF), solstice-sow-draft.docx (draft SOW)

From: Julia Park <julia.park@solsticehealth.com>

title: Post-call: Pilot handoff & launch-readiness for Solstice Health
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Julia Park <julia.park@solsticehealth.com>; it-admin@solsticehealth.com
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Aug 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call: Pilot handoff & launch-readiness for Solstice Health

Hi Julia / Solstice team,

Thanks again for a productive conversation today. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have shared alignment before we kick off the pilot: 

Top pain points you described
- High volume of short-form clinical note summarization with inconsistent templates across facilities
- Latency spikes when agents hit bursts during morning triage windows
- Strict PHI handling and audit log requirements for any vendor-hosted inference

Pilot goals (what success looks like)
1) 90%+ template consistency for generated notes across 3 clinic templates
2) Median inference latency < 250ms for single-request path under expected load profile
3) Full audit logs for all requests and automated retention to meet your compliance schedule

Proposed pilot scope (4 weeks)
- Dataset: ~10k de-identified historical notes (we'll provide the ingestion script; you provide exported files via secure bucket)
- Evaluation: prompt set of 120 representative examples (we'll run daily evals and share quality dashboard)
- Owners: Julia (Solstice) — product owner; Kimberly (Redwood) — engagement lead; Sean (Redwood) — ops/onboarding lead
- Success metrics: template F1 on labeled examples, 95th latency percentile target, and manual QA pass rate >= 85%

Access & setup asks (items required to start week 1)
- Grant a short-lived service account with read access to the S3 bucket (instructions in attachment)
- Confirm who will provide de-identification: Solstice internal ETL or Redwood script? (If you want Redwood to run it, we need a signed data-processing addendum)
- Approve the pilot SOW draft (attached) or suggest edits by EOD Friday

Timeline
- Week 0 (this week): Access & SOW signoff
- Week 1: Data ingest + baseline runs
- Weeks 2–3: Prompt tuning, evals, and latency profiling
- Week 4: Acceptance testing and handoff runbook

Attachments: pilot-access-checklist.xlsx (bucket/service-account template), redwood-onboarding-guide.pdf, solstice-sow-draft.docx

Immediate asks for this thread
- Julia: confirm you can provide the service account by Friday and who owns de-id (internal vs. Redwood)
- IT Admin: please confirm the preferred method to share the bucket (cross-account role vs. pre-signed URLs)
- Sean / Kimberly (internal): we will follow up with an ops kickoff once access is confirmed

Thanks,

Kimberly Park
Senior Solutions Manager | Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com
+1 (415) 555-0128

--
Attachment(s): pilot-access-checklist.xlsx (spreadsheet), redwood-onboarding-guide.pdf (PDF), solstice-sow-draft.docx (draft SOW)

source.subject: Procurement onboarding & kickoff: Brightleaf - calendar consolidation
messages:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: procurement@brightleaf.com, eng@brightleaf.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-11T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Procurement onboarding & kickoff: Brightleaf - calendar consolidation

Hi team —

Thanks for the interest in Redwood. I’m Aisha (AE) and I’ll coordinate the kickoff and mutual action plan between our teams. Suggested attendees on the Redwood side: me (AE), Karthik (SE) and Monica (CS). From your side, we expect procurement + an engineering/security representative.

To get the procurement process moving, can you confirm a preferred 60-min kickoff in one of these windows (all PDT):
- Tue Jul 13, 10:00–11:00
- Wed Jul 14, 13:00–14:00
- Thu Jul 15, 09:00–10:00

If none of those work, send 2–3 alternatives and we’ll try to accommodate.

Also attaching a short Mutual Action Plan draft outlining typical onboarding milestones and evidence we’ll provide (security questionnaire, SOC2 summary, deployment runbook). If you have a procurement intake form or PO flow, please share. Our goals for the kickoff: align on timeline, identify evidence owners, and lock a demo + security mini-session.

Attachment: Brightleaf-MAP-20270711.pdf (1 page — draft)

Thanks — looking forward to getting this scheduled.

— Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aisha@redwood.com
From: procurement@brightleaf.com
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, eng@brightleaf.com
Cc: Lina Ortiz <lina.ortiz@brightleaf.com>
Date: 2027-07-11T09:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Procurement onboarding & kickoff: Brightleaf - calendar consolidation

Hi Aisha —

Thanks for the note and the MAP. Wed Jul 14 13:00–14:00 PDT works best for our procurement lead and engineering manager.

Before we lock, two asks so we can route internally: 1) SOC 2 Type II evidence (summary or letter) and 2) clarification on where customer data is hosted (region/cloud). Our security engineer Lina (cc’d) will review — she may request a 20-min security-only pre-call depending on the SOC2 document.

We’ll pencil Jul 14, please send calendar invite and the SOC2 summary if available.

— procurement@brightleaf.com
Brightleaf Procurement
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: procurement@brightleaf.com, eng@brightleaf.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-11T10:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Procurement onboarding & kickoff: Brightleaf - calendar consolidation

Thanks — Wed Jul 14 13:00–14:00 is confirmed on our side.

Per Lina’s note, attaching a short SOC2 summary (redacted). High-level answers: Redwood stores customer data in regional cloud buckets (US-West primary, EU optional for EU customers). We will include the precise hosting region for your account during the kickoff.

Attachment: SOC2-Summary-Redwood-2026.pdf (2 pages)

If Lina would like the 20-min security pre-call, Karthik can take that — Karthik, are you available Jul 13 between 11:00–11:30 or Jul 14 at 12:30 for a quick sync?

Next steps I’ll take after this message: 1) send calendar invite with attendee list and agenda, 2) upload the MAP to a shared Drive and share link, 3) confirm evidence owners internally.

Thanks —
Aisha
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-12T08:05:00-07:00
Subject: Internal: Brightleaf kickoff — evidence & who-owns-what

Internal note — before the kickoff: we should prepare the following deliverables and owners so we can respond fast to Lina/procurement requests:
- SOC2 summary + point of contact for auditor letter: Sean
- Network/data residency doc (short blurb per region): Karthik
- Demo environment access & test account for eng: Monica
- Mutual Action Plan finalization & DocuSign link: Aisha

Sean — can you confirm you can pull the auditor letter and upload to Drive by EOD Jul 12? Monica — please prepare a one-slide runbook showing demo env and what level of access we’ll hand over during POC. I’ll own the 20-min security pre-call and the network residency note.

Thanks —
Karthik
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-12T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Internal: Brightleaf kickoff — evidence & who-owns-what

Got it — I’ll prepare the demo runbook and a temporary test account. I’ve scheduled a short internal prep session for Jul 13 10:00–10:30 to rehearse the demo flow and handoff points.

Drive link (internal): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1BrightleafOnramp

I’ll upload the one-slide runbook there by EOD Jul 12.

— Monica
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com>
To: procurement@brightleaf.com, eng@brightleaf.com
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-07-12T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Procurement onboarding & kickoff: Brightleaf - calendar consolidation

Hi — calendar invite sent for Wed Jul 14, 13:00–14:00 PDT (Google Calendar). Agenda in invite: 1) introductions & timeline, 2) demo (20m), 3) security/hosting Q&A (15m), 4) procurement/next steps (15m).

DocuSign: I’ve sent the MAP for signature — you should receive a DocuSign link shortly (https://app.docusign.com/brightleaf/ds12345).

On Mon, Jul 11, 2027 at 10:22 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks — Wed Jul 14 13:00–14:00 is confirmed on our side.
> 
> Per Lina’s note, attaching a short SOC2 summary (redacted). High-level answers: Redwood stores customer data in regional cloud buckets (US-West primary, EU optional for EU customers). We will include the precise hosting region for your account during the kickoff.
> 
> Attachment: SOC2-Summary-Redwood-2026.pdf (2 pages)
> 
> If Lina would like the 20-min security pre-call, Karthik can take that — Karthik, are you available Jul 13 between 11:00–11:30 or Jul 14 at 12:30 for a quick sync?
> 
> Next steps I’ll take after this message: 1) send calendar invite with attendee list and agenda, 2) upload the MAP to a shared Drive and share link, 3) confirm evidence owners internally.
> 
> Thanks —
> Aisha

source.subject: Executive signoff requested: BrightWave pilot latency grace + commercial concession
messages:
From: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Team,

BrightWave (Samir/Elena copied on thread) just signed up for a 90-day pilot for our inference platform. During the kickoff conversation they asked for two commercial concessions to make the PoC viable for their performance-sensitive use case:

- Temporal latency tolerance: allow 99th percentile tail latency up to 600ms (our standard 99p is 300ms) for the first 90 days of the pilot only.
- Pilot commercial concession: a one-time 20% usage credit for the pilot months, or a cap of $12,000 in total credits (whichever is lower).

Customer rationale: they expect heavy bursty traffic as they run parallel experiments and want to avoid early failures while they tune prompts and caching. They will run the pilot in our us-west region and can accept instrumentation hooks (trace ids, token-level logs) so we can monitor.

Proposed mitigations from AE side: prefix caching enabled, request-level throttling for flagged test runs, and a short-term routing preference to dedicated nodes during peak windows.

Attachments: bw_draft_amendment.pdf (proposed amendment with SLO language), pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx (discount math).

Asking for:
- Commercial signoff on the 20% pilot credit capped at $12k (Finance approval needed)
- Executive waiver to temporarily allow 99p latency to 600ms for 90 days and to cap service credits at 5% of monthly invoice during that pilot (Legal to confirm non-precedent language).

Reason for exec ask: the customer is on a tight timeline (internal demo 7/15) and wants an explicit amendment they can sign. Without the concession they may pause the pilot.

My recommendation: approve both concessions with written limits: pilot-only clause, explicit auto-revert to standard SLOs after 90 days, monitoring/reporting requirements, and a non-precedent clause.

Please advise — Marissa/Jonas/Sean, can you triage? If we get a greenlight today I can send the amendment for signature and schedule the kickoff.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.ai
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0147

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Avery — thanks for the clear ask and materials.

From a commercial/sales POV I’m comfortable supporting a constrained pilot concession here:
- 20% pilot credit capped at $12k (explicit cap required)
- Temporal SLO tolerance to 600ms at 99p for exactly 90 days

Conditions I want to confirm before signoff:
1) Finance (Sean) must confirm the $12k cap and impact to ARR/recognition.
2) Legal (Jonas) to confirm the amendment language is non-precedential and includes auto-revert and monitoring/reporting obligations.
3) Limit applies to this account only and is explicitly tied to the pilot deal id BW-2027-391.

If legal & finance greenlight, I will issue a commercial approval email that Sales Ops can attach to the DocuSign packet. Samir — appreciate your patience, we will get a formal amendment over today if approvals are fast.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Head of Sales | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Quick legal read — I reviewed the proposed amendment (see amendment_redline.docx) and have two substantive asks:

1) Define "Pilot Period" precisely (start date = DocuSign completion; duration = 90 calendar days).
2) Add explicit non-precedent and no-modification-of-SLA clauses: the concession must not change standard SLOs beyond the pilot period and must not be cited by customer as a precedent for future negotiations.

Also recommend adding an instrumentation & reporting requirement: weekly performance snapshot (p95/p99, request distribution) shared with the customer and Redwood CS during the pilot. If performance metrics deviate beyond 3x expected patterns we reserve the right to pause pilot or require mitigation.

I do not object to the 5% credit cap for monthly service credits during the pilot or the one-time 20% usage credit capped at $12k — both look contractable provided the clauses above are included.

I have attached the redline with tracked language and a short definition section. If Sales wants a faster route: I can pre-approve final language and ask Legal Ops to stamp and route for signoff after Finance confirms the cap.

—Jonas
Jonas Weber
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
jonas_weber@redwood.ai

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-02T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Finance here — reviewed the numbers in pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx. Approve the commercial concession under these constraints:
- 20% one-time pilot credit approved, capped at $12,000 total.
- Monthly service credit exposure during the pilot limited to 5% of that month’s invoice (cap is per-month, not cumulative).
- Discount must be booked as a contra-revenue promotion and cannot be applied to committed reserved capacity charges.

Please confirm: the customer will not be eligible for cumulative credits beyond the 5% monthly cap during the 90-day pilot. Also confirm that the pilot is billed on usage (not reserved) so the cap can be enforced.

Once Sales confirms the billing model and Legal finalizes definitions, Finance will issue the formal approval email for Sales Ops to include in the DocuSign packet.

—Sean Gallagher
Head of Finance | Redwood Inference
sean_gallagher@redwood.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-07-02T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Final commercial approval — BrightWave pilot concessions (DocuSign next steps)

Samir / Elena — thanks for your patience. Internal approvals are complete with the following conditions (summarized):

1) Pilot period: begins on execution of the attached amendment and runs exactly 90 calendar days.
2) Latency SLO: 99p tail latency tolerance increased to 600ms for the pilot period only.
3) Credits: one-time 20% usage credit capped at $12,000; monthly service-credit exposure during pilot capped at 5% of that month’s invoice.
4) Instrumentation & reporting: weekly performance snapshots (p95/p99 and request distribution) shared with BrightWave and Redwood CS. If metrics breach agreed safety thresholds, Redwood may require mitigation or pause the pilot.
5) Non-precedent & auto-revert: concession is pilot-only and does not modify our standard SLAs for future contracts.

Next steps: Sales Ops will prepare the DocuSign packet (amendment + commercial approval email + pricing sheet). Please sign the DocuSign packet to start the pilot. DocuSign packet: https://www.docusign.net/exec/packet/0a1b2c3d

Avery — please send the signed packet back to me and coordinate the kickoff.

Appreciate the team’s quick turnaround. Samir — looking forward to getting you up and running on 7/06 once signatures are in place.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Head of Sales | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

-----Original Message-----

>

>

> 
> Asking for:

> - Executive waiver to temporarily allow 99p latency to 600ms for 90 days and to cap service credits at 5% of monthly invoice during that pilot (Legal to confirm non-precedent language).
> 
> My recommendation: approve both concessions with written limits: pilot-only clause, explicit auto-revert to standard SLOs after 90 days, monitoring/reporting requirements, and a non-precedent clause.
> 
> Please advise — Marissa/Jonas/Sean, can you triage? If we get a greenlight today I can send the amendment for signature and schedule the kickoff.
