Team,

BrightWave (Samir/Elena copied on thread) just signed up for a 90-day pilot for our inference platform. During the kickoff conversation they asked for two commercial concessions to make the PoC viable for their performance-sensitive use case:

- Temporal latency tolerance: allow 99th percentile tail latency up to 600ms (our standard 99p is 300ms) for the first 90 days of the pilot only.
- Pilot commercial concession: a one-time 20% usage credit for the pilot months, or a cap of $12,000 in total credits (whichever is lower).

Customer rationale: they expect heavy bursty traffic as they run parallel experiments and want to avoid early failures while they tune prompts and caching. They will run the pilot in our us-west region and can accept instrumentation hooks (trace ids, token-level logs) so we can monitor.

Proposed mitigations from AE side: prefix caching enabled, request-level throttling for flagged test runs, and a short-term routing preference to dedicated nodes during peak windows.

Attachments: bw_draft_amendment.pdf (proposed amendment with SLO language), pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx (discount math).

Asking for:
- Commercial signoff on the 20% pilot credit capped at $12k (Finance approval needed)
- Executive waiver to temporarily allow 99p latency to 600ms for 90 days and to cap service credits at 5% of monthly invoice during that pilot (Legal to confirm non-precedent language).

Reason for exec ask: the customer is on a tight timeline (internal demo 7/15) and wants an explicit amendment they can sign. Without the concession they may pause the pilot.

My recommendation: approve both concessions with written limits: pilot-only clause, explicit auto-revert to standard SLOs after 90 days, monitoring/reporting requirements, and a non-precedent clause.

Please advise — Marissa/Jonas/Sean, can you triage? If we get a greenlight today I can send the amendment for signature and schedule the kickoff.

Thanks,
Avery Johnson
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
avery_johnson@redwood.ai
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0147

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T11:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Avery — thanks for the clear ask and materials.

From a commercial/sales POV I’m comfortable supporting a constrained pilot concession here:
- 20% pilot credit capped at $12k (explicit cap required)
- Temporal SLO tolerance to 600ms at 99p for exactly 90 days

Conditions I want to confirm before signoff:
1) Finance (Sean) must confirm the $12k cap and impact to ARR/recognition.
2) Legal (Jonas) to confirm the amendment language is non-precedential and includes auto-revert and monitoring/reporting obligations.
3) Limit applies to this account only and is explicitly tied to the pilot deal id BW-2027-391.

If legal & finance greenlight, I will issue a commercial approval email that Sales Ops can attach to the DocuSign packet. Samir — appreciate your patience, we will get a formal amendment over today if approvals are fast.

—Marissa
Marissa Cole
Head of Sales | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

From: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-01T14:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Quick legal read — I reviewed the proposed amendment (see amendment_redline.docx) and have two substantive asks:

1) Define \"Pilot Period\" precisely (start date = DocuSign completion; duration = 90 calendar days).
2) Add explicit non-precedent and no-modification-of-SLA clauses: the concession must not change standard SLOs beyond the pilot period and must not be cited by customer as a precedent for future negotiations.

Also recommend adding an instrumentation & reporting requirement: weekly performance snapshot (p95/p99, request distribution) shared with the customer and Redwood CS during the pilot. If performance metrics deviate beyond 3x expected patterns we reserve the right to pause pilot or require mitigation.

I do not object to the 5% credit cap for monthly service credits during the pilot or the one-time 20% usage credit capped at $12k — both look contractable provided the clauses above are included.

I have attached the redline with tracked language and a short definition section. If Sales wants a faster route: I can pre-approve final language and ask Legal Ops to stamp and route for signoff after Finance confirms the cap.

—Jonas
Jonas Weber
Senior Counsel | Redwood Inference
jonas_weber@redwood.ai

From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>, Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>
Date: 2027-07-02T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: BrightWave pilot - temporary latency grace and pilot credit

Finance here — reviewed the numbers in pilot_pricing_sheet.xlsx. Approve the commercial concession under these constraints:
- 20% one-time pilot credit approved, capped at $12,000 total.
- Monthly service credit exposure during the pilot limited to 5% of that month’s invoice (cap is per-month, not cumulative).
- Discount must be booked as a contra-revenue promotion and cannot be applied to committed reserved capacity charges.

Please confirm: the customer will not be eligible for cumulative credits beyond the 5% monthly cap during the 90-day pilot. Also confirm that the pilot is billed on usage (not reserved) so the cap can be enforced.

Once Sales confirms the billing model and Legal finalizes definitions, Finance will issue the formal approval email for Sales Ops to include in the DocuSign packet.

—Sean Gallagher
Head of Finance | Redwood Inference
sean_gallagher@redwood.ai

From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir.p@brightwave.ai>, Avery Johnson <avery_johnson@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren_bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-07-02T16:40:00-07:00
Subject: Final commercial approval — BrightWave pilot concessions (DocuSign next steps)

Samir / Elena — thanks for your patience. Internal approvals are complete with the following conditions (summarized):

1) Pilot period: begins on execution of the attached amendment and runs exactly 90 calendar days.
2) Latency SLO: 99p tail latency tolerance increased to 600ms for the pilot period only.
3) Credits: one-time 20% usage credit capped at $12,000; monthly service-credit exposure during pilot capped at 5% of that month’s invoice.
4) Instrumentation & reporting: weekly performance snapshots (p95/p99 and request distribution) shared with BrightWave and Redwood CS. If metrics breach agreed safety thresholds, Redwood may require mitigation or pause the pilot.
5) Non-precedent & auto-revert: concession is pilot-only and does not modify our standard SLAs for future contracts.

Next steps: Sales Ops will prepare the DocuSign packet (amendment + commercial approval email + pricing sheet). Please sign the DocuSign packet to start the pilot. DocuSign packet: https://www.docusign.net/exec/packet/0a1b2c3d

Avery — please send the signed packet back to me and coordinate the kickoff.

Appreciate the team’s quick turnaround. Samir — looking forward to getting you up and running on 7/06 once signatures are in place.

Best,
Marissa Cole
Head of Sales | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwood.ai

source.subject: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — SLA impact and credit request
messages:
From: Amir Shah <amir@shoplane.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Cc: Megha Patel <megha.patel@shoplane.com>, ShopLane SRE <ops@shoplane.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — observed 10% error spike

Hi Hannah —

We observed an unusual packet-loss surge into your /generate endpoint yesterday between 02:38 and 02:57 UTC (our logs show retries and 502/503 responses). It caused a ~9–12% error rate on critical checkout flows for a 19-minute window which materially affected our conversion.

Our contract references 99.95% monthly availability and an incident of this magnitude crosses the threshold for an SLA credit evaluation. Can you confirm Redwood's observed downtime window, what telemetry you have (raw traces, ingress packet counters), and start a request for remediation/credit?

I've attached our HAR and a short trace sample. We need an initial confirmation today for internal billing and to decide whether to pause our rollout.

— Amir
ShopLane — Head of Platform

Attachments: shoplane-traces-0629.har (HAR), sample_request.log
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Amir Shah <amir@shoplane.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2027 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — observed 10% error spike

Hi Amir —

Thanks for the heads-up and for sending traces. I'm really sorry ShopLane saw that impact. We're treating this with high priority. Next steps from our side: 

- Sean is pulling raw ingress counters and host-level NIC stats from us-east-1 for the 02:00–03:30 UTC window.
- Laura will align with Billing on the credit path and gather the relevant SLA calculation template.
- We'll confirm the exact eligible window within 48 hours and follow up with a recommended credit amount and postmortem timeline.

Can you confirm whether the attached HAR is from a single gateway or multiple regions? That will help us isolate whether this was client-side pathing or a regional networking event.

I expect an initial confirmation by EOD PST.

— Hannah
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: requesting-evidence-checklist.pdf
From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2027 11:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — observed 10% error spike

Hannah / Laura —

Initial telemetry pull attached here (internal link). Early findings:

- Ingress packet drop metric spikes on two GPU nodes in us-east-1 at 02:40–02:58 UTC, correlating with increased retransmits on the host NIC.
- Application-level 502/503s rose in the same window; upstream (LB) logs show a short-lived connectivity flurry, not CPU/GPU saturation.
- No simultaneous control-plane operations (no deployments, no scheduled maintenance).

I'm collecting pcap slices and kmsg entries; should have them by EOD.

Provisional eligible window (based on start of packet drops to recovery): 2027-06-29T02:40:00Z — 2027-06-29T02:58:30Z. Recommend we ask Billing to compute a prorated credit for that 18.5-minute window.

— Sean
SRE Manager
From: Amir Shah <amir@shoplane.com>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
Cc: Megha Patel <megha.patel@shoplane.com>, ShopLane SRE <ops@shoplane.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2027 07:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — observed 10% error spike

Thanks Hannah/Sean. The HAR I sent includes traffic from our east-coast and EU POPs (we were A/B testing failover that morning). We've pulled additional traces and attached two short samples to this thread.

Couple of asks from our side: 
1) Please confirm the precise UTC window you will use for eligibility so we can reconcile with our billing logs.
2) Can you share the evidence bundle (pcaps/kmsg, ingress counters) you plan to use for the credit calculation? We'll include it in our procurement packet.
3) We want a commitment on the postmortem publish date — our legal/procurement needs something formal.

If Redwood confirms the window and provides the evidence by EOD tomorrow we'll pause opening a formal billing dispute. Otherwise procurement will escalate.

— Amir
From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Amir Shah <amir@shoplane.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 01 Jul 2027 16:44:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Packet-loss surge affecting /generate — observed 10% error spike

Amir —

Appreciate the additional traces and context. Final confirmation and next steps below: 

- Confirmed eligibility window (UTC): 2027-06-29T02:40:00Z — 2027-06-29T02:58:30Z. We will use this window to calculate the prorated credit.
- Evidence bundle: we're attaching the following to a secure Drive folder (link above) — pcap slices from the two affected hosts, host NIC retransmit counters, load balancer health logs, and the application error histogram.
- Credit calculation: Laura (Billing) will compute the credit using the standard SLA formula and share a draft by tomorrow EOD PST. Expect a prorated credit applied to July billing if the window is accepted.
- Postmortem publish date: initial internal RCA in 72 hours; public postmortem and remediation plan within 7 business days. We'll share interim technical notes to your SRE in the secure folder as we compile.

If ShopLane would like a formal sign-off on the credit amount before it posts, let Laura know and we'll route an approval doc via DocuSign.

I'll keep this thread as the coordination channel and update the Drive folder as artifacts arrive. Sorry again for the disruption — we'll work to prevent recurrence (networking guardrails and more aggressive host-level NIC alerting are part of the remediation plan).

— Hannah
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: shoplane-traces-0629.har, slo-uptime-snapshot-20270629.csv, requesting-evidence-checklist.pdf

source.subject: Urgent: HealthSpire 5xx/429 surge, retry amplification — immediate action & exec notification
messages:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Cole <ethan@healthspire.com>, procurement@healthspire.com
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-06-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: HealthSpire: sudden 5xx/429 spike — need immediate sync

Hi Ethan / HealthSpire team,

We just observed a sharp increase in error responses from your production traffic routed to your dedicated pool (us-west-2-dedicated-3). Key metrics in the last 18 minutes:
- Overall request failure (5xx/429) ~18% (baseline <1%)
- 5xx specifically ~12%
- p95 latency up from ~120ms to ~2.4s for text-generation routes
- Significant increase in client-side timeouts and retry attempts, producing what looks like retry amplification.

I've attached a short error log snippet and flagged this to our SRE team. Initial triage points we see: GPU node saturation in your dedicated pool, autoscaling lag on adding new nodes, and long-running requests (large sequence lengths) holding KV caches. Ops is prioritizing this but I want to get an immediate alignment on two things:
1) Can HealthSpire accept a short ~30 min failover to our hosted fallback model (lower cost but more elastic) while we stabilize the dedicated pool?

source.subject: Gate-hold & standby routing for Pinebrook onsite — VP arrival contingency
messages:
From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Hi Jordan / Marissa,

Quick update from our travel desk: VP Hernandez's connection into SFO is delayed due to an inbound mechanical. He's now showing an ETA of 11:00am instead of 9:50am and the airline has placed him on a gate hold at DEN pending maintenance clearance. They said they'd attempt to get him on the 10:40 SFO flight if available, otherwise standby.

We still plan the 2:00pm prep call at the hotel but wanted to surface the risk — he may arrive later and need a secure vehicle + expedited entrance. Can Redwood arrange standby routing or an alternate seat on a later nonstop? We can provide VIP lane access at the airport if that helps.

I've attached our VIP arrival ETA sheet (Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx) with contact numbers and the driver's details.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Program Manager, Events — Pinebrook Health
elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachment: Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)
From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com>, Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 08:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Thanks for the heads up, Elena — sorry to hear about the mechanical.

Plan from our side:
- Working with our travel partner to lock two standby options for VP Hernandez (10:40 nonstop and 13:15 connection) and a confirmed seat on the 15:05 if standby fails.
- We'll pre-authorize car/driver for an 11:30 pickup window and hold a hotel suite until we get final ETA.
- If the airline confirms gate-hold > 30 minutes, we'll request priority rebooking (we've done this for execs before).

Karthik — can you confirm standby ticketing and confirm policy for refundable last-minute buyouts? If cost exceeds threshold we'll escalate for approval.

Marissa / Sean — please stand by to coordinate on-ground pickup and security escort once Elena confirms final flight.

Jordan Blake
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com
Office: +1 (628) 555-0102

Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/company/98765
From: Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 09:07:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Appreciate the quick reaction, Jordan.

One additional ask — if VP Hernandez ends up on standby, can Redwood have a secondary room reserved at the hotel for a quiet debrief in case he misses the 2:00pm session? He may want to join remotely for the first 45 minutes before heading to the site. Also please confirm any special dietary or medical notes for his driver (he requires low-scent environment).

I can confirm driver details once airline provides final confirmation; ETA updates incoming.

Thanks,
Natalie
Senior Events Coordinator, Pinebrook Health

> 
> Quick update from our travel desk: VP Hernandez's connection into SFO is delayed due to an inbound mechanical...

To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal — standby & ticketing options (Pinebrook VP)

Jordan / Marissa — internal note with next steps (do not forward to Pinebrook unless we need to surface cost):

- Travel partner options: 1) Hold two standby seats on AA 10:40 (nonstop) and AS 13:15 (one-stop); 2) Pre-book refundable ticket on UA 15:05 as a last-resort buyout.
- Cost threshold: up to $750 internal approval; above that we need finance signoff. Refundable buyout will be ~ $1,150 estimate.
- I'll place the standbys now and will only hit the buyout if airline confirms no available seats 40 minutes prior to departure.
- I'll also push the driver's ETA and low-scent request to our ground vendor and add the driver to the VIP manifest.

Attaching boarding window guidance for team (boarding-window.pdf).

Karthik Iyer
Sr. Sales Operations Manager, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 12:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Final: Standby routing & arrival plan for VP Hernandez — Pinebrook workshop

Elena / Natalie — confirming final plan after coordination with travel: 

1) Standby attempts placed on AA 10:40 and AS 13:15; refundable buyout on UA 15:05 will be executed only if no confirmed seat is available 40 minutes prior to departure.
2) Driver reserved for 11:30–12:00 pickup window; low-scent driver assigned and noted on manifest.
3) Secondary quiet room reserved at the hotel for private briefing; VP can dial into the 2:00pm session if he's en route or prefers remote join for first 45 minutes.
4) Redwood will cover any buyout up to $750. Anything above that will go to Pinebrook for approval per our mutual action plan (Deal D-9483).

Attachments included: Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx, boarding-window.pdf
DocuSign boarding approval link: https://www.docusign.com/recipient/XYZ-123-boarding-approval

title: Gate-hold & standby routing for Pinebrook onsite — VP arrival contingency
preview:
From: Elena Soto <elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Hi Jordan / Marissa,

Quick update from our travel desk: VP Hernandez's connection into SFO is delayed due to an inbound mechanical. He's now showing an ETA of 11:00am instead of 9:50am and the airline has placed him on a gate hold at DEN pending maintenance clearance. They said they'd attempt to get him on the 10:40 SFO flight if available, otherwise standby.

We still plan the 2:00pm prep call at the hotel but wanted to surface the risk — he may arrive later and need a secure vehicle + expedited entrance. Can Redwood arrange standby routing or an alternate seat on a later nonstop? We can provide VIP lane access at the airport if that helps.

I've attached our VIP arrival ETA sheet (Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx) with contact numbers and the driver's details.

Thanks,
Elena Soto
Program Manager, Events — Pinebrook Health
elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com
Mobile: +1 (415) 555-0147

Attachment: Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Subject: Re: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Thanks for the heads up, Elena — sorry to hear about the mechanical.

Plan from our side:
- Working with our travel partner to lock two standby options for VP Hernandez (10:40 nonstop and 13:15 connection) and a confirmed seat on the 15:05 if standby fails.
- We'll pre-authorize car/driver for an 11:30 pickup window and hold a hotel suite until we get final ETA.
- If the airline confirms gate-hold > 30 minutes, we'll request priority rebooking (we've done this for execs before).

Karthik — can you confirm standby ticketing and confirm policy for refundable last-minute buyouts? If cost exceeds threshold we'll escalate for approval.

Marissa / Sean — please stand by to coordinate on-ground pickup and security escort once Elena confirms final flight.

Jordan Blake
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference
jordan.blake@redwood.com
Office: +1 (628) 555-0102

Related link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/company/98765


From: Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 09:07:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Pinebrook exec workshop — arrival update & gate hold

Appreciate the quick reaction, Jordan.

One additional ask — if VP Hernandez ends up on standby, can Redwood have a secondary room reserved at the hotel for a quiet debrief in case he misses the 2:00pm session? He may want to join remotely for the first 45 minutes before heading to the site. Also please confirm any special dietary or medical notes for his driver (he requires low-scent environment).

I can confirm driver details once airline provides final confirmation; ETA updates incoming.

Thanks,
Natalie
Senior Events Coordinator, Pinebrook Health

> Quick update from our travel desk: VP Hernandez's connection into SFO is delayed due to an inbound mechanical...


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal — standby & ticketing options (Pinebrook VP)

Jordan / Marissa — internal note with next steps (do not forward to Pinebrook unless we need to surface cost):

- Travel partner options: 1) Hold two standby seats on AA 10:40 (nonstop) and AS 13:15 (one-stop); 2) Pre-book refundable ticket on UA 15:05 as a last-resort buyout.
- Cost threshold: up to $750 internal approval; above that we need finance signoff. Refundable buyout will be ~ $1,150 estimate.
- I'll place the standbys now and will only hit the buyout if airline confirms no available seats 40 minutes prior to departure.
- I'll also push the driver's ETA and low-scent request to our ground vendor and add the driver to the VIP manifest.

Attaching boarding window guidance for team (boarding-window.pdf).

Karthik Iyer
Sr. Sales Operations Manager, Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

Attachment: boarding-window.pdf (PDF stub)


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Elena Soto <elena.soto@pinebrookhealth.com>, Natalie O'Neill <natalie.oneill@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 27 Jun 2027 12:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Final: Standby routing & arrival plan for VP Hernandez — Pinebrook workshop

Elena / Natalie — confirming final plan after coordination with travel: 

1) Standby attempts placed on AA 10:40 and AS 13:15; refundable buyout on UA 15:05 will be executed only if no confirmed seat is available 40 minutes prior to departure.
2) Driver reserved for 11:30–12:00 pickup window; low-scent driver assigned and noted on manifest.
3) Secondary quiet room reserved at the hotel for private briefing; VP can dial into the 2:00pm session if he's en route or prefers remote join for first 45 minutes.
4) Redwood will cover any buyout up to $750. Anything above that will go to Pinebrook for approval per our mutual action plan (Deal D-9483).

Attachments included: Pinebrook-VIP-ETA.xlsx, boarding-window.pdf
DocuSign boarding approval link: https://www.docusign.com/recipient/XYZ-123-boarding-approval

Please reply to confirm and we will lock final purchases.

Best,
Jordan

Onward: once Elena confirms final airline message we will notify ground crew and send the driver's mobile to your on-site contact.

source.subject: Hosted API eval - demo slot tie-break + ops backstop
messages:
From: Alex Turner <alex@quickcart.com>
To: irene@redwood.ai
Date: Fri, 25 Jun 2027 15:10:00 -0700
Subject: Hosted API eval request

Hi Irene / Redwood team,

I just completed the self-serve signup and would like to book a 30min eval to walk through the Hosted API sandbox and SSO options. We want to validate token limits, KV cache behavior for sessionized workloads, and the minimal steps to get from trial -> prod.

Availability next week: Mon/Tue afternoons PST (3pm-5pm). If you have an early-morning slot that's better, we can try to free someone up.

Attendees: me (Platform Lead) + Priya Rao (security).

Thanks,
Alex Turner
Platform Lead, QuickCart
alex@quickcart.com
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@quickcart.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 25 Jun 2027 15:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API eval request

Hi Alex — thanks for signing up and for the details. Happy to run a focused 30min session covering sandbox access, SSO checklist, token/throughput expectations, and a short demo of prefix caching/KV behavior.

I can offer two primary slots next week (PST):
- Mon 2027-06-28 3:30pm - 4:00pm PT
- Tue 2027-06-29 4:00pm - 4:30pm PT

If neither works, backup options: Wed 9:00am PT or Thu 11:00am PT.

Quick asks before we lock a time:
- Can Priya attend for a 10-min security Q&A?
- Do you want a short post-demo ops follow-up to discuss egress/retention?

Calendars: https://calendar.redwood.com/event/abcd-1234 (proposed tentative invite)
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/QC-2027-0891

If you confirm a slot I’ll send a calendar invite with a sandbox link and an SSO checklist. If I’m pulled into another meeting last-minute, Sean (cc’d) will step in as ops/backstop.

— Irene Choi
SDR, Redwood Inference
irene@redwood.ai
From: Alex Turner <alex@quickcart.com>
To: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priya Rao <priya@quickcart.com>
Date: Sat, 26 Jun 2027 08:32:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API eval request

Thanks Irene — quick update: Mon/Tue afternoons conflict with a leadership offsite that just popped up. Wed morning would work better for us. Our preference: Wed 2027-06-30 9:00am PT (we can do a 30min block).

I’ve attached our SSO requirements doc so you can see what Priya will want to review during the call. Can you also confirm whether an engineer from your side will be there for any short technical deep-dive? If not, Sean stepping in is fine as long as there’s an ops person who can speak to sandbox quotas and token limits.

Attaching: quickcart-sso-requirements.pdf

Cheers,
Alex
On Fri, Jun 25, 2027 at 3:45 PM Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Alex — thanks for signing up and for the details. Happy to run a focused 30min session...
From: Irene Choi <irene@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Turner <alex@quickcart.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>, Priya Rao <priya@quickcart.com>
Date: Sat, 26 Jun 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Hosted API eval request — confirmed Wed 9am PT

Alex — Wed 2027-06-30 9:00am PT is confirmed. I’ve sent a calendar invite and included the sandbox link + short pre-demo checklist in the invite notes. Link: https://calendar.redwood.com/event/abcd-1234

Operational notes / tie-break policy: if I’m double-booked or pulled into a product emergency, Sean (cc) will run the session and cover quotas, sandbox limits, and fallback routes (e.g., run-on-dedicated vs hosted throttling). If you want an engineer on the call, Sean can bring one or we can add a quick follow-up technical sync.

Prep for Priya: we’ll review SSO flows, token rotation, and audit log retention. I reviewed the attached quickcart-sso-requirements.pdf — looks straightforward; we’ll flag the one item that requires a security checklist for Private deployments and note it in the follow-up.

HubSpot deal: QC-2027-0891 (link: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/QC-2027-0891)
Attachments: quickcart-sso-requirements.pdf (included)

See you Wed 9am PT. If anything changes please reply and we’ll move to Thu 11:00am PT as the backstop.

Best,
Irene Choi
SDR, Redwood Inference
irene@redwood.ai
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0198

On Sat, Jun 26, 2027 at 8:32 AM Alex Turner <alex@quickcart.com> wrote:
> Thanks Irene — quick update: Mon/Tue afternoons conflict with a leadership offsite that just popped up...

source.subject: Vendor onboarding kickoff — readiness, calendar, MAP
source.body:
From: Olivia Park <olivia.park@acmehealth.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.inference.com>
Cc: procure@acmehealth.com
Date: Mon, 21 Jun 2027 13:12:00 -0400
Subject: Redwood onboarding kickoff — request and availability

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for the intro earlier. Were ready to kick off vendor setup with Redwood for the integration project (Acme Health / Redwood - POC -> production). Can we schedule a ~45 minute kickoff next week to align on onboarding steps, PO process, and the Mutual Action Plan (MAP)?

What we need from Redwood up front:
- A checklist of required onboarding docs (W-9, vendor form, security questionnaire)
- PO flow and billing terms (we usually do Net30; let us know exceptions)
- Initial MAP with milestones and owners (sample works fine)

Availability: Tue/Wed mornings EST are best for our procurement and engineering.

Thanks,
Olivia Park
Senior Procurement Manager
Acme Health
procure@acmehealth.com

source.subject: Request for uptime relief + one-off term carveout for Thermion pilot
messages:
From: Clara D'Souza <clara@thermion.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@thermion.com
Date: Sun, 13 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot commercial ask: temporary uptime relief and credit cap

Jordan —

Quick note from procurement as we finalize the pilot SOW. Our operations team flagged that for the first 90 days of the pilot we need a reduced uptime target (99.5% instead of 99.9%) with a capped service credit remedy (no greater than 10% of monthly invoice) and a one-time onboarding discount to accommodate integration work. This is non-standard but it's a gating item for our legal team.

Attached: our redlines (SLA_redline_v2.pdf) and a SOC2 summary. Can Redwood confirm whether deal desk / execs can approve a temporary concession and the discount? We can sign once that's in place.

—Clara
Clara D'Souza
Head of Procurement, Thermion
From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Clara D'Souza <clara@thermion.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 13 Jun 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot commercial ask: temporary uptime relief and credit cap

Hi Clara —

Thanks for the clear ask and for sending the redlines. We're supportive of getting Thermion on the platform quickly. Two quick clarifications before I loop in deal desk:

- Confirm the pilot term length (you noted 90 days in the email; is that exactly 90 days from go-live?)
- Is the onboarding discount requested as a percent off the first invoice or as a fixed credit?

Marissa / Sean — can you review the redline and advise whether we can offer: 99.5% uptime for 90 days, a maximum service-credit cap of 10% for that period, and a one-time onboarding credit (I believe they requested 20% of first month; clarify below)? If we need exec signoff I can route.

Jordan
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 10:30:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Pilot commercial ask: temporary uptime relief and credit cap

Team —

I reviewed Thermion's SLA redline. Summary of proposed commercial carveout:

- Uptime: 99.5% for first 90 days (pilot only)
- Service credit remedy: capped at 10% of monthly invoice for any single eligibility period (pilot window)
- Onboarding discount: one-time 20% credit applied to first invoice

Risks/notes:
- Sets a non-standard precedence for future pilots in the same vertical (industrial controls). Recommend restricting clause: "applies to this pilot only; not precedent for future deals."
- SOC2 summary looks fine; security team does not require additional attestation for pilot.
- We should tie approval to a single executive authorization (VP Sales or CRO) because the concession impacts revenue recognition and SLA exposure.

Proposed ask for exec: Approve temporary uptime target of 99.5% for 90 days, capped remedy at 10%, and a one-time 20% onboarding credit. Suggested exec language: "Approved for Thermion pilot only; subject to standard escalation if repeat requests arise."

If you agree, Jordan, I'll prepare a brief memo and route to Sean for VP signoff.

Marissa
Director, Deal Desk | Redwood Inference
(Attachment: redline summary attached internally)
From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot commercial ask: temporary uptime relief and credit cap — Executive approval

Marissa / Jordan —

I reviewed the memo and the commercial impact. I authorize the following for deal HB-2027-THERM-0193 (Thermion pilot):

- Approve 99.5% uptime target for pilot term (90 days from go-live)
- Approve a maximum service credit remedy equal to 10% of the monthly invoice for any single eligibility period during the pilot
- Approve a one-time onboarding credit of 20% of the first invoice

Conditions:
- Add explicit language limiting this concession to the Thermion pilot and requiring escalation to deal desk for any future or expanded engagement.
- Require a security post-mortem and operational runbook (shared within 30 days of go-live) to be attached to the deal record.

Jordan — please confirm on the thread back to Thermion and attach the DocuSign link once they accept.

Sean Gallagher
VP, Sales Operations | Redwood Inference
From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Clara D'Souza <clara@thermion.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>, procurement@thermion.com
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Final: Thermion pilot concession — approved and next steps

Clara —

Thanks for your patience. Internal approval is in place. Per VP signoff we've approved the pilot carveout on these limited terms:

- 90-day pilot uptime: 99.5% (pilot-only concession)
- Remedy cap: maximum service credit of 10% of monthly invoice for any one eligibility period during the pilot
- One-time onboarding credit: 20% applied to first invoice

Actions for us to close:
- I attached our redline with the approved language (SLA_redline_v2.pdf). Please confirm and return via DocuSign (link: https://www.docusign.com/envelope/view?id=0c1a2b3c4d5e6f).
- We'll attach the operational runbook and SOC2 summary to the HubSpot deal record after go-live.

If you want the concession memorialized in a short amendment rather than a full SLA swap, we can do that — let me know preference.

Best —
Jordan Blake
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
(Phone: +1 415 555 0193)

Attachments (referenced): SLA_redline_v2.pdf, Thermion_SOC2_summary.pdf

source.subject: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — triage & product trade-offs
messages:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Team —

Monitoring flagged a sustained p95 spike for NeonApps between 07:40–08:05 PDT (see attached screenshot). Error rates remained low but tail latency jumped ~3x for gen endpoints serving their traffic. Initial notes:

- Affected route: /v1/generate (model: rwd-7b-v2-q4f)
- Traffic: NeonApps steady ramp (no unusual token length)
- Top symptoms: increased server CPU on some pods, prolonged kernel scheduling waits in traces

Immediate asks:
1) Connor — can you confirm if a scheduling/driver event is visible in infra logs?
2) Marissa — product input on whether we should activate fallback routing (lower-cost model or region failover) while we triage. Trade-offs: temporary quality dip vs SLA breaches.
3) Karthik/SRE — propose a short ops play (traffic shaping/route pin) we can push with minimal rollback risk.

I've attached the screenshot and the focused service logs. If we need to message NeonApps now I can send a short heads-up; otherwise we can align on an internal plan first.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 08:27:15 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Eve — thanks, got the logs. Quick triage:

- Infra findings: we see kernel scheduling delays on two GPU hosts in us-west-2; one host shows a driver bump at 07:38 that correlates with the start of the spike. No OOMs.
- App trace: KV cache lookups fine, but per-request scheduling latency increased.

Proposed immediate actions (30–90m):
- Activate fallback routing for NeonApps to route 40% of traffic to rwd-7b-v2-q4f-quant (lower latency) — can be toggled off quickly.
- Shrink batch target from 16->8 for their routed pool to reduce jitter.
- Evacuate or cordon the two suspect hosts and move traffic to healthy capacity.

I'll start the host cordon and change the batch target; need product sign-off on the routing fallback because of quality impact. If product prefers, we can do a region-shift to us-east as an alternative — will be slower to enact.

Connor
Connor O'Brien
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:05:42 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Thanks — I prefer minimizing SLO impact for the customer. My product take:

- Approve a temporary fallback routing to quantized variant but cap at 50% initially. We should clearly note expected output differences to the customer.
- Approve the batch-size reduction. That should lower p95 quickly and avoids a full rollback.
- For longer remediation, prioritize a hotfix for the kernel/driver interaction and include a postmortem with action items.

Trade-offs I want captured in the message to NeonApps:
- Short-term quality variance for a limited percentage of requests (we'll measure deltas).
- Estimated impact window: 2–6 hours for initial mitigation while we remediate hosts.

Connor/Karthik — give me an ETA for the changes and what rollback looks like. Sean — I'd like you looped for customer comms and potential credit asks if SLA is impacted.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
VP Product, Redwood Inference
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:24:10 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — proposed ops plan + cust note draft

Planned ops steps (will start immediately,ETA in parentheses):
1) Cordon and migrate workloads off two hosts (Connor) — (15–25m)
2) Apply batch-size reduction to NeonApps route (8) — (5m rollout, can revert instantly)
3) Toggle fallback routing to quantized model for 40% of traffic — (5–10m), monitor downstream quality metrics
4) If p95 remains > target after 30m, shift an additional 30% traffic to fallback and open a driver support ticket with vendor.

Rollback: revert routing toggle and batch-size in the console (automated playbook) — expected <5m to revert.

Draft customer note (please edit):
---
Subject: Temporary mitigation for increased latency on your Redwood endpoints

Hi Alden —

We detected a tail latency increase impacting a subset of requests for NeonApps this morning and are taking immediate mitigation steps to restore SLOs. To reduce impact on your application we will temporarily route up to 40% of traffic to a lower-latency model variant and reduce batch size for your traffic while we remediate the underlying host instability.

What to expect:
- Small percentage of requests may show slight differences in output quality during the mitigation window (we will measure and share delta metrics).
- We expect initial mitigation effects within 10–30 minutes and a full operational window for remediation of 2–6 hours.

We will follow up with a technical summary and postmortem once we stabilize. Please reply if you'd like us to pause the routing change until we discuss.

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability
---

I can send this once product confirms the routing cap. Sean — pls prep a brief note on credits/SLA language in case we miss the SLO.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Platform Operations
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Alden Parker <alden.parker@neonapps.com>, ops@neonapps.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 12:10:05 -0700
Subject: Temporary mitigation for increased latency on your Redwood endpoints

Hi Alden —

We wanted to give you a brief heads-up: this morning we detected a tail-latency increase impacting a subset of requests to your /v1/generate endpoints. Our on-call SREs are actively remediating and we are taking the following mitigations now to restore response-time SLOs:

- Route up to 40% of your traffic to a lower-latency model variant (temporary).
- Reduce batch size for your routed pool to lower jitter.
- Evacuate two unstable hosts; further shifts only if needed.

Expected impact: you may see small differences in generation quality for a portion of requests while we mitigate. We expect the mitigation to begin showing improvements within 10–30 minutes and to have the situation stabilized within 2–6 hours at the latest.

If you would prefer we hold the routing change until we sync, reply to this message and we will pause until you confirm. I'll send a follow-up with metrics and a short postmortem once we have a full picture.

Apologies for the disruption — we know reliability is critical and we're on it.

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: p95-spike-screenshot.png, service-logs-06215.txt

4) If p95 remains > target after 30m, shift an additional 30% traffic to fallback and open a driver support ticket with vendor.

Rollback: revert routing toggle and batch-size in the console (automated playbook) — expected <5m to revert.

Draft customer note (please edit):
---
Subject: Temporary mitigation for increased latency on your Redwood endpoints

Hi Alden —

We detected a tail latency increase impacting a subset of requests for NeonApps this morning and are taking immediate mitigation steps to restore SLOs. To reduce impact on your application we will temporarily route up to 40% of traffic to a lower-latency model variant and reduce batch size for your traffic while we remediate the underlying host instability.

What to expect:
- Small percentage of requests may show slight differences in output quality during the mitigation window (we will measure and share delta metrics).
- We expect initial mitigation effects within 10–30 minutes and a full operational window for remediation of 2–6 hours.

We will follow up with a technical summary and postmortem once we stabilize. Please reply if you'd like us to pause the routing change until we discuss.

Thanks,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability
---

I can send this once product confirms the routing cap. Sean — pls prep a brief note on credits/SLA language in case we miss the SLO.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Director, Platform Operations


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Alden Parker <alden.parker@neonapps.com>, ops@neonapps.com
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 12:10:05 -0700
Subject: Temporary mitigation for increased latency on your Redwood endpoints

Hi Alden —

We wanted to give you a brief heads-up: this morning we detected a tail-latency increase impacting a subset of requests to your /v1/generate endpoints. Our on-call SREs are actively remediating and we are taking the following mitigations now to restore response-time SLOs:

- Route up to 40% of your traffic to a lower-latency model variant (temporary).
- Reduce batch size for your routed pool to lower jitter.
- Evacuate two unstable hosts; further shifts only if needed.

Expected impact: you may see small differences in generation quality for a portion of requests while we mitigate. We expect the mitigation to begin showing improvements within 10–30 minutes and to have the situation stabilized within 2–6 hours at the latest.

If you would prefer we hold the routing change until we sync, reply to this message and we will pause until you confirm. I'll send a follow-up with metrics and a short postmortem once we have a full picture.

Apologies for the disruption — we know reliability is critical and we're on it.

Best,
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachments: p95-spike-screenshot.png, service-logs-06215.txt
text:
From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Team —

Monitoring flagged a sustained p95 spike for NeonApps between 07:40–08:05 PDT (see attached screenshot). Error rates remained low but tail latency jumped ~3x for gen endpoints serving their traffic. Initial notes:

- Affected route: /v1/generate (model: rwd-7b-v2-q4f)
- Traffic: NeonApps steady ramp (no unusual token length)
- Top symptoms: increased server CPU on some pods, prolonged kernel scheduling waits in traces

Immediate asks:
1) Connor — can you confirm if a scheduling/driver event is visible in infra logs?
2) Marissa — product input on whether we should activate fallback routing (lower-cost model or region failover) while we triage. Trade-offs: temporary quality dip vs SLA breaches.
3) Karthik/SRE — propose a short ops play (traffic shaping/route pin) we can push with minimal rollback risk.

I've attached the screenshot and the focused service logs. If we need to message NeonApps now I can send a short heads-up; otherwise we can align on an internal plan first.

Evelyn
Evelyn Hart
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 08:27:15 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Eve — thanks, got the logs. Quick triage:

- Infra findings: we see kernel scheduling delays on two GPU hosts in us-west-2; one host shows a driver bump at 07:38 that correlates with the start of the spike. No OOMs.
- App trace: KV cache lookups fine, but per-request scheduling latency increased.

Proposed immediate actions (30–90m):
- Activate fallback routing for NeonApps to route 40% of traffic to rwd-7b-v2-q4f-quant (lower latency) — can be toggled off quickly.
- Shrink batch target from 16->8 for their routed pool to reduce jitter.
- Evacuate or cordon the two suspect hosts and move traffic to healthy capacity.

I'll start the host cordon and change the batch target; need product sign-off on the routing fallback because of quality impact. If product prefers, we can do a region-shift to us-east as an alternative — will be slower to enact.

Connor
Connor O'Brien
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 09:05:42 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — observed 30m window

Thanks — I prefer minimizing SLO impact for the customer. My product take:

- Approve a temporary fallback routing to quantized variant but cap at 50% initially. We should clearly note expected output differences to the customer.
- Approve the batch-size reduction. That should lower p95 quickly and avoids a full rollback.
- For longer remediation, prioritize a hotfix for the kernel/driver interaction and include a postmortem with action items.

Trade-offs I want captured in the message to NeonApps:
- Short-term quality variance for a limited percentage of requests (we'll measure deltas).
- Estimated impact window: 2–6 hours for initial mitigation while we remediate hosts.

Connor/Karthik — give me an ETA for the changes and what rollback looks like. Sean — I'd like you looped for customer comms and potential credit asks if SLA is impacted.

Marissa
Marissa Cole
VP Product, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwood.ai>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 15 Jun 2027 11:24:10 -0700
Subject: Re: SLO hit: sustained p95 latency spike for NeonApps — proposed ops plan + cust note draft

1) Cordon and migrate workloads off two hosts (Connor) — (15–25m)
2) Apply batch-size reduction to NeonApps route (8) — (5m rollout, can revert instantly)
3) Toggle fallback routing to quantized model for 40% of traffic — (5–10m), monitor downstream quality metrics
4) If p95 remains > target after 30m, shift an additional 30% traffic to fallback and open a driver support ticket with vendor.

source.subject: Reprofile request: close window variance and compact MAP update
messages:
From: Maria Gomez <maria@brightgrid.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Tom Ellis <t.ellis@procurebright.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Close window update — procurement gating; need MAP revision

Hi Kimberly,

Quick note from procurement + legal — they pushed our internal sign-off by two weeks because the vendor security questionnaire flagged a few items around retention and key management. That means our planned eval window (June 14–25) is now impacted and we can’t complete the procurement step until the week of June 21 at the earliest.

I want to be transparent: the customer execs are asking whether this shifts our close date. We still want Redwood — this is procedural — but need an updated MAP and a short list of mitigations so we can show our board the plan. Can you send an updated MAP / risk log and a proposed rebaseline by EOD tomorrow?

I attached BrightGrid's procurement statement for context (procurement_statement.pdf).

Thanks,
Maria
Maria Gomez
Head of Data Platforms — BrightGrid Analytics

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@brightgrid.com>
Cc: Tom Ellis <t.ellis@procurebright.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Close window update — procurement gating; need MAP revision

Hi Maria — thanks for the heads up and for the procurement statement.

Short answer: we can accommodate a compact rebaseline and keep the close within a 2‑week slip if BrightGrid is comfortable with a compressed eval cadence. Proposed changes below (please confirm):

Proposed rebaseline
- Original close: 2027-06-30
- Proposed close: 2027-07-14 (two-week shift) — target final signature week of July 12–16

MAP (compact) — highlights
- Week of Jun 21: procurement sign-off (BrightGrid) — Redwood to provide expedited SOC2 evidence & KM details (we will bump to priority).
- Jun 22–Jun 28: compressed eval window (continuous overnight runs + warmed cache).
- Jun 29–Jul 6: integration & cutover dry-run; final SLAs & SOW finalization.
- Jul 7–Jul 13: contract signatures & legal walk-down; go/no-go on production cutover.

Key risks and mitigations (short list)
1) Procurement gating — mitigation: assign dedicated Solutions engineer for evidence pack; provide 48h turnaround on items we control.
2) Eval regressions — mitigation: run parallel quantized pilot and baseline generation; add an intermediate eval gate at 50% of token budget.
3) Capacity bottleneck mid-eval — mitigation: reserve a dedicated pool for the pilot week (we can commit a small reserved window).

Attachments: MAP_v1.2.pdf (proposed compact MAP), evaluation_schedule_q2.xlsx (detailed daily cadence).

If this looks reasonable I'll: (a) route to Aditya to confirm the compressed eval approach, (b) ask Laura for quick approval on an one-time credit if procurement delays require an extended trial, and (c) schedule a 30-min sync with BrightGrid tomorrow.

Can you confirm procurement’s earliest available sign-off date? If Jun 21 holds, I’ll push the MAP and calendar invite.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mobile: 415-555-0172

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 10:10:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: BrightGrid — proposed rebaseline and compact MAP (need approvals)

Team — forwarding Maria's note and my proposed compact MAP. Need confirmations by tomorrow morning so I can lock dates with the customer. Quick asks: 

1) Aditya — can the infra/serving team comfortably run a compressed eval (continuous overnight runs + warmed cache) Jun 22–28? Any accuracy/regression concerns with quantized variants? Also confirm availability of a dedicated pool for week of Jun 21 (estimated 2 GPUs x 7d).

2) Laura — procurement delay may push a 2‑week slip. Can we approve a one-time credit (proposal: $12,000 account credit applied on signature) and SLA reassurance language for this customer? Need your sign-off to include in the MAP.

3) Sean — I'll likely need a prioritized SOC2 evidence packet and an owner to fast-track the vendor questionnaire replies. Can you assign someone?

Risk tolerances / escalations: if procurement cannot sign by Jun 25 we escalate to GTM leadership for an exec‑to‑exec call. Please reply with yes/no and any redlines.

Thanks — Kim

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: BrightGrid — proposed rebaseline and compact MAP (need approvals)

Kim — short technical note: we can support the compressed eval with the following caveats/assumptions:
- Dedicated pool availability: confirmed for Jun 21–28 (2x A100 equiv reserved).
- Accuracy: running quantized LLM variants + warmed KV caches should shave token-processing time ~25–35%, allowing more iterations overnight. Expect slight delta in edge-case outputs; recommend adding a 50% token-sample intermediate gate to catch regressions.
- Operational ask: we’ll provision a one-week pilot environment and pre-populate prefix caches from a subset corpus (needs BrightGrid transfer by Jun 20).

I’ll prepare a short perf pack and a sample regression checklist by EOD today. If Laura approves credit, infra can prioritize the pool reservation.

—Aditya
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime | Redwood Inference

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@brightgrid.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Tom Ellis <t.ellis@procurebright.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 15:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Close window update — procurement gating; final MAP & rebaseline confirmation

Maria / Tom — thanks for working through the procurement detail and for confirming the earliest sign-off window. Per our sync, BrightGrid confirmed procurement sign-off on Jun 21, so we’ve agreed the follow-up MAP and rebaseline below. I’m summarizing the decisions and the risk log for your board.

Agreed rebaseline
- New target close: 2027-07-14 (final signatures week of Jul 12–16).

Final compact MAP milestones (agreed)
- Jun 21: procurement sign-off (BrightGrid).
- Jun 22–28: compressed eval (Redwood dedicated pool reserved).
- Jun 29–Jul 6: integration & cutover dry-run.
- Jul 7–Jul 13: legal walk-down & signatures.

Risk register (short form)
- Risk 1: Procurement slips beyond Jun 21 — impact: +2 weeks. Mitigation: exec escalation path to GTM leadership (owner: Laura Bennett).
- Risk 2: Eval regression on quantized variant — impact: extend eval window; Mitigation: intermediate eval gate and rollback to baseline model if >3% regression on key metrics (owner: Aditya Rao).
- Risk 3: Capacity spike during pilot — impact: degraded throughput; Mitigation: reserved pool + pre-warm cache (owner: Sean Gallagher).

Commercial note: Laura approved a one-time account credit of $12,000 to offset delay-related trial time; the credit will be applied at signature.

Attachments included: MAP_v1.2.pdf (compact MAP), evaluation_schedule_q2.xlsx (daily runbook). BrightGrid procurement statement is retained in case legal needs it.

Next steps / commitments
- BrightGrid: share the sample corpus for cache pre-population by Jun 20 (owner: Maria).
- Redwood: provide perf/regression pack and intermediate gate checklist by Jun 11 (owner: Aditya).
- Redwood GTM: Laura to prepare credit memo for legal.
- Schedule: I will send a 30-minute sync invite for Jun 18 to confirm readiness and run the SOW walk-through.

If procurement slips again, we will escalate as noted. Thanks everyone for turning quickly on this — we believe the compact MAP preserves the go‑to‑production window while giving the customer the evidence they need.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
Phone: 415-555-0172

----
Attachments (stubs): MAP_v1.2.pdf (attachment), evaluation_schedule_q2.xlsx (attachment), procurement_statement.pdf (attached earlier)

Calendar: Jun 18 10:00–10:30 PDT — SOW & readiness sync (invite sent).

Key risks and mitigations (short list)
1) Procurement gating — mitigation: assign dedicated Solutions engineer for evidence pack; provide 48h turnaround on items we control.
2) Eval regressions — mitigation: run parallel quantized pilot and baseline generation; add an intermediate eval gate at 50% of token budget.
3) Capacity bottleneck mid-eval — mitigation: reserve a dedicated pool for the pilot week (we can commit a small reserved window).

Attachments: MAP_v1.2.pdf (proposed compact MAP), evaluation_schedule_q2.xlsx (detailed daily cadence).

If this looks reasonable I'll: (a) route to Aditya to confirm the compressed eval approach, (b) ask Laura for quick approval on an one-time credit if procurement delays require an extended trial, and (c) schedule a 30-min sync with BrightGrid tomorrow.

Can you confirm procurement’s earliest available sign-off date? If Jun 21 holds, I’ll push the MAP and calendar invite.

Best,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
mobile: 415-555-0172



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 10:10:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: BrightGrid — proposed rebaseline and compact MAP (need approvals)

Team — forwarding Maria's note and my proposed compact MAP. Need confirmations by tomorrow morning so I can lock dates with the customer. Quick asks: 

1) Aditya — can the infra/serving team comfortably run a compressed eval (continuous overnight runs + warmed cache) Jun 22–28? Any accuracy/regression concerns with quantized variants? Also confirm availability of a dedicated pool for week of Jun 21 (estimated 2 GPUs x 7d).

2) Laura — procurement delay may push a 2‑week slip. Can we approve a one-time credit (proposal: $12,000 account credit applied on signature) and SLA reassurance language for this customer? Need your sign-off to include in the MAP.

3) Sean — I'll likely need a prioritized SOC2 evidence packet and an owner to fast-track the vendor questionnaire replies. Can you assign someone?

Risk tolerances / escalations: if procurement cannot sign by Jun 25 we escalate to GTM leadership for an exec‑to‑exec call. Please reply with yes/no and any redlines.

Thanks — Kim



From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Jun 2027 11:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: BrightGrid — proposed rebaseline and compact MAP (need approvals)

Kim — short technical note: we can support the compressed eval with the following caveats/assumptions:
- Dedicated pool availability: confirmed for Jun 21–28 (2x A100 equiv reserved).
- Accuracy: running quantized LLM variants + warmed KV caches should shave token-processing time ~25–35%, allowing more iterations overnight. Expect slight delta in edge-case outputs; recommend adding a 50% token-sample intermediate gate to catch regressions.
- Operational ask: we’ll provision a one-week pilot environment and pre-populate prefix caches from a subset corpus (needs BrightGrid transfer by Jun 20).

I’ll prepare a short perf pack and a sample regression checklist by EOD today. If Laura approves credit, infra can prioritize the pool reservation.

—Aditya
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime | Redwood Inference



From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Maria Gomez <maria@brightgrid.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.com>, Tom Ellis <t.ellis@procurebright.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Jun 2027 15:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Close window update — procurement gating; final MAP & rebaseline confirmation

Maria / Tom — thanks for working through the procurement detail and for confirming the earliest sign-off window. Per our sync, BrightGrid confirmed procurement sign-off on Jun 21, so we’ve agreed the follow-up MAP and rebaseline below. I’m summarizing the decisions and the risk log for your board.

Agreed rebaseline
- New target close: 2027-07-14 (final signatures week of Jul 12–16).

Final compact MAP milestones (agreed)
- Jun 21: procurement sign-off (BrightGrid).
- Jun 22–28: compressed eval (Redwood dedicated pool reserved).
- Jun 29–Jul 6: integration & cutover dry-run.
- Jul 7–Jul 13: legal walk-down & signatures.

Risk register (short form)
- Risk 1: Procurement slips beyond Jun 21 — impact: +2 weeks. Mitigation: exec escalation path to GTM leadership (owner: Laura Bennett).
- Risk 2: Eval regression on quantized variant — impact: extend eval window; Mitigation: intermediate eval gate and rollback to baseline model if >3% regression on key metrics (owner: Aditya Rao).
- Risk 3: Capacity spike during pilot — impact: degraded throughput; Mitigation: reserved pool + pre-warm cache (owner: Sean Gallagher).

Commercial note: Laura approved a one-time account credit of $12,000 to offset delay-related trial time; the credit will be applied at signature.

Attachments included: MAP_v1.2.pdf (compact MAP), evaluation_schedule_q2.xlsx (daily runbook). BrightGrid procurement statement is retained in case legal needs it.

Next steps / commitments
- BrightGrid: share the sample corpus for cache pre-population by Jun 20 (owner: Maria).
- Redwood: provide perf/regression pack and intermediate gate checklist by Jun 11 (owner: Aditya).
- Redwood GTM: Laura to prepare credit memo for legal.
- Schedule: I will send a 30-minute sync invite for Jun 18 to confirm readiness and run the SOW walk-through.

If procurement slips again, we will escalate as noted. Thanks everyone for turning quickly on this — we believe the compact MAP preserves the go‑to‑production window while giving the customer the evidence they need.

Best,
Kimberly Park

source.subject: High p99 & RTT spike — HelioHealth hosted API degradation
messages:
From: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: HelioHealth Engineering <eng@heliohealth.com>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 07:13:00 +0000
Subject: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Hi Jordan —

We started seeing a dramatic increase in response times for our production service hitting Redwood’s hosted API at ~07:00 UTC. p99 jumped from ~450ms to ~2.1s, and we saw error rates climb to ~2.5% for a 30-minute window. Throughput also dipped briefly. This is impacting user-facing flows.

Can you confirm if there are known incidents in EU/Frankfurt region? We’ve attached sample traces and timestamps.

Thanks,
Maria
Senior Engineering Manager, HelioHealth
[attachment: hh_traces_excerpt.txt (18KB)]

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 07:27:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Maria —

Thanks for flagging this and for the traces. I’m escalating immediately to infrastructure and routing. Hannah/Laura — can you triage? I’ll pull in additional context and keep HelioHealth updated every 30 mins until we have a mitigation.

Jordan Blake
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--
On Fri, May 28, 2027 at 07:13 AM Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com> wrote:
> Hi Jordan —
> 
> We started seeing a dramatic increase...

From: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 07:35:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Taking this on. Initial dashboard check: there was a short-lived spike in inter-region hops for eu-west customers routed via eu-north edge (07:01–07:22 UTC). Backend host RTTS are normal; the increase appears to be in fronting/edge networking.

I’m opening INF-3412 with network and routing teams; copying Sean for routing policy checks. Laura — can you pull the runtime KV cache hit rates and any prefix-cache evictions during that window?

-Hannah

[attachment: monitoring_snapshot_0705.png (120KB)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 08:02:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Quick findings from runtime metrics (07:00–07:25 UTC):
- KV cache hit rate dipped ~4.5% for HelioHealth’s common prefixes (likely unrelated but exacerbates tail latency for repeated requests).
- No model-level slowdowns. Token generation times unchanged on host side.
- Noticed anomalous cross-region path: requests from HelioHealth’s eu-west endpoint were routed through eu-north edge nodes and then back to eu-west capacity. Extra RTT consistent with the observed p99 jump.

I’ve attached the token timing CSV and a short trace bundle. Next step: confirm whether routing policy changes were deployed near 07:00.

Attachments: debug_traces_20270528.zip (see top-level trace for cross-region hop)

-Laura

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 09:15:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Routing update: Network team rolled a fix at 08:48 UTC for an ingress policy that unintentionally preferred eu-north egress for some eu-west origin prefixes. The policy change earlier this morning (06:58 UTC) had a bug in prefix specificity that created a higher-priority route via eu-north edge.

Mitigation performed: reverted faulty policy, added a temporary explicit route for HelioHealth’s ASN to ensure eu-west egress. Post-fix observations: p99 dropped from ~2.1s to ~520ms within 7 minutes and error rate returned to baseline.

I’ve flagged a postmortem and a follow-up rollout rule to prevent regressions. Will update INF-3412 with details.

-Sean

On Fri, May 28, 2027 at 08:02 AM Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick findings...

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>
To: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 28 May 2027 09:28:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Sudden p99 & throughput drop — Hosted API (Europe)

Maria —

Quick update from infra: the spike was caused by a routing policy regression that incorrectly preferred eu-north edges for some eu-west prefixes. Team reverted the change at 08:48 UTC and put an explicit rule in place for your ASN. We’re collecting post-fix telemetry and will send a short report with traces and mitigation timeline by EOD.

I’m sorry for the disruption — we know this impacted critical HelioHealth flows. We’ll include proposed goodwill credit in the follow-up.

Jordan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
--
On Fri, May 28, 2027 at 09:15 AM Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Routing update: Network team rolled a fix...

From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 29 May 2027 14:04:00 +0000
Subject: Post-incident packet: HelioHealth latency spike (28 May)

Maria —

Attached is the packet with timeline, impact, and root-cause analysis: p99 spike caused by an ingress routing policy that increased cross-region egress for eu-west origins. Key points:
- Incident window: 07:01–07:22 UTC (28 May)
- Root cause: prefix specificity bug in policy rollout (06:58 UTC) that preferred eu-north egress
- Customer impact: transient p99 up to ~2.1s, ~2.5% errors; throughput dip during window
- Fix: policy reverted at 08:48 UTC; explicit ASN route added; rollout gating implemented

We’ve attached the traces and an SLO impact summary (p99/SLO breach count). We propose: 1) a one-time service credit to HelioHealth for the impacted window, 2) a joint verification session this week to validate routing behaviour from HelioHealth’s vantage, and 3) a post-deploy checklist change to add canary checks for route specificity.

Attachments: p99_slo_report_may_2027.pdf, debug_traces_20270528.zip

—Rachel Kim
Head of Support Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: HelioHealth Engineering <eng@heliohealth.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@heliohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 09:42:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-incident packet: HelioHealth latency spike (28 May)

Jordan/Rachel —

Thanks for the rapid investigation and the packet. The traces and timeline align with what we saw. We appreciate the explicit route and the proposed credit. HelioHealth accepts the goodwill credit and we’d like to schedule the joint verification session on Wed (June 2) 09:00 UTC. Please confirm invite.

Also please include a short notes doc that we can share with our compliance team summarizing the incident and mitigation steps.

Best,
Maria
Senior Engineering Manager, HelioHealth
