Subject: Post-incident packet: HelioHealth latency spike (28 May)

Maria —

Attached is the packet with timeline, impact, and root-cause analysis: p99 spike caused by an ingress routing policy that increased cross-region egress for eu-west origins. Key points:
- Incident window: 07:01–07:22 UTC (28 May)
- Root cause: prefix specificity bug in policy rollout (06:58 UTC) that preferred eu-north egress
- Customer impact: transient p99 up to ~2.1s, ~2.5% errors; throughput dip during window
- Fix: policy reverted at 08:48 UTC; explicit ASN route added; rollout gating implemented

We’ve attached the traces and an SLO impact summary (p99/SLO breach count). We propose: 1) a one-time service credit to HelioHealth for the impacted window, 2) a joint verification session this week to validate routing behaviour from HelioHealth’s vantage, and 3) a post-deploy checklist change to add canary checks for route specificity.

Attachments: p99_slo_report_may_2027.pdf, debug_traces_20270528.zip

—Rachel Kim
Head of Support Operations, Redwood Inference

From: Maria Gonzalez <maria.gonzalez@heliohealth.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
Cc: HelioHealth Engineering <eng@heliohealth.com>, Carlos Mendes <carlos.mendes@heliohealth.com>
Date: Mon, 31 May 2027 09:42:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Post-incident packet: HelioHealth latency spike (28 May)

Jordan/Rachel —

Thanks for the rapid investigation and the packet. The traces and timeline align with what we saw. We appreciate the explicit route and the proposed credit. HelioHealth accepts the goodwill credit and we’d like to schedule the joint verification session on Wed (June 2) 09:00 UTC. Please confirm invite.

Also please include a short notes doc that we can share with our compliance team summarizing the incident and mitigation steps.

Best,
Maria
Senior Engineering Manager, HelioHealth

source.subject: Request: Approval for non-standard SLA & capped remedy for CloudWorks deal (DW-4821)
source.messages:
From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Li <ethan.li@cloudworks.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Proposed SLA amendment + pricing alignment for CloudWorks (DW-4821)

Ethan — great to meet yesterday. Following up with the amendment we discussed during the pilot wrap-up. CloudWorks wants a tighter latency commitment for the 24/7 inference tier, but their procurement team is also asking us to accept a lower uptime target on a specific model variant to keep unit costs reasonable.

Attached: cloudworks_proposed_amendment_20270530.pdf (proposed SLA tables + remedies)

High level ask: can Deal Desk / Execs approve the following for the pilot-to-production order?
- Uptime target: 99.50% (standard is 99.95%) for Model Variant B only (non-critical traffic routed to Variant A at 99.95%)
- Remedy cap: maximum service credit across all incidents for billing month capped at 2% of monthly fee
- One-time 18% list-price discount for first 12 months in exchange for 12-month committed usage (counts toward revenue threshold)

Rationale: CloudWorks is price-sensitive and willing to accept a slightly lower uptime for this variant if we cap exposure. This keeps the operational complexity in check while preserving incentives to prioritize the higher-tier variant. They want written confirmation today to close before EOD.

Requesting: Marissa — please confirm Deal Desk position. Lauren — any legal red flags with the proposed remedy cap / variant carve-out? Sean — would you consider exec approval if Deal Desk and Legal sign off?

Thanks,
Jonas Weber
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0184

---

From: Ethan Li <ethan.li@cloudworks.com>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@cloudworks.com
Date: 2027-05-30T10:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Proposed SLA amendment + pricing alignment for CloudWorks (DW-4821)

Jonas — thanks for the note and the PDF. We reviewed the tables offline with our ops and procurement. Two clarifying points from our side:
1) We need the remedy cap to be hard-capped (no acceleration beyond 2% in any billing month) — otherwise procurement will push for a larger holdback.
2) We also need a clause that gives us prioritized scheduling for capacity during incidents affecting Variant B (so that we can failover to Variant A without extra cost).

I've attached our procurement form and a short note from our ops director. If you can confirm the hard cap and the scheduling guarantee, we'll sign the amendment and route the PO today.

Attachment: cloudworks_procurement_form.pdf

—Ethan
Ethan Li, Head of Platform — CloudWorks
ethan.li@cloudworks.com | +1 (617) 555-0123

---

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T12:20:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Proposed SLA amendment + pricing alignment for CloudWorks (DW-4821)

Team — quick Deal Desk read:
- Pricing: 18% one-time discount for 12 months is larger than our standard autonomous allowance (up to 12%), so this requires Exec approval. Recommend we soften to 15% if Exec is hesitant, but I know Jon pushed for 18% to match the competitive offer.
- SLA: Variant carve-out (99.5% for Variant B) OK from a commercial risk perspective if legal signs the remedy language and we hard-cap monthly credit at 2% of MRC.
- Operational ask (prioritized scheduling): Operations is okay to include a best-effort scheduling priority for Variant B incidents but wants explicit language that does not create a hard SLA on capacity scheduling — propose "priority consideration" language.

Lauren — attaching my suggested redlines to the amendment (redlines_dealdesk_20270530.docx). If Legal can accept the remedy cap and the priority consideration language, we need Sean for exec clearance on the discount size (>15%).

Action requested for Sean: Approve one of the three options so we can reply to CloudWorks and unblock PO:
A) Full approval: 18% discount + allow Variant B at 99.5% with 2% monthly credit cap and priority-consideration clause.
B) Conditional: 15% discount + same SLA carve-out and 2% cap.
C) Decline: ask CloudWorks to reprice to standard 99.95% uptime for all variants or increase committed spend to unlock the 18% discount.

Marissa Cole
Senior Deal Desk Manager — Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 (650) 555-0199

Attachment: redlines_dealdesk_20270530.docx

---

From: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-05-30T15:43:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Proposed SLA amendment + pricing alignment for CloudWorks (DW-4821)

Appreciate the context. I reviewed the redlines and the commercial trade-offs. I will approve option A (Full approval) with the following binding conditions to limit downstream risk:
1) Remedy cap must be explicit and absolute: "Service credits for any calendar month shall not exceed 2% of the monthly recurring charges invoiced for that month." No ad hoc expansions.
2) Priority language must be non-contractual operational practice: "CloudWorks will receive priority consideration for recovery scheduling for Variant B incidents; however, Redwood makes no guaranteed scheduling commitments and will not be liable for failure to schedule beyond the service credit remedies above."
3) The 18% discount applies only to the first 12 months and requires a signed amendment + PO within 72 hours of this approval. If PO is not received in that window, the discount defaults to 10%.
4) Legal (Lauren) to confirm redlines are executed as agreed and to prepare a final DocuSign amendment link.

Lauren — please prepare the final amendment incorporating the cap and the priority-consideration wording and send me the DocuSign link. Jon — once DocuSign is out, please push for PO and keep procurement@cloudworks.com in copy.

Sean Gallagher
VP, Commercial — Redwood Inference
sean.gallagher@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0177

---

On Wed, May 30, 2027 at 16:05 Ethan Li <ethan.li@cloudworks.com> wrote:
> Thanks Jonas — appreciate the quick turn.
>
> We will route PO as soon as we have the signed amendment and the explicit 2% hard cap language. Can you get DocuSign over to us by COB?
>
> —Ethan
>
> (quoted above)
